### Vibecode Agent
*2/23/2026, 12:14:24 PM*

To connect your own Claude API key, you'll need to do two things:

1. **Get your Anthropic API key** — Go to [console.anthropic.com](https://console.anthropic.com), sign in (or create an account), and generate an API key from the API Keys section.

2. **Add it in the ENV tab** — On the Vibecode app, go to the **ENV** tab and add a new environment variable called `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` with your API key as the value.

Once you've added the key, let me know and I can help you build whatever AI-powered feature you have in mind.

### User
*2/23/2026, 12:30:48 PM*

Prompt 1 (of 6): System Architecture Foundation

Build a modern, scalable web application called "Personality Insights."

This application is a personality-driven personal intelligence platform designed to help users understand themselves, grow personally, and improve relationships using personality insights and adaptive AI guidance.

The platform must include:

• Secure user authentication (email/password and social login ready)
• Private user accounts (privacy-first architecture)
• Scalable architecture that can later support mobile apps
• Clean, modern, professional UI that feels intelligent, calm, and trustworthy
• Fast performance and responsive design

Core architectural requirements:

• Frontend: modern, responsive web app
• Backend: scalable API architecture
• Database: structured to support personality profiles, AI chat, connections, and insights
• AI integration ready for Claude API
• Notification system ready for daily insight notifications

Design philosophy:

The app must feel:

• Intelligent, not gimmicky
• Modern, but not childish
• Social, but privacy-first
• Emotionally safe and trustworthy

Primary user outcome:

The user should feel like this app understands them and helps them grow continuously.

Build the core application foundation and structure first.

{"description":"Set up database and auth backend","id":"019c8bc7-050b-77a6-b579-9756046788f4","prompt":"Set up the database schema, authentication, and core backend API for \"Personality Insights\" — a personality-driven personal intelligence platform.\n\nFollow the database-auth skill instructions carefully. Here's what to do:\n\n## 1. Install packages\n\nBackend:\n```bash\ncd /home/user/workspace/backend \u0026\u0026 bun add better-auth @prisma/client@6 @hono/zod-validator @vibecodeapp/backend-sdk \u0026\u0026 bun add -d prisma@6\n```\n\nWebapp:\n```bash\ncd /home/user/workspace/webapp \u0026\u0026 bun add better-auth\n```\n\n## 2. Create Prisma Schema\n\nCreate `backend/prisma/schema.prisma` with the Better Auth required models (User, Session, Account, Verification) PLUS these app-specific models:\n\n```prisma\ngenerator client {\n  provider = \"prisma-client-js\"\n}\n\ndatasource db {\n  provider = \"sqlite\"\n  url      = env(\"DATABASE_URL\")\n}\n\n// Better Auth required models\nmodel User {\n  id            String    @id\n  name          String\n  email         String    @unique\n  emailVerified Boolean   @default(false)\n  image         String?\n  createdAt     DateTime  @default(now())\n  updatedAt     DateTime  @updatedAt\n  sessions      Session[]\n  accounts      Account[]\n  \n  // App-specific relations\n  personalityProfile PersonalityProfile?\n  chatConversations  ChatConversation[]\n  insights           Insight[]\n  journalEntries     JournalEntry[]\n}\n\nmodel Session {\n  id        String   @id\n  expiresAt DateTime\n  token     String   @unique\n  createdAt DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt\n  ipAddress String?\n  userAgent String?\n  userId    String\n  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n}\n\nmodel Account {\n  id                    String    @id\n  accountId             String\n  providerId            String\n  userId                String\n  user                  User      @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  accessToken           String?\n  refreshToken          String?\n  idToken               String?\n  accessTokenExpiresAt  DateTime?\n  refreshTokenExpiresAt DateTime?\n  scope                 String?\n  password              String?\n  createdAt             DateTime  @default(now())\n  updatedAt             DateTime  @updatedAt\n}\n\nmodel Verification {\n  id         String   @id\n  identifier String\n  value      String\n  expiresAt  DateTime\n  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt  DateTime @updatedAt\n}\n\n// App-specific models\n\nmodel PersonalityProfile {\n  id              String   @id @default(cuid())\n  userId          String   @unique\n  user            User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  \n  // Core personality data (stored as JSON strings)\n  personalityType String?  // e.g., MBTI type or custom classification\n  traits          String?  // JSON: array of trait scores\n  strengths       String?  // JSON: array of strengths\n  growthAreas     String?  // JSON: array of growth areas\n  values          String?  // JSON: array of core values\n  communicationStyle String? // JSON: communication preferences\n  \n  // Assessment tracking\n  assessmentCompleted Boolean @default(false)\n  assessmentVersion   Int     @default(0)\n  \n  createdAt DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt\n}\n\nmodel ChatConversation {\n  id        String   @id @default(cuid())\n  userId    String\n  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  title     String   @default(\"New Conversation\")\n  context   String?  // JSON: conversation context/topic\n  messages  ChatMessage[]\n  createdAt DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt\n}\n\nmodel ChatMessage {\n  id             String   @id @default(cuid())\n  conversationId String\n  conversation   ChatConversation @relation(fields: [conversationId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  role           String   // \"user\" or \"assistant\"\n  content        String\n  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())\n}\n\nmodel Insight {\n  id        String   @id @default(cuid())\n  userId    String\n  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  type      String   // \"daily\", \"weekly\", \"personality\", \"growth\", \"relationship\"\n  title     String\n  content   String\n  metadata  String?  // JSON: additional data\n  isRead    Boolean  @default(false)\n  createdAt DateTime @default(now())\n}\n\nmodel JournalEntry {\n  id        String   @id @default(cuid())\n  userId    String\n  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  content   String\n  mood      String?  // e.g., \"happy\", \"reflective\", \"anxious\"\n  tags      String?  // JSON: array of tags\n  aiReflection String? // AI-generated reflection\n  createdAt DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt\n}\n```\n\n## 3. Add DATABASE_URL to backend/.env\n\n```bash\ngrep -q \"^DATABASE_URL=\" /home/user/workspace/backend/.env || echo 'DATABASE_URL=\"file:./dev.db\"' \u003e\u003e /home/user/workspace/backend/.env\n```\n\n## 4. Update backend/src/env.ts to validate DATABASE_URL and BETTER_AUTH_SECRET\n\nRead the existing env.ts first, then update it to include:\n- DATABASE_URL: z.string().default(\"file:./dev.db\")\n- BETTER_AUTH_SECRET: z.string().min(1)\n- ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: z.string().min(1) (should already be there)\n\n## 5. Create backend/src/prisma.ts\n\n```typescript\nimport { PrismaClient } from \"@prisma/client\";\nconst prisma = new PrismaClient();\nasync function initSqlitePragmas(prisma: PrismaClient) {\n  await prisma.$queryRawUnsafe(\"PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;\");\n  await prisma.$queryRawUnsafe(\"PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;\");\n  await prisma.$queryRawUnsafe(\"PRAGMA busy_timeout = 10000;\");\n  await prisma.$queryRawUnsafe(\"PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL;\");\n}\ninitSqlitePragmas(prisma);\nexport { prisma };\n```\n\n## 6. Generate BETTER_AUTH_SECRET and add to .env\n\n```bash\ngrep -q \"^BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=\" /home/user/workspace/backend/.env || echo \"BETTER_AUTH_SECRET=\\\"$(openssl rand -base64 32)\\\"\" \u003e\u003e /home/user/workspace/backend/.env\n```\n\n## 7. Create backend/src/auth.ts\n\nFollow the skill exactly. Use fromName: \"Personality Insights\" in the email OTP config.\n\n## 8. Update backend/src/index.ts\n\nRead the existing file first. Then update it to:\n- Add the typed Hono app with user/session Variables\n- Add auth middleware that populates user/session for all routes\n- Mount auth handler on /api/auth/*\n- Keep existing CORS middleware and health check\n- Keep the proxy import\n- Remove the sample route import/mount\n\n## 9. Create backend/src/types.ts for shared Zod schemas\n\nCreate schemas for API request/response types that will be shared with frontend.\n\n## 10. Generate Prisma client and push DB\n\n```bash\ncd /home/user/workspace/backend \u0026\u0026 bunx prisma generate \u0026\u0026 bunx prisma db push\n```\n\n## 11. Create initial API routes\n\nCreate these route files in backend/src/routes/:\n\n**backend/src/routes/profile.ts** - Personality profile CRUD\n- GET /api/profile - Get current user's personality profile\n- PUT /api/profile - Update personality profile\n\n**backend/src/routes/chat.ts** - AI chat conversations  \n- GET /api/chat/conversations - List conversations\n- POST /api/chat/conversations - Create new conversation\n- GET /api/chat/conversations/:id/messages - Get messages for a conversation\n- POST /api/chat/conversations/:id/messages - Send message and get AI response (use Anthropic API with the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var to generate responses. Use the @anthropic-ai/sdk package - install it with `cd /home/user/workspace/backend \u0026\u0026 bun add @anthropic-ai/sdk`)\n\n**backend/src/routes/insights.ts** - Insights\n- GET /api/insights - List user's insights\n- PATCH /api/insights/:id/read - Mark insight as read\n\n**backend/src/routes/journal.ts** - Journal entries\n- GET /api/journal - List journal entries\n- POST /api/journal - Create journal entry\n- PUT /api/journal/:id - Update journal entry\n\nAll routes must:\n- Check for authenticated user with `const user = c.get(\"user\"); if (!user) return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);`\n- Return data in { data: ... } envelope\n- Use proper error handling\n\nFor the chat AI response, create a system prompt that establishes the AI as a personality-aware guide that helps the user understand themselves. If they have a personality profile, include relevant traits in the system prompt context.\n\n## Important notes:\n- Read existing files before editing them\n- Follow the { data: T } response envelope pattern\n- All routes prefixed with /api/\n- Use c.json() for responses\n- The backend server auto-restarts, don't try to start it manually\n- Don't add .env to .gitignore","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:backend-developer"}

{"description":"Build frontend design system","id":"019c8bcd-6bd3-74c7-9521-5d81a0dfc93d","prompt":"I need you to set up the design system for \"Personality Insights\" — a personality-driven personal intelligence platform. The app should feel intelligent, calm, trustworthy, and modern. NOT gimmicky or childish.\n\n## Design Direction\n\nThink Linear meets a wellness app. Clean, minimal, with depth. The aesthetic should feel like a premium intelligence tool — not a generic SaaS dashboard.\n\n**Color palette:** Deep, sophisticated dark theme with warm accents.\n- Background: Very deep navy/slate (not pure black)\n- Foreground: Warm off-white\n- Primary: A warm amber/gold — represents insight and illumination\n- Accent: A soft teal/sage — represents growth and calm\n- Muted tones for secondary surfaces\n- Subtle borders\n\n**Typography:** Use \"DM Sans\" for body (clean, readable) and \"Playfair Display\" for headings (elegant, intelligent feel). Both from Google Fonts.\n\n**Design tokens:** Generous spacing, subtle rounded corners, soft shadows, glass-morphism effects for cards.\n\n## Files to update\n\n### 1. `/home/user/workspace/webapp/index.html`\n- Update title to \"Personality Insights\"\n- Update meta description to something fitting\n- Update og:title and og:description\n\n### 2. `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/index.css`\nRead the existing file first. Then update the CSS variables for both light and dark modes. The app should primarily be dark mode. Define:\n\n```css\n@import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=DM+Sans:ital,opsz,wght@0,9..40,100..1000;1,9..40,100..1000\u0026family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400..900;1,400..900\u0026display=swap');\n```\n\nUpdate the CSS variables in :root for a dark-first design:\n- --background: deep navy (like 222 47% 6%)\n- --foreground: warm off-white (like 40 20% 94%)\n- --primary: warm amber/gold (like 38 92% 55%)\n- --primary-foreground: dark (for text on primary buttons)\n- --secondary: slightly lighter navy\n- --accent: sage/teal (like 165 35% 45%)\n- --accent-foreground: light\n- --muted: dark navy surface\n- --muted-foreground: muted warm gray\n- --card: slightly elevated from background\n- --border: subtle warm border\n- --destructive: warm red\n- --ring: amber glow\n\nAlso add utility classes:\n- `.glass` - glassmorphism effect (backdrop-blur, semi-transparent bg, subtle border)\n- Smooth scroll behavior\n- Custom scrollbar styling (thin, subtle)\n\n### 3. `/home/user/workspace/webapp/tailwind.config.ts`\nRead the existing file. Update to add:\n- Font families: `display: [\"Playfair Display\", \"serif\"]` and `sans: [\"DM Sans\", \"sans-serif\"]`\n- Keep existing color references to CSS variables\n- Add any custom animations needed (fade-in, slide-up, etc.)\n\n### 4. Delete `/home/user/workspace/webapp/App.css` if it exists (it's legacy)\n\nIMPORTANT: Read all files before editing. Keep existing Tailwind/shadcn infrastructure intact — only update the design tokens and add the new fonts. Do NOT break the existing shadcn component variable system.","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

{"description":"Build frontend auth and core pages","id":"019c8bcf-cb5b-7237-9d6f-93d49fc4e475","prompt":"Build the complete frontend for \"Personality Insights\" — a personality-driven personal intelligence platform. The design system is already set up (deep navy background, warm amber/gold primary, sage teal accent, DM Sans body font, Playfair Display heading font).\n\nRead ALL existing files before editing. The existing api.ts, index.css, and tailwind.config.ts are already configured.\n\n## Critical Context\n\nThe backend API is at VITE_BACKEND_URL (or relative URLs in prod). The API uses `{ data: T }` envelope — the api.ts helper auto-unwraps it. All endpoints require auth except /api/auth/*.\n\nBackend types are in `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/types.ts` — these are the shared Zod schemas. Import types from there in the frontend.\n\n## Files to Create/Update\n\n### 1. Auth Client - `webapp/src/lib/auth-client.ts`\n```typescript\nimport { createAuthClient } from \"better-auth/react\";\nimport { emailOTPClient } from \"better-auth/client/plugins\";\n\nexport const authClient = createAuthClient({\n  baseURL: import.meta.env.VITE_BACKEND_URL || undefined,\n  plugins: [emailOTPClient()],\n  fetchOptions: {\n    credentials: \"include\",\n  },\n});\n\nexport const { useSession, signOut } = authClient;\n```\n\n### 2. Route Guards\n\nCreate `webapp/src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx` and `webapp/src/components/GuestRoute.tsx` following the database-auth skill. ProtectedRoute redirects to /login if no session. GuestRoute redirects to /dashboard if session exists.\n\nFor the loading state, use a beautiful full-page loading spinner that fits the design system — a subtle pulsing amber dot or similar.\n\n### 3. App Layout - `webapp/src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`\n\nCreate a beautiful app shell with:\n- **Left sidebar** (collapsible on mobile) with navigation:\n  - Dashboard (home icon)\n  - Chat with AI (message-circle icon) \n  - Journal (book-open icon)\n  - Insights (lightbulb icon)\n  - Profile (user icon)\n- **Bottom of sidebar**: User avatar/email, sign out button\n- **Top bar** on mobile with hamburger menu\n- The sidebar should use the glass effect and feel premium\n- Use lucide-react icons\n- Active nav item highlighted with primary color\n- Smooth transitions\n\n### 4. Auth Pages\n\n**`webapp/src/pages/Login.tsx`** - Email input screen\n- Full-page with the app branding\n- Centered card with glass effect\n- \"Personality Insights\" logo/title in Playfair Display\n- Subtitle: \"Understand yourself. Grow continuously.\"\n- Email input with a golden \"Continue\" button\n- Beautiful background with subtle gradient/pattern\n- Use `authClient.emailOtp.sendVerificationOtp({ email, type: \"sign-in\" })` then navigate to /verify-otp with email in state\n\n**`webapp/src/pages/VerifyOtp.tsx`** - OTP verification screen\n- Similar styling to login page\n- Show the email address\n- 6-digit OTP input (use individual digit boxes for each digit, not a single input)\n- CRITICAL: Use `authClient.signIn.emailOtp({ email, otp })` NOT `authClient.emailOtp.verifyEmail()`\n- \"Back to login\" link\n- Auto-submit when all 6 digits entered\n- Resend OTP button with countdown timer\n\n### 5. Dashboard Page - `webapp/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`\n\nThe main landing page after login. Should feel like a personal intelligence command center:\n\n- **Greeting section**: \"Good morning, {name}\" with a warm, personalized feel using Playfair Display\n- **Quick stats row**: Cards showing:\n  - Journal entries count\n  - Insights received  \n  - Chat conversations\n  - Days active\n- **Recent insights section**: Show latest 3 insights in cards\n- **Quick actions**: Buttons to start a chat, write in journal, view insights\n- All with staggered fade-in animations\n- Use the glass card style\n\n### 6. Chat Page - `webapp/src/pages/Chat.tsx`\n\nA real-time AI chat interface:\n- Left panel: List of conversations (create new, select existing)\n- Right panel: Chat messages with the AI\n- Clean message bubbles (user messages right-aligned, AI left-aligned)\n- Message input at the bottom with send button\n- Loading indicator when AI is responding\n- Creates a new conversation automatically on first message if none selected\n- Mobile: Show conversation list or chat (not both), with back button\n- Use the glass effect for message bubbles\n\n### 7. Journal Page - `webapp/src/pages/Journal.tsx`\n\nA reflective journaling interface:\n- List of journal entries on the left (or top on mobile)\n- Entry editor on the right\n- Mood selector (emoji-based: happy, reflective, anxious, grateful, energized, calm)\n- Rich text area for writing\n- AI reflection shown below entry (if available)\n- Create new entry button\n- Clean, minimal, writing-focused interface\n\n### 8. Insights Page - `webapp/src/pages/Insights.tsx`\n\nA feed of personalized insights:\n- Filter tabs: All, Daily, Weekly, Personality, Growth, Relationship\n- Card-based layout for each insight\n- Unread indicator (amber dot)\n- Mark as read on click\n- Empty state for when there are no insights yet\n- Beautiful card designs with different accent colors per type\n\n### 9. Profile Page - `webapp/src/pages/Profile.tsx`\n\nUser's personality profile:\n- Display user info (name, email)\n- Personality type (if set)\n- Traits, strengths, growth areas displayed as tags/chips\n- Values and communication style\n- Assessment status indicator\n- Edit profile capability\n- Clean, card-based layout\n- Empty state encouraging the user to start their personality assessment (linking to chat)\n\n### 10. Update App.tsx\n\nUpdate the router in `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/App.tsx` to include all routes:\n- `/login` → GuestRoute → Login\n- `/verify-otp` → GuestRoute → VerifyOtp  \n- `/` → ProtectedRoute → AppLayout → Dashboard\n- `/chat` → ProtectedRoute → AppLayout → Chat\n- `/journal` → ProtectedRoute → AppLayout → Journal\n- `/insights` → ProtectedRoute → AppLayout → Insights\n- `/profile` → ProtectedRoute → AppLayout → Profile\n- `*` → NotFound\n\n### 11. Update `webapp/src/lib/api.ts`\n\nRemove the sample types and sample API functions at the bottom. Keep everything else as-is.\n\n## Design Guidelines\n\n- Use `font-display` class on headings for Playfair Display\n- Use `bg-background`, `bg-card`, `bg-secondary`, `bg-muted` for surfaces\n- Use `text-primary` for accent text, `text-foreground` for body, `text-muted-foreground` for secondary text\n- Use `glass` and `glass-subtle` utility classes for elevated cards\n- Use `glow-primary` for highlighted elements\n- Use `animate-fade-in-up` with staggered animation-delay for page load reveals\n- Keep spacing generous — the app should breathe\n- Use the `border-border` for subtle separations\n- Rounded corners with `rounded-lg` or `rounded-xl`\n- Use React Query (useQuery, useMutation) for all API calls\n- Show toast notifications for errors and successes using the existing Sonner/toast\n- Use `framer-motion` for page transitions and micro-interactions where it adds value\n\n## Type imports from backend\n\nImport types from the backend types file:\n```typescript\nimport type { PersonalityProfile, ChatConversation, ChatMessage, Insight, JournalEntry } from \"../../../../backend/src/types\";\n```\n\nOr define equivalent interfaces in the frontend if the path import doesn't work — keep them compatible.\n\n## IMPORTANT\n\n- Read files before editing\n- Extract components into separate files — avoid monolithic page files\n- Use small, focused components\n- All styling via Tailwind classes, never inline styles\n- Use shadcn/ui components (Button, Card, Input, Badge, Tabs, Dialog, Sheet, etc.)\n- Make it responsive — works on both mobile and desktop\n- The overall feel should be: intelligent, calm, trustworthy, premium","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

### Vibecode Agent
*2/23/2026, 12:57:28 PM*

The **Personality Insights** foundation is fully built. Here's what's in place:

**Backend:**
- SQLite database with Prisma (User, PersonalityProfile, ChatConversation, ChatMessage, Insight, JournalEntry)
- Better Auth with email OTP login
- API routes for profile, AI chat (powered by your Claude API key), journal, and insights
- All routes are authenticated and follow the `{ data: T }` envelope pattern

**Frontend:**
- Deep navy design system with amber/gold primary and sage teal accent
- DM Sans body font + Playfair Display headings
- Email OTP login flow (login page + OTP verification)
- App shell with glass sidebar navigation
- 5 core pages: Dashboard, Chat, Journal, Insights, Profile
- All pages are responsive (mobile + desktop)
- React Query for data fetching, Framer Motion animations

You should see the login screen in your preview now. Try logging in with your email to test the full flow. Let me know what you'd like to build next -- the personality assessment, more AI features, or any design tweaks.

---

Project in the magic buffer

---
### User
*2/23/2026, 1:00:34 PM*

Prompt 2: Database and Data Model

Create a scalable database schema for Personality Insights with the following core entities:

Users table:

• id
• name
• email
• password hash
• personality_type
• personality_scores
• communication_style
• growth_focus
• created_at

Personality Profile table:

• id
• user_id
• strengths
• growth_areas
• communication_preferences
• behavioral_tendencies
• stress_tendencies

Connections table:

• id
• user_id
• connected_user_id
• connection_status (pending, accepted)
• created_at

Chat History table:

• id
• user_id
• message
• response
• context
• created_at

Daily Insights table:

• id
• user_id
• insight_text
• delivered_at

Privacy Settings table:

• user_id
• profile_visibility (private, connections_only, public)

The database must be optimized for:

• fast reads and writes
• secure user data
• scalable growth

Create backend API endpoints to support these models.

### System — `Executing tool: Task`
*2/23/2026, 1:01:45 PM*

{"description":"Extend backend schema and routes","id":"019c8be1-031b-7a12-8881-e5eda22e2ce5","prompt":"I need to extend the Personality Insights backend with new models and API routes. Read all files before editing.\n\n## 1. Fix auth.ts to use Vibecode SDK for email OTP\n\nThe current auth.ts at `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/auth.ts` only logs OTPs to console. Fix it to actually send emails using the Vibecode SDK:\n\n```typescript\nimport { createVibecodeSDK } from \"@vibecodeapp/backend-sdk\";\n\nconst vibecode = createVibecodeSDK();\n```\n\nThen in the emailOTP plugin config, update the sendVerificationOTP:\n\n```typescript\nemailOTP({\n  async sendVerificationOTP({ email, otp, type }) {\n    if (type !== \"sign-in\") return;\n    await vibecode.email.sendOTP({\n      to: email,\n      code: String(otp),\n      fromName: \"Personality Insights\",\n      lang: \"en\",\n    });\n  },\n  otpLength: 6,\n}),\n```\n\nAlso add the `advanced` section to the betterAuth config:\n```typescript\nadvanced: {\n  trustedProxyHeaders: true,\n  disableCSRFCheck: true,\n  defaultCookieAttributes: {\n    sameSite: \"none\",\n    secure: true,\n    partitioned: true,\n  },\n},\n```\n\nKeep the existing `secret: env.BETTER_AUTH_SECRET` and everything else. Just update the OTP sending and add the advanced section.\n\n## 2. Update Prisma schema\n\nUpdate `/home/user/workspace/backend/prisma/schema.prisma` to add these new models and extend existing ones.\n\n**Extend User model** - add these fields:\n```prisma\npersonalityType     String?  // e.g., MBTI classification\npersonalityScores   String?  // JSON: detailed scores\ncommunicationStyle  String?  // e.g., \"direct\", \"diplomatic\"\ngrowthFocus         String?  // JSON: current growth areas\n```\n\nAlso add relations:\n```prisma\nsentConnections     Connection[] @relation(\"SentConnections\")\nreceivedConnections Connection[] @relation(\"ReceivedConnections\")\nprivacySettings     PrivacySettings?\n```\n\n**Extend PersonalityProfile model** - add:\n```prisma\nbehavioralTendencies String?  // JSON: behavioral patterns\nstressTendencies     String?  // JSON: stress response patterns\ncommunicationPreferences String? // JSON: how they prefer to communicate\n```\n\n**Add Connection model:**\n```prisma\nmodel Connection {\n  id              String   @id @default(cuid())\n  userId          String\n  user            User     @relation(\"SentConnections\", fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  connectedUserId String\n  connectedUser   User     @relation(\"ReceivedConnections\", fields: [connectedUserId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  status          String   @default(\"pending\") // \"pending\", \"accepted\", \"declined\"\n  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt\n\n  @@unique([userId, connectedUserId])\n}\n```\n\n**Add PrivacySettings model:**\n```prisma\nmodel PrivacySettings {\n  id                String   @id @default(cuid())\n  userId            String   @unique\n  user              User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  profileVisibility String   @default(\"private\") // \"private\", \"connections_only\", \"public\"\n  showPersonalityType Boolean @default(true)\n  showTraits        Boolean  @default(false)\n  showGrowthAreas   Boolean  @default(false)\n  createdAt         DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt         DateTime @updatedAt\n}\n```\n\n## 3. Push schema changes\n\nAfter editing schema.prisma, run:\n```bash\ncd /home/user/workspace/backend \u0026\u0026 bunx prisma db push\n```\n\n## 4. Update types.ts\n\nAdd new Zod schemas to `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/types.ts`:\n\n**Update PersonalityProfileSchema** to include the new fields (behavioralTendencies, stressTendencies, communicationPreferences).\n\n**Update UpdateProfileSchema** to include the new fields.\n\n**Add ConnectionSchema:**\n```typescript\nexport const ConnectionSchema = z.object({\n  id: z.string(),\n  userId: z.string(),\n  connectedUserId: z.string(),\n  status: z.enum([\"pending\", \"accepted\", \"declined\"]),\n  createdAt: z.string(),\n  updatedAt: z.string(),\n});\nexport type Connection = z.infer\u003ctypeof ConnectionSchema\u003e;\n```\n\n**Add CreateConnectionSchema:**\n```typescript\nexport const CreateConnectionSchema = z.object({\n  email: z.string().email(), // Connect by email\n});\nexport type CreateConnectionInput = z.infer\u003ctypeof CreateConnectionSchema\u003e;\n```\n\n**Add PrivacySettingsSchema:**\n```typescript\nexport const PrivacySettingsSchema = z.object({\n  id: z.string(),\n  userId: z.string(),\n  profileVisibility: z.enum([\"private\", \"connections_only\", \"public\"]),\n  showPersonalityType: z.boolean(),\n  showTraits: z.boolean(),\n  showGrowthAreas: z.boolean(),\n  createdAt: z.string(),\n  updatedAt: z.string(),\n});\nexport type PrivacySettings = z.infer\u003ctypeof PrivacySettingsSchema\u003e;\n\nexport const UpdatePrivacySettingsSchema = z.object({\n  profileVisibility: z.enum([\"private\", \"connections_only\", \"public\"]).optional(),\n  showPersonalityType: z.boolean().optional(),\n  showTraits: z.boolean().optional(),\n  showGrowthAreas: z.boolean().optional(),\n});\nexport type UpdatePrivacySettingsInput = z.infer\u003ctypeof UpdatePrivacySettingsSchema\u003e;\n```\n\n**Add DashboardStatsSchema:**\n```typescript\nexport const DashboardStatsSchema = z.object({\n  journalEntries: z.number(),\n  insightsReceived: z.number(),\n  chatConversations: z.number(),\n  daysActive: z.number(),\n  connectionsCount: z.number(),\n});\nexport type DashboardStats = z.infer\u003ctypeof DashboardStatsSchema\u003e;\n```\n\n## 5. Create new API routes\n\n**Create `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/connections.ts`:**\n\n```\nGET /api/connections - List user's connections (both sent and received that are accepted, plus pending received)\nPOST /api/connections - Send connection request (by email)\nPATCH /api/connections/:id/accept - Accept a connection request\nPATCH /api/connections/:id/decline - Decline a connection request\nDELETE /api/connections/:id - Remove a connection\n```\n\nFor the connection response, include the connected user's basic info (name, email, image). Use Prisma includes.\n\n**Create `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/privacy.ts`:**\n\n```\nGET /api/privacy - Get current user's privacy settings (create defaults if none exist)\nPUT /api/privacy - Update privacy settings\n```\n\n**Create `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/dashboard.ts`:**\n\n```\nGET /api/dashboard/stats - Get dashboard statistics\n```\n\nThis should return counts of: journal entries, insights, chat conversations, days active (days since account creation), and connections count.\n\n## 6. Mount new routes in index.ts\n\nAdd to `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/index.ts`:\n```typescript\nimport { connectionsRouter } from \"./routes/connections\";\nimport { privacyRouter } from \"./routes/privacy\";\nimport { dashboardRouter } from \"./routes/dashboard\";\n\n// ... existing routes ...\napp.route(\"/api/connections\", connectionsRouter);\napp.route(\"/api/privacy\", privacyRouter);\napp.route(\"/api/dashboard\", dashboardRouter);\n```\n\n## 7. Update profile route\n\nUpdate `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/profile.ts` to also update the User-level personality fields (personalityType, personalityScores, communicationStyle, growthFocus) when the profile is updated. Add a `UpdateUserProfileSchema` that includes those fields, or extend the existing update to also write to the User model.\n\n## Important rules:\n- All routes return `{ data: T }` envelope\n- All routes check for authenticated user\n- Use `c.json({ error: { message, code } }, statusCode)` for errors\n- Read files before editing\n- Use the existing Variables type pattern from other route files\n- Don't start the server manually - it auto-restarts","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:backend-developer"}

{"description":"Build frontend connections and privacy","id":"019c8be5-7203-7a8b-884f-650ee738b4ad","prompt":"I need to update the frontend for \"Personality Insights\" to support new Connections and Privacy Settings features. Read all files before editing.\n\n## 1. Update frontend types (`/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/types/index.ts`)\n\nRead the existing file first. Add these new types:\n\n```typescript\nexport interface Connection {\n  id: string;\n  userId: string;\n  connectedUserId: string;\n  status: \"pending\" | \"accepted\" | \"declined\";\n  createdAt: string;\n  updatedAt: string;\n  // Included from API\n  user?: { id: string; name: string; email: string; image: string | null };\n  connectedUser?: { id: string; name: string; email: string; image: string | null };\n}\n\nexport interface CreateConnectionInput {\n  email: string;\n}\n\nexport interface PrivacySettings {\n  id: string;\n  userId: string;\n  profileVisibility: \"private\" | \"connections_only\" | \"public\";\n  showPersonalityType: boolean;\n  showTraits: boolean;\n  showGrowthAreas: boolean;\n  createdAt: string;\n  updatedAt: string;\n}\n\nexport interface UpdatePrivacySettingsInput {\n  profileVisibility?: \"private\" | \"connections_only\" | \"public\";\n  showPersonalityType?: boolean;\n  showTraits?: boolean;\n  showGrowthAreas?: boolean;\n}\n```\n\nAlso update `DashboardStats` to include `connectionsCount: number`.\n\nAlso update `PersonalityProfile` to include:\n```typescript\nbehavioralTendencies: string | null;\nstressTendencies: string | null;\ncommunicationPreferences: string | null;\n```\n\nAnd `UpdateProfileInput` to include those as optional fields.\n\n## 2. Create Connections page (`/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Connections.tsx`)\n\nA social connections management page:\n\n- **Header**: \"Connections\" title with \"Connect with others to share insights and grow together\" subtitle\n- **Add connection section**: A glass card at the top with an email input and \"Send Request\" button\n- **Tabs**: \"My Connections\" | \"Pending Requests\"\n- **My Connections tab**: Grid of connection cards showing:\n  - Avatar with initials\n  - Name and email\n  - Connected since date\n  - \"Remove\" button (subtle, destructive)\n  - Empty state: \"No connections yet. Send a request to get started.\"\n- **Pending Requests tab**: List of incoming requests with:\n  - Avatar, name, email\n  - \"Accept\" and \"Decline\" buttons\n  - Empty state: \"No pending requests.\"\n\nUse React Query for fetching (`/api/connections`) and mutations for accept/decline/remove/create.\nUse toast notifications for success/error.\nUse the glass card style and staggered animations.\n\nExtract sub-components:\n- `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/connections/ConnectionCard.tsx`\n- `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/connections/PendingRequestCard.tsx`\n- `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/connections/AddConnectionForm.tsx`\n\n## 3. Add Privacy Settings to Profile page\n\nUpdate `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Profile.tsx` to add a \"Privacy \u0026 Visibility\" section below the existing personality section. Read the existing file first.\n\nAdd this section as a glass card:\n- **Profile Visibility**: Radio group or select with options: Private, Connections Only, Public\n- **Toggle switches** for:\n  - Show personality type\n  - Show traits\n  - Show growth areas\n- Auto-save when changed (use mutation with debounce or save on change)\n- Use React Query to fetch from `/api/privacy` and mutations to update\n\nAlso add the new personality profile fields (behavioralTendencies, stressTendencies, communicationPreferences) to the profile display if they have values.\n\n## 4. Update navigation\n\nUpdate `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/layout/SidebarNav.tsx` to add \"Connections\" nav item between \"Insights\" and \"Profile\":\n```typescript\n{ to: \"/connections\", icon: Users, label: \"Connections\" },\n```\nImport `Users` from lucide-react.\n\n## 5. Update App.tsx routes\n\nUpdate `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/App.tsx` to add the Connections route:\n```typescript\nimport Connections from \"@/pages/Connections\";\n// In the protected routes:\n\u003cRoute path=\"/connections\" element={\u003cConnections /\u003e} /\u003e\n```\n\n## 6. Update StatsRow\n\nUpdate `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/dashboard/StatsRow.tsx` to show 5 stats instead of 4 — add a \"Connections\" stat using the `Users` icon. Change the grid to `grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-5`.\n\n## Design guidelines:\n- Use `font-display` for headings (Playfair Display)\n- Use `glass` class for cards\n- Use `animate-fade-in-up` with staggered delays\n- Use `text-primary` for amber accents, `text-accent` for teal\n- Use shadcn/ui components (Button, Input, Tabs, Switch, Select, Avatar, Badge, Card)\n- Use `toast` from sonner for notifications\n- Make everything responsive\n- Keep the calm, intelligent, trustworthy aesthetic","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

### Vibecode Agent
*2/23/2026, 1:13:03 PM*

Prompt 2 is complete. Here's what was added:

**Database (extended):**
- **User model**: Added `personalityType`, `personalityScores`, `communicationStyle`, `growthFocus` fields
- **PersonalityProfile**: Added `behavioralTendencies`, `stressTendencies`, `communicationPreferences`
- **Connection model**: Full social connections with pending/accepted/declined status and unique constraint
- **PrivacySettings model**: Profile visibility (private/connections_only/public) with granular toggle controls

**New API endpoints:**
- `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/connections` — Full connection management
- `GET/PUT /api/privacy` — Privacy settings with auto-create defaults
- `GET /api/dashboard/stats` — Dashboard statistics including connection count
- `GET/PUT /api/profile/user` — User-level personality field management

**Frontend (new):**
- **Connections page** with add-by-email, accepted connections grid, and pending requests tab
- **Privacy settings** section on Profile page with visibility select and toggle switches
- Updated sidebar navigation with Connections link
- Dashboard stats now shows 5 metrics including connections

**Auth fix:** OTP emails now actually send via Vibecode SDK instead of just logging to console.

You should see the app in your preview. Try logging in with your email to test. Ready for prompt 3 when you are.

### User
*2/23/2026, 2:00:27 PM*

Prompt 3: Core Application Interface

Create the main application interface with five primary tabs in the bottom navigation:

Home
Chat
Connections
Growth
Profile

Each tab must be designed with a clean, modern, professional interface.

Home tab must include:

• Daily Insight card
• Quick access to AI Chat
• Personality summary snapshot
• Growth suggestion

Chat tab must include:

• Conversational AI interface
• Message history
• Clean chat bubbles
• Input field

Connections tab must include:

• List of connections
• Add connection option
• View connection insights

Growth tab must include:

• Personality insights
• Growth recommendations
• Training content area

Profile tab must include:

• Personality overview
• Strengths
• Growth areas
• Settings

The interface must feel:

• Calm
• Intelligent
• Modern
• Professional

Avoid bright or childish design.

{"description":"Build bottom nav and theme","id":"019c8c18-770a-75bc-82ad-635324ee37ef","prompt":"I need you to create the bottom navigation component and update the AppLayout to use a bottom tab bar instead of the sidebar. The app is \"Personality Insights\" - a calm, intelligent, modern, professional app.\n\n## Requirements\n\n### Bottom Navigation\n- 5 tabs: Home, Chat, Connections, Growth, Profile\n- Fixed at the bottom of the screen on mobile\n- On desktop (md+), show as a compact bottom bar or a slim sidebar - but the prompt says \"bottom navigation\" so keep it as bottom nav on all screen sizes\n- Active tab should have a subtle glow/highlight using the primary color\n- Icons: Home, MessageCircle, Users, TrendingUp, User from lucide-react\n- Clean, minimal design - no labels on mobile, show labels on larger screens. Actually, show small labels always for clarity.\n\n### File: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/layout/BottomNav.tsx`\n\nCreate this new component:\n```tsx\nimport { NavLink, useLocation } from \"react-router-dom\";\nimport { Home, MessageCircle, Users, TrendingUp, User } from \"lucide-react\";\nimport { cn } from \"@/lib/utils\";\n\nconst tabs = [\n  { to: \"/\", icon: Home, label: \"Home\" },\n  { to: \"/chat\", icon: MessageCircle, label: \"Chat\" },\n  { to: \"/connections\", icon: Users, label: \"Connect\" },\n  { to: \"/growth\", icon: TrendingUp, label: \"Growth\" },\n  { to: \"/profile\", icon: User, label: \"Profile\" },\n];\n\nexport default function BottomNav() {\n  const location = useLocation();\n  \n  return (\n    \u003cnav className=\"fixed bottom-0 inset-x-0 z-50 border-t border-border/30 bg-card/80 backdrop-blur-xl safe-area-bottom\"\u003e\n      \u003cdiv className=\"flex items-center justify-around h-16 max-w-lg mx-auto px-2\"\u003e\n        {tabs.map((tab) =\u003e {\n          const isActive = tab.to === \"/\" \n            ? location.pathname === \"/\" \n            : location.pathname.startsWith(tab.to);\n          \n          return (\n            \u003cNavLink\n              key={tab.to}\n              to={tab.to}\n              className=\"flex flex-col items-center justify-center gap-0.5 flex-1 py-1 group\"\n            \u003e\n              \u003cdiv\n                className={cn(\n                  \"flex items-center justify-center w-10 h-10 rounded-2xl transition-all duration-300\",\n                  isActive\n                    ? \"bg-primary/12 text-primary scale-105\"\n                    : \"text-muted-foreground group-hover:text-foreground\"\n                )}\n              \u003e\n                \u003ctab.icon className={cn(\"h-5 w-5 transition-all duration-300\", isActive \u0026\u0026 \"stroke-[2.5]\")} /\u003e\n              \u003c/div\u003e\n              \u003cspan\n                className={cn(\n                  \"text-[10px] font-medium transition-all duration-300\",\n                  isActive ? \"text-primary\" : \"text-muted-foreground\"\n                )}\n              \u003e\n                {tab.label}\n              \u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003c/NavLink\u003e\n          );\n        })}\n      \u003c/div\u003e\n    \u003c/nav\u003e\n  );\n}\n```\n\n### File: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx`\n\nReplace the entire file with a new layout that uses bottom navigation instead of sidebar:\n\n```tsx\nimport { Outlet } from \"react-router-dom\";\nimport BottomNav from \"./BottomNav\";\n\nexport default function AppLayout() {\n  return (\n    \u003cdiv className=\"min-h-screen bg-background\"\u003e\n      \u003cmain className=\"pb-20\"\u003e\n        \u003cOutlet /\u003e\n      \u003c/main\u003e\n      \u003cBottomNav /\u003e\n    \u003c/div\u003e\n  );\n}\n```\n\nThis removes the sidebar entirely and uses a simple bottom nav. The `pb-20` gives space for the fixed bottom nav.\n\n### CSS Addition to `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/index.css`\n\nAdd this after the existing utilities:\n\n```css\n.safe-area-bottom {\n  padding-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px);\n}\n```\n\nNow please create the BottomNav.tsx file and update AppLayout.tsx and index.css exactly as described. Read the existing files first to make sure you're editing correctly.\n","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

{"description":"Redesign Home tab","id":"019c8c19-c99d-74c7-bdab-2864fef646c6","prompt":"Redesign the Home tab (Dashboard page) for the \"Personality Insights\" app. The current file is at `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`. I need you to completely rewrite it along with new sub-components.\n\n## Design Requirements\n- Calm, intelligent, modern, professional feel\n- Dark theme with golden primary color and teal accent\n- Uses existing design system: glass cards, font-display for headings, DM Sans for body, animate-fade-in-up animations with staggered delays\n- The app now uses bottom navigation (no sidebar), so pages should have their own header area\n\n## Home Tab Content Required:\n1. **Daily Insight Card** - A prominent card showing the latest insight for the day\n2. **Quick Access to AI Chat** - A tappable card that navigates to /chat\n3. **Personality Summary Snapshot** - A compact display of the user's personality type and top traits\n4. **Growth Suggestion** - A card with the latest growth recommendation\n\n## Data Sources\nThe app uses React Query with these existing API endpoints:\n- `api.get\u003cDashboardStats\u003e(\"/api/dashboard/stats\")` - returns `{ journalEntries, insightsReceived, chatConversations, daysActive, connectionsCount }`\n- `api.get\u003cInsight[]\u003e(\"/api/insights?limit=3\")` - returns array of insights\n- `api.get\u003cPersonalityProfile\u003e(\"/api/profile\")` - returns personality profile\n\nTypes are imported from `@/types`:\n```ts\ninterface PersonalityProfile {\n  personalityType: string | null;\n  traits: string | null; // comma-separated\n  strengths: string | null; // comma-separated\n  growthAreas: string | null; // comma-separated\n  assessmentCompleted: boolean;\n  // ...more fields\n}\n\ninterface Insight {\n  id: string;\n  type: \"daily\" | \"weekly\" | \"personality\" | \"growth\" | \"relationship\";\n  title: string;\n  content: string;\n  isRead: boolean;\n  createdAt: string;\n}\n\ninterface DashboardStats {\n  journalEntries: number;\n  insightsReceived: number;\n  chatConversations: number;\n  daysActive: number;\n  connectionsCount: number;\n}\n```\n\n## Implementation\n\n### Create: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/home/DailyInsightCard.tsx`\nA prominent glass card with:\n- A small \"Daily Insight\" label at top with a Sparkles icon\n- The insight title in font-display\n- The insight content (2-3 line clamp)\n- If no insight, show a gentle empty state saying \"Your daily insight will appear here as you use the app\"\n- A subtle border-l-2 with primary color\n\n### Create: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/home/AIChatCard.tsx`\nA tappable card that links to /chat:\n- MessageCircle icon with primary color\n- \"Chat with AI\" heading\n- \"Get personalized insights through conversation\" subtext\n- An ArrowRight icon to indicate tappable\n- Use Link from react-router-dom\n- Should feel like a prominent action card\n\n### Create: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/home/PersonalitySnapshot.tsx`\nA compact card showing:\n- User's personality type (if set) as the heading\n- Top 3 traits as small badges\n- If no profile yet, show \"Complete your profile\" call-to-action linking to /chat\n- Use the `parseCSV` helper to split comma-separated values\n\n### Create: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/home/GrowthSuggestionCard.tsx`\nA card showing:\n- TrendingUp icon with accent color\n- \"Growth Focus\" label\n- The latest growth-type insight content, or a default message\n- Link to /growth for more\n\n### Rewrite: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`\nThe main Home page that combines all the above:\n- Greeting with time-based logic (keep existing pattern)\n- Fetches stats, insights, and profile via React Query\n- Layout: greeting at top, then DailyInsightCard, then a 2-column grid with AIChatCard and PersonalitySnapshot side by side, then GrowthSuggestionCard\n- On mobile, everything stacks vertically\n- Loading skeleton state\n- Staggered animations\n\nUse `useSession` from `@/lib/auth-client` for user name. Use `api` from `@/lib/api`. Use `useQuery` from `@tanstack/react-query`.\n\n**Important**: Keep `date-fns` imports if needed (it's already a dependency). Use `formatDistanceToNow` for time display.\n\nPlease create all 4 new component files and rewrite Dashboard.tsx. Make sure there are no TypeScript errors.","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

{"description":"Build Growth tab page","id":"019c8c1a-09d8-714b-a415-bd2ffcaa8ab2","prompt":"Build a new Growth tab page for the \"Personality Insights\" app. This tab combines personality insights, growth recommendations, and training content.\n\n## Design Requirements\n- Calm, intelligent, modern, professional feel\n- Dark theme with golden primary color (`primary`) and teal accent (`accent`)\n- Uses existing design system: glass cards, font-display for headings, DM Sans body, animate-fade-in-up with staggered delays\n- Bottom nav app, so pages have their own header\n\n## Growth Tab Content Required:\n1. **Personality Insights** section - Shows the user's insights filtered by type\n2. **Growth Recommendations** - Personalized growth tips\n3. **Training Content Area** - Curated content areas for self-improvement\n\n## Data Sources\nThe app uses React Query with these existing API endpoints:\n- `api.get\u003cInsight[]\u003e(\"/api/insights\")` - returns array of insights\n- `api.get\u003cPersonalityProfile\u003e(\"/api/profile\")` - returns personality profile\n\nTypes (from `@/types`):\n```ts\ninterface Insight {\n  id: string;\n  userId: string;\n  type: \"daily\" | \"weekly\" | \"personality\" | \"growth\" | \"relationship\";\n  title: string;\n  content: string;\n  metadata: string | null;\n  isRead: boolean;\n  createdAt: string;\n}\n\ninterface PersonalityProfile {\n  personalityType: string | null;\n  traits: string | null;\n  strengths: string | null;\n  growthAreas: string | null;\n  values: string | null;\n  communicationStyle: string | null;\n  behavioralTendencies: string | null;\n  stressTendencies: string | null;\n  communicationPreferences: string | null;\n  assessmentCompleted: boolean;\n}\n```\n\nAlso has: `api.patch\u003cInsight\u003e(\"/api/insights/${id}/read\")` for marking insights as read.\n\n## Implementation\n\n### Create: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/growth/InsightsSection.tsx`\nShows personality \u0026 growth insights:\n- Section header \"Your Insights\" with Lightbulb icon\n- Filter chips/tabs: All, Personality, Growth, Relationship\n- List of insight cards with:\n  - Left border color based on type (primary for personality, accent for growth, muted for relationship)\n  - Title, content (line-clamp-3), time ago\n  - Unread dot indicator\n  - onClick mark as read\n- Empty state if no insights\n\n### Create: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/growth/GrowthRecommendations.tsx`\nGrowth recommendations section:\n- Section header \"Growth Recommendations\" with Target icon (from lucide)\n- If user has growthAreas in their profile, show them as actionable recommendations\n- Each growth area displayed in a card with a subtle glass effect\n- If no profile/growth areas, show a card encouraging them to chat with AI to discover growth areas\n- Parse growthAreas from comma-separated string\n\n### Create: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/growth/TrainingContent.tsx`\nTraining content area:\n- Section header \"Training \u0026 Resources\" with BookOpen icon\n- Show curated training categories as cards:\n  - \"Self-Awareness\" - with Brain icon\n  - \"Communication\" - with MessageSquare icon\n  - \"Emotional Intelligence\" - with Heart icon\n  - \"Leadership\" - with Crown icon\n- Each card has an icon, title, and brief description\n- These are static for now (will be dynamic later)\n- Make them look like tappable cards even though they don't navigate anywhere yet\n- Use glass styling\n\n### Create: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Growth.tsx`\nThe main Growth page:\n- Page header with title \"Growth\" and subtitle\n- Fetches insights and profile via React Query\n- Layout: InsightsSection first, then GrowthRecommendations, then TrainingContent\n- Loading skeleton state\n- Staggered animations with animationDelay and animationFillMode: \"backwards\"\n\nImport patterns:\n```ts\nimport { useQuery, useMutation, useQueryClient } from \"@tanstack/react-query\";\nimport { api } from \"@/lib/api\";\nimport type { Insight, PersonalityProfile } from \"@/types\";\n```\n\nUse `formatDistanceToNow` from `date-fns` for time display. Use `toast` from `sonner` for error messages. Use `cn` from `@/lib/utils` for class merging.\n\nMake sure there are no TypeScript errors. Create all files.","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

{"description":"Clean up routing","id":"019c8c1f-9d6a-7486-8b3d-67ac0cf26b6f","prompt":"Clean up the App.tsx routing at `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/App.tsx`. \n\nThe app now has 5 main tabs: Home (/), Chat (/chat), Connections (/connections), Growth (/growth), Profile (/profile).\n\nThe old Journal (/journal) and Insights (/insights) routes should still work but redirect to the appropriate new pages. Journal should redirect to /chat, and Insights should redirect to /growth.\n\nRead the current App.tsx and update it:\n1. Keep Journal and Insights imports but wrap them as redirects instead. Actually, simpler approach: just use Navigate from react-router-dom to redirect:\n   - `/journal` → redirect to `/`\n   - `/insights` → redirect to `/growth`\n\nReplace the Journal and Insights route elements with:\n```tsx\nimport { BrowserRouter, Routes, Route, Navigate } from \"react-router-dom\";\n```\nAnd:\n```tsx\n\u003cRoute path=\"/journal\" element={\u003cNavigate to=\"/\" replace /\u003e} /\u003e\n\u003cRoute path=\"/insights\" element={\u003cNavigate to=\"/growth\" replace /\u003e} /\u003e\n```\n\nRemove the Journal and Insights page imports since they're no longer directly used.\n\nAlso delete the old SidebarNav.tsx and UserFooter.tsx files since they're no longer imported by AppLayout. Actually, don't delete them — just leave them. They're not hurting anything and the layout no longer imports them.\n\nRead App.tsx first and make these changes.\n","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

### Vibecode Agent
*2/23/2026, 2:12:42 PM*

All tasks are complete. Here's a summary of what was built:

**Bottom Navigation** - Replaced the sidebar with a fixed 5-tab bottom nav bar (Home, Chat, Connect, Growth, Profile) with active state highlighting and glassmorphic backdrop blur.

**Home Tab** - Redesigned with:
- Time-based greeting
- Daily Insight card with unread indicator
- Quick access AI Chat card
- Personality summary snapshot (type + top traits)
- Growth suggestion card
- Compact stats row

**Chat Tab** - Updated height calculations to account for bottom nav. Keeps the full conversation list + chat interface.

**Connections Tab** - Narrowed to mobile-friendly width, added bottom padding, stacked layout for connection cards.

**Growth Tab** (new) - Built with three sections:
- Filterable insights (All, Personality, Growth, Relationship) with mark-as-read
- Growth recommendations from personality profile
- Training & resources cards (Self-Awareness, Communication, Emotional Intelligence, Leadership)

**Profile Tab** - Narrowed to mobile-friendly width, kept all personality sections, added sign-out button in settings.

**Routing** - Old `/journal` redirects to `/`, old `/insights` redirects to `/growth`. TypeScript compiles cleanly with zero errors.

### User
*2/23/2026, 2:14:28 PM*

Prompt 4: Adaptive AI Personality Coach System

Integrate Claude AI to function as an adaptive personality-aware coach.

The AI must:

Adapt its tone and communication style based on the user's personality type:

High D personality:

• concise
• direct
• outcome-focused

High I personality:

• encouraging
• optimistic
• relational

High S personality:

• calm
• supportive
• steady

High C personality:

• logical
• structured
• precise

The AI must also adapt based on:

• user communication style
• emotional context
• type of question asked

The AI must always feel:

• intelligent
• emotionally aware
• respectful
• helpful

Never robotic.

Never generic.

The AI should help users understand themselves, improve relationships, and grow personally.

Store chat history securely.

{"description":"Build adaptive AI coach backend","id":"019c8c25-af85-7465-99eb-38ffb21f219f","prompt":"I need to significantly upgrade the AI personality coach system in the backend chat route. The file is at `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/chat.ts`.\n\n## Current State\nThe current `buildSystemPrompt()` function is basic - it just appends personality data points to a generic prompt. It needs to become a sophisticated adaptive coach that changes its communication style based on the user's DISC personality type.\n\n## Requirements\n\nReplace the `buildSystemPrompt` function with a comprehensive version that:\n\n### 1. DISC-Based Tone Adaptation\nThe AI must adapt its communication style based on the user's personality type:\n\n- **High D (Dominant/Driver)**: concise, direct, outcome-focused. Skip small talk, lead with the answer, use bullet points, focus on results and action items.\n- **High I (Influential/Inspiring)**: encouraging, optimistic, relational. Use warm language, celebrate progress, tell stories, focus on relationships and possibilities.\n- **High S (Steady/Supportive)**: calm, supportive, steady. Be patient, validate feelings, take things step-by-step, provide reassurance, avoid pressure.\n- **High C (Conscientious/Analytical)**: logical, structured, precise. Use data and frameworks, provide thorough explanations, cite reasoning, be methodical.\n\n### 2. Full Profile Context\nInclude ALL available profile fields in the AI context:\n- personalityType\n- traits\n- strengths  \n- growthAreas\n- values\n- communicationStyle\n- behavioralTendencies\n- stressTendencies\n- communicationPreferences\n\n### 3. Emotional Intelligence\nThe AI should:\n- Detect emotional context from the user's message\n- Adjust response style accordingly\n- Be empathetic without being patronizing\n- Never be robotic or generic\n\n### 4. Conversation Context\nUse the conversation's `context` field if it exists to shape the AI's approach.\n\n### 5. User's Name\nInclude the user's name in the system prompt so the AI can use it naturally (not every message, but occasionally).\n\n## Implementation\n\nHere's exactly what the updated `buildSystemPrompt` function should look like. Replace the existing function AND update the message endpoint to pass the user's name and conversation context.\n\nRead the current file first, then make these changes:\n\n### Updated `buildSystemPrompt` function:\n\n```typescript\nfunction getDiscStyleInstructions(personalityType: string | null): string {\n  if (!personalityType) return \"\";\n  \n  const type = personalityType.toLowerCase();\n  \n  // DISC-based style adaptation\n  if (type.includes(\"d\") \u0026\u0026 (type.includes(\"dominant\") || type.includes(\"driver\") || type === \"d\" || type.startsWith(\"high d\"))) {\n    return `\nCOMMUNICATION STYLE DIRECTIVE — High D (Dominant/Driver):\n- Be concise and direct. Lead with the answer, then explain if needed.\n- Focus on outcomes, results, and action items.\n- Skip unnecessary pleasantries and small talk.\n- Use bullet points and clear structure.\n- Respect their time — don't over-explain.\n- Challenge them productively — they appreciate directness.\n- Frame growth in terms of results and competitive advantage.`;\n  }\n  \n  if (type.includes(\"i\") \u0026\u0026 (type.includes(\"influen\") || type.includes(\"inspir\") || type === \"i\" || type.startsWith(\"high i\"))) {\n    return `\nCOMMUNICATION STYLE DIRECTIVE — High I (Influential/Inspiring):\n- Be warm, enthusiastic, and encouraging.\n- Celebrate their progress and strengths openly.\n- Use stories, analogies, and relatable examples.\n- Focus on relationships, possibilities, and big-picture thinking.\n- Keep the tone optimistic and energizing.\n- Use conversational, friendly language.\n- Frame growth in terms of impact on relationships and personal expression.`;\n  }\n  \n  if (type.includes(\"s\") \u0026\u0026 (type.includes(\"steady\") || type.includes(\"support\") || type === \"s\" || type.startsWith(\"high s\"))) {\n    return `\nCOMMUNICATION STYLE DIRECTIVE — High S (Steady/Supportive):\n- Be calm, patient, and reassuring.\n- Take things step-by-step — don't rush.\n- Validate their feelings before offering advice.\n- Provide stability and consistency in your responses.\n- Avoid pressure or urgency in your language.\n- Use gentle, supportive phrasing.\n- Frame growth as a natural, gradual process — no sudden changes needed.`;\n  }\n  \n  if (type.includes(\"c\") \u0026\u0026 (type.includes(\"conscien\") || type.includes(\"analytic\") || type === \"c\" || type.startsWith(\"high c\"))) {\n    return `\nCOMMUNICATION STYLE DIRECTIVE — High C (Conscientious/Analytical):\n- Be logical, structured, and precise.\n- Provide thorough explanations with clear reasoning.\n- Use frameworks, models, and data when relevant.\n- Be methodical — organize thoughts with numbered steps or categories.\n- Cite psychological principles where appropriate.\n- Avoid vague or overly emotional language.\n- Frame growth in terms of systematic improvement and measurable progress.`;\n  }\n  \n  // Fallback for other personality types (MBTI, Big Five, etc.)\n  return `\nThe user's personality type is \"${personalityType}\". Adapt your communication style to match what would resonate best with this personality profile.`;\n}\n\nfunction buildSystemPrompt(context: {\n  profile: {\n    personalityType: string | null;\n    traits: string | null;\n    strengths: string | null;\n    growthAreas: string | null;\n    values: string | null;\n    communicationStyle: string | null;\n    behavioralTendencies: string | null;\n    stressTendencies: string | null;\n    communicationPreferences: string | null;\n  } | null;\n  userName: string | null;\n  conversationContext: string | null;\n}): string {\n  const { profile, userName, conversationContext } = context;\n  \n  let systemPrompt = `You are an adaptive personality coach within the Personality Insights platform. You are intelligent, emotionally aware, respectful, and genuinely helpful. You help users understand themselves, improve their relationships, and grow personally.\n\nCORE PRINCIPLES:\n- Never be robotic or generic. Every response should feel personal and thoughtful.\n- Be emotionally intelligent — read between the lines of what users say.\n- Adapt your depth based on the question: simple questions get clear answers, deep questions get reflective exploration.\n- Use the user's own language patterns back to them when it feels natural.\n- Balance warmth with substance — be kind but never empty.\n- When you notice emotional weight in a message, acknowledge it before moving to advice.\n- Help users see patterns they might not see themselves.\n- Offer frameworks for thinking, not just answers.`;\n\n  if (userName) {\n    systemPrompt += `\\n\\nThe user's name is ${userName}. Use it occasionally and naturally — not in every response, but enough to feel personal.`;\n  }\n\n  if (profile) {\n    // Add DISC-based communication style\n    const discStyle = getDiscStyleInstructions(profile.personalityType);\n    if (discStyle) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\n${discStyle}`;\n    }\n\n    systemPrompt += `\\n\\nUSER PERSONALITY PROFILE:`;\n    \n    if (profile.personalityType) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nPersonality Type: ${profile.personalityType}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.traits) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nKey Traits: ${profile.traits}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.strengths) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nStrengths: ${profile.strengths}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.growthAreas) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nGrowth Areas: ${profile.growthAreas}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.values) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nCore Values: ${profile.values}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.communicationStyle) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nCommunication Style: ${profile.communicationStyle}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.behavioralTendencies) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nBehavioral Tendencies: ${profile.behavioralTendencies}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.stressTendencies) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nStress Response Patterns: ${profile.stressTendencies}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.communicationPreferences) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nCommunication Preferences: ${profile.communicationPreferences}`;\n    }\n\n    systemPrompt += `\\n\\nLeverage this profile to personalize your coaching. Reference specific traits when relevant, but weave them in naturally — don't list them back to the user. Your goal is to make the user feel deeply understood.`;\n  } else {\n    systemPrompt += `\\n\\nThis user hasn't completed their personality profile yet. Gently encourage self-exploration through your conversation. Ask reflective questions that help them discover their patterns. If appropriate, suggest they explore the personality assessment features of the platform.`;\n  }\n\n  if (conversationContext) {\n    systemPrompt += `\\n\\nCONVERSATION CONTEXT: ${conversationContext}`;\n  }\n\n  return systemPrompt;\n}\n```\n\n### Updated message endpoint (POST /conversations/:id/messages):\n\nIn the section where we call `buildSystemPrompt`, change it from:\n```typescript\nconst response = await anthropic.messages.create({\n  model: \"claude-sonnet-4-20250514\",\n  max_tokens: 1024,\n  system: buildSystemPrompt(profile),\n  messages: anthropicMessages,\n});\n```\n\nTo:\n```typescript\nconst response = await anthropic.messages.create({\n  model: \"claude-sonnet-4-20250514\",\n  max_tokens: 1024,\n  system: buildSystemPrompt({\n    profile,\n    userName: user.name ?? null,\n    conversationContext: conversation.context,\n  }),\n  messages: anthropicMessages,\n});\n```\n\nMake sure the `user` object from auth has a `name` field (it should from Better Auth). The `conversation` object already has `context` from the earlier query.\n\nRead the file first, then apply the changes. Make sure there are no TypeScript errors.\n","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:backend-developer"}

*2/23/2026, 2:20:24 PM*

All done. Here's what was implemented:

**Backend — Adaptive AI Coach System** (`backend/src/routes/chat.ts`):
- **DISC-based tone adaptation**: The AI detects the user's personality type and adjusts its entire communication style:
  - High D: concise, direct, outcome-focused, no small talk
  - High I: warm, encouraging, story-driven, relational
  - High S: calm, patient, validates feelings, step-by-step
  - High C: logical, structured, data-driven, methodical
- **Full personality context**: All 9 profile fields (traits, strengths, growth areas, values, communication style, behavioral tendencies, stress patterns, communication preferences) are injected into the system prompt
- **User's name** included for natural personalization
- **Conversation context** shapes the coaching approach
- **Emotionally intelligent core**: The AI reads between the lines, acknowledges emotional weight before advice, and helps users see patterns
- **No-profile fallback**: For new users without a profile, the AI gently encourages self-exploration

**Frontend — Coach UX** (`webapp/src/components/chat/`):
- Welcome screen with "Your AI Coach" branding and Sparkles icon
- Three quick-start suggestion chips: personality understanding, communication, and growth focus
- Updated input placeholder: "Ask your coach anything..."
- Chat history stored securely in SQLite via Prisma

### User
*2/24/2026, 9:47:03 AM*

Prompt 5: Daily Insight System

Create a system that generates one personalized daily personality insight per user.

The insight must be based on:

• personality type
• growth areas
• behavioral tendencies

Each insight must:

• be encouraging
• be practical
• be concise

Display the insight on the Home screen.

Prepare the system for push notifications in the future.

Store insights in the database.

### System — `Executing tool: Task`
*2/24/2026, 9:49:13 AM*

{"description":"Build backend daily insight system","id":"019c9057-1947-795a-9fcf-e57abf793705","prompt":"You need to build the daily insight generation system for the backend. Here's what needs to happen:\n\n## Context\nThis is a personality coaching app. The backend is at /home/user/workspace/backend/ using Hono + Prisma (SQLite) + Anthropic API. The Insight model already exists in the schema.\n\n## 1. Update Prisma Schema\nAdd a `PushToken` model and a `lastInsightGeneratedAt` field to User for push notification readiness and insight scheduling.\n\nEdit `/home/user/workspace/backend/prisma/schema.prisma`:\n- Add to User model: `lastInsightGeneratedAt DateTime?` and a relation to `pushTokens PushToken[]`\n- Add new model:\n```prisma\nmodel PushToken {\n  id        String   @id @default(cuid())\n  userId    String\n  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  token     String\n  platform  String   @default(\"web\") // \"ios\", \"android\", \"web\"\n  createdAt DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt\n\n  @@unique([userId, token])\n}\n```\n\nAfter editing, run: `cd /home/user/workspace/backend \u0026\u0026 bunx prisma db push`\n\n## 2. Create the Daily Insight Generation Route\nCreate/update `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/insights.ts` to add a `POST /api/insights/daily/generate` endpoint that:\n\n1. Checks if user is authenticated\n2. Checks if user already has a daily insight from today (compare dates using UTC - if the most recent \"daily\" insight was created today, return it instead of generating a new one)\n3. Fetches the user's PersonalityProfile\n4. Fetches the user's 5 most recent journal entries\n5. Fetches the user's 3 most recent chat conversations with their last 2 messages each\n6. Builds a prompt for Claude (use the existing Anthropic client pattern from chat.ts):\n\nSystem prompt: \"You are a personality coach. Generate ONE personalized daily insight for the user. The insight must be encouraging, practical, and concise (2-3 sentences max for content). Return a JSON object with exactly two fields: `title` (a short inspiring title, max 8 words) and `content` (the insight body, 2-3 sentences, encouraging and practical).\"\n\nUser prompt: Include the personality profile data, recent journal moods/themes, and recent chat topics.\n\n7. Parse the AI response as JSON\n8. Store the insight in the database with type=\"daily\" and metadata containing generation context\n9. Update user's lastInsightGeneratedAt\n10. Return the insight\n\nUse the same Anthropic SDK pattern as in chat.ts. The Anthropic API key comes from `env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. Use model `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`.\n\nAlso add a `GET /api/insights/daily` endpoint that returns just today's daily insight (or null if none).\n\n## 3. Update types.ts\nEdit `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/types.ts` to add:\n```typescript\n// ─── Daily Insight Generation ───────────────────────────────────────\nexport const GenerateDailyInsightResponseSchema = z.object({\n  title: z.string(),\n  content: z.string(),\n});\n\nexport type GenerateDailyInsightResponse = z.infer\u003ctypeof GenerateDailyInsightResponseSchema\u003e;\n\n// ─── Push Token ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nexport const RegisterPushTokenSchema = z.object({\n  token: z.string().min(1),\n  platform: z.enum([\"ios\", \"android\", \"web\"]).default(\"web\"),\n});\n\nexport type RegisterPushTokenInput = z.infer\u003ctypeof RegisterPushTokenSchema\u003e;\n```\n\n## Important Implementation Details\n- The env module is at `../env` and exports `env` with `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`\n- Prisma client is at `../prisma` and exports `prisma`\n- Use `import Anthropic from \"@anthropic-ai/sdk\"` \n- Auth types: `import type { User, Session } from \"../auth\"`\n- All app routes return `{ data: ... }` envelope\n- Error responses use `{ error: { message, code } }` pattern\n- For date comparison, use start of today UTC: `new Date(new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0])`\n- If no personality profile exists, generate a generic encouraging insight about self-discovery\n","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:backend-developer"}

{"description":"Build frontend daily insight UI","id":"019c905a-394e-7381-bd9b-3efd2d61bca5","prompt":"You need to enhance the frontend Daily Insight system for the Home screen. The app is at /home/user/workspace/webapp/. It uses React, TailwindCSS, shadcn/ui, Tanstack React Query, and date-fns.\n\n## What already exists\n- `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/home/DailyInsightCard.tsx` - shows an insight or placeholder\n- `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx` - Home page that fetches insights from `/api/insights?limit=3`\n- `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/lib/api.ts` - API client (auto-unwraps `{ data: T }`)\n- `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/types/index.ts` - TypeScript types\n\n## New Backend Endpoints\n- `GET /api/insights/daily` - Returns today's daily insight or `null`\n- `POST /api/insights/daily/generate` - Generates a new daily insight (idempotent per day)\n\n## Changes Needed\n\n### 1. Update DailyInsightCard.tsx\nReplace the current component with a smarter version that:\n- Receives `insight` prop (Insight | null | undefined)\n- Has a \"Generate Today's Insight\" button when no insight exists (or shows the placeholder text)\n- Shows a loading/generating state with a shimmer animation when generating\n- When an insight exists and is unread, shows the \"New\" badge and allows tapping to mark as read\n- Has a subtle refresh button (RefreshCw icon) that regenerates the insight (this will only work once per day due to idempotency)\n- Uses `useMutation` from tanstack query to call `POST /api/insights/daily/generate`\n- After generating, invalidates the `[\"daily-insight\"]` query key\n- Uses `useMutation` to call `PATCH /api/insights/{id}/read` when user taps to mark as read\n\nDesign notes for the card:\n- Keep the existing glass/primary style but enhance it\n- The generate button should be a nice gradient button (primary color)\n- Add a subtle sparkle/glow animation when a new insight appears\n- Show the insight creation time using `formatDistanceToNow` from date-fns\n- The card should have a smooth transition when switching from placeholder to insight\n\n### 2. Update Dashboard.tsx  \nModify the Dashboard to:\n- Fetch today's daily insight using a dedicated query: `useQuery({ queryKey: [\"daily-insight\"], queryFn: () =\u003e api.get\u003cInsight | null\u003e(\"/api/insights/daily\") })`\n- Auto-trigger generation on mount if no daily insight exists (use a `useEffect` that calls the generate mutation if data is null and user has a profile)\n- Pass the daily insight to DailyInsightCard\n- Keep all other existing functionality (stats, growth insight, personality snapshot, AI chat card)\n- Still fetch recent insights for the growth card\n\n### 3. Important API patterns\n```typescript\n// GET (auto-unwraps { data: T })\nconst insight = await api.get\u003cInsight | null\u003e(\"/api/insights/daily\");\n\n// POST (auto-unwraps { data: T })\nconst generated = await api.post\u003cInsight\u003e(\"/api/insights/daily/generate\");\n\n// PATCH (auto-unwraps { data: T })\nconst updated = await api.patch\u003cInsight\u003e(`/api/insights/${id}/read`);\n```\n\nImport types from `@/types`:\n```typescript\nimport type { Insight } from \"@/types\";\n```\n\nImport API from `@/lib/api`:\n```typescript\nimport { api } from \"@/lib/api\";\n```\n\nUse React Query:\n```typescript\nimport { useQuery, useMutation, useQueryClient } from \"@tanstack/react-query\";\n```\n\n### Design System\nThe app uses a dark theme with these key CSS variables/classes:\n- `glass` class for glassmorphism cards\n- `bg-primary` = warm gold (38 92% 55%)\n- `bg-accent` = teal (165 35% 45%)\n- `text-foreground` for main text\n- `text-muted-foreground` for secondary text\n- `glow-primary` class for a subtle glow effect\n- `animate-fade-in-up` for entrance animation\n- Font: DM Sans (body), Playfair Display (display/font-display class)\n- Use `lucide-react` for icons (Sparkles, RefreshCw, Sun, etc.)\n\nKeep the aesthetic dark, sophisticated, and calm. No generic AI vibes. The DailyInsightCard should feel premium and thoughtful.\n","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

### Vibecode Agent
*2/24/2026, 9:55:52 AM*

The backend is running and the new endpoints are working: `GET /api/insights/daily` returns 200 and `POST /api/insights/daily/generate` is accessible. The routes are properly registered. I can see a user is already logged in and the endpoints are responding correctly.

Now let me check there's a route ordering issue -- the `/:id/read` parameterized route might catch `/daily` and `/daily/generate`. Let me verify.

Looking at the insights route file again -- the routes are defined in this order:
1. `GET /` (line 20)
2. `PATCH /:id/read` (line 42)  
3. `GET /daily` (line 68)
4. `POST /daily/generate` (line 90)

The `/:id/read` route at line 42 would match `daily/read` but NOT `daily` or `daily/generate` since those paths don't have the `/read` suffix. However, to be safe and follow best practices, the static routes should come before the parameterized ones. But looking at the logs, the routes are returning 200, so Hono handles this correctly. Let me still fix the ordering to be safe.

However, let me verify the routes are actually working by checking that `GET /api/insights/daily` returned 200 in the logs -- yes it did (line 201 in the server logs). Everything is fine.

### Vibecode Agent
*2/24/2026, 9:56:40 AM*

The Daily Insight System is fully implemented. Here's what was built:

**Backend:**
- `GET /api/insights/daily` — Returns today's daily insight or null
- `POST /api/insights/daily/generate` — Generates a personalized insight using Claude AI, idempotent per day. Pulls context from the user's personality profile, recent journal entries (last 5), and recent chat conversations (last 3 with messages)
- `PushToken` model added to the database for future push notification support
- `lastInsightGeneratedAt` field on User for scheduling tracking

**Frontend:**
- DailyInsightCard now has three states: generating (shimmer skeleton), no insight (with "Generate Today's Insight" gold gradient button), and insight display (with title, content, time, mark-as-read, refresh)
- Auto-generates a daily insight on Home screen load if the user has completed their personality assessment
- Unread insights get a sparkle glow animation and "New" badge
- Mark as read button with loading spinner
- Refresh button (idempotent, won't duplicate within the same day)

**Push notifications** are prepared via the `PushToken` database model — ready to integrate when you're ready to add that feature.

Prompt 6: Privacy-First Connection System

Implement a privacy-first connection system.

Requirements:

User profiles must be private by default.

Users can:

• send connection requests
• accept or decline requests
• control profile visibility

Connection insights must only be visible to accepted connections.

No public follower counts.

No popularity metrics.

This system must prioritize emotional safety and trust.
