{"description":"Build privacy-first backend system","id":"019c9061-dedb-7ca2-977b-e894e8a6ce60","prompt":"You need to implement a comprehensive privacy-first connection system on the backend at /home/user/workspace/backend/. The backend uses Hono + Prisma (SQLite) + Better Auth.\n\n## IMPORTANT FILES TO READ FIRST\nRead these files before making any changes:\n- /home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/connections.ts\n- /home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/privacy.ts\n- /home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/profile.ts\n- /home/user/workspace/backend/src/types.ts\n- /home/user/workspace/backend/prisma/schema.prisma\n\n## CHANGES NEEDED\n\n### 1. Update Prisma Schema\nEdit `/home/user/workspace/backend/prisma/schema.prisma`:\n\nAdd to PrivacySettings model:\n```\n  showStrengths       Boolean  @default(false)\n  showValues          Boolean  @default(false)\n  showCommunicationStyle Boolean @default(false)\n```\n\nAfter editing, run: `cd /home/user/workspace/backend \u0026\u0026 bunx prisma db push`\n\n### 2. Update types.ts\nEdit `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/types.ts`:\n\nAdd to PrivacySettingsSchema:\n```typescript\n  showStrengths: z.boolean(),\n  showValues: z.boolean(),\n  showCommunicationStyle: z.boolean(),\n```\n\nAdd to UpdatePrivacySettingsSchema:\n```typescript\n  showStrengths: z.boolean().optional(),\n  showValues: z.boolean().optional(),\n  showCommunicationStyle: z.boolean().optional(),\n```\n\nAdd a new schema for connection profiles:\n```typescript\n// ─── Connection Profile (privacy-filtered) ──────────────────────────\nexport const ConnectionProfileSchema = z.object({\n  id: z.string(),\n  name: z.string(),\n  image: z.string().nullable(),\n  personalityType: z.string().nullable().optional(),\n  traits: z.string().nullable().optional(),\n  strengths: z.string().nullable().optional(),\n  growthAreas: z.string().nullable().optional(),\n  values: z.string().nullable().optional(),\n  communicationStyle: z.string().nullable().optional(),\n  connectedSince: z.string().nullable(),\n});\n\nexport type ConnectionProfile = z.infer\u003ctypeof ConnectionProfileSchema\u003e;\n```\n\n### 3. Rewrite connections.ts\nCompletely rewrite `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/connections.ts` with:\n\n**GET /api/connections** - Enhanced to return 3 categories:\n- Accepted connections (both directions)\n- Pending received requests\n- Pending sent requests (NEW - user can see what they sent)\n- EXCLUDE declined connections from the response\n\nInclude user data: `{ id, name, email, image }`\n\n**POST /api/connections** - Keep mostly the same but:\n- When a connection already exists with status \"declined\", DELETE the old record and create a fresh pending one (allows re-requesting after decline)\n- Still prevent self-connections and duplicates for non-declined\n\n**PATCH /api/connections/:id/accept** - Keep same\n\n**PATCH /api/connections/:id/decline** - Change to DELETE the record instead of updating status to \"declined\". This way a declined request is fully removed, allowing the sender to try again later.\n\n**DELETE /api/connections/:id** - Keep same (either party can remove)\n\n**GET /api/connections/:id/profile** - NEW endpoint:\n1. Verify the requesting user and the target user have an accepted connection\n2. The `:id` here is the CONNECTION id\n3. Fetch the other user's PersonalityProfile\n4. Fetch the other user's PrivacySettings\n5. Apply privacy filtering:\n   - If profileVisibility is \"private\": return only { id, name, image, connectedSince }\n   - If profileVisibility is \"connections_only\" or \"public\": return fields based on show* toggles\n   - showPersonalityType → include personalityType\n   - showTraits → include traits\n   - showStrengths → include strengths\n   - showGrowthAreas → include growthAreas\n   - showValues → include values\n   - showCommunicationStyle → include communicationStyle\n6. Return the filtered profile with the `{ data: ... }` envelope\n\n### 4. Update privacy.ts  \nEdit `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/privacy.ts`:\n\nAdd PATCH support alongside PUT (the frontend uses PATCH):\n```typescript\nprivacyRouter.patch(\n  \"/\",\n  zValidator(\"json\", UpdatePrivacySettingsSchema),\n  async (c) =\u003e {\n    // Same logic as PUT\n  }\n);\n```\n\nUpdate the GET defaults to include new fields:\n```typescript\ncreate: {\n  userId: user.id,\n  profileVisibility: \"private\",\n  showPersonalityType: true,\n  showTraits: false,\n  showGrowthAreas: false,\n  showStrengths: false,\n  showValues: false,\n  showCommunicationStyle: false,\n},\n```\n\n### PATTERNS TO FOLLOW\n- All routes return `{ data: ... }` envelope\n- Errors: `{ error: { message, code } }` with appropriate status codes\n- Auth check: `const user = c.get(\"user\"); if (!user) return 401`\n- Import Prisma from `../prisma`, types from `../types`, auth from `../auth`\n- Use zValidator for input validation\n","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:backend-developer"}

TOKEN2="xurKqQ8BP40XAycchA2tByjfENwF63QY"

# User 1 should see it in pendingSent
echo "=== GET connections (user 1 - pendingSent should have 1) ==="

  -H "Cookie: __Secure-better-auth.session_token=$TOKEN1" 2>&1 | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'accepted: {len(d[\"data\"][\"accepted\"])}, pendingReceived: {len(d[\"data\"][\"pendingReceived\"])}, pendingSent: {len(d[\"data\"][\"pendingSent\"])}')"

echo ""

# User 2 should see it in pendingReceived

  -H "Cookie: __Secure-better-auth.session_token=$TOKEN2" 2>&1 | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'accepted: {len(d[\"data\"][\"accepted\"])}, pendingReceived: {len(d[\"data\"][\"pendingReceived\"])}, pendingSent: {len(d[\"data\"][\"pendingSent\"])}')"

echo ""

# Now both users should see it in accepted

  -H "Cookie: __Secure-better-auth.session_token=$TOKEN1" 2>&1 | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'accepted: {len(d[\"data\"][\"accepted\"])}, pendingReceived: {len(d[\"data\"][\"pendingReceived\"])}, pendingSent: {len(d[\"data\"][\"pendingSent\"])}')" – Test connection listing categories and accept flow

### System — `Executing tool: Bash`
*2/24/2026, 10:05:34 AM*

bash → TOKEN1='GsAiuHotGqrR611pbMlENRRJXQkA6Bvg.%2FBWeJjtyzkOnDkPI270ue9ARourHaFRe51nZMw1QGTw%3D'
TOKEN2='I9wBdCKe9PkYqOv0A6L1MafzOqOanV6Z.M2kTVoV1At4Hctut1Q9PDAKTea3qa%2Fae5vYyznZuDbA%3D'

# First, delete the existing connection
echo "=== DELETE existing connection ==="
curl -s -X DELETE "$BACKEND_URL/api/connections/cmm0spjls0009m2a2bn4pans5" \
  -H "Cookie: __Secure-better-auth.session_token=$TOKEN1" 2>&1

echo ""

# Create a new connection request from user 1 to user 2
echo "=== POST new connection ==="
RESULT=$(curl -s -X POST "$BACKEND_URL/api/connections" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Cookie: __Secure-better-auth.session_token=$TOKEN1" \
  -d '{"email": "testconn2@test.com"}' 2>&1)
echo "$RESULT"

echo ""

# Extract the new connection ID
NEW_CONN_ID=$(echo "$RESULT" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['data']['id'])")
echo "New connection ID: $NEW_CONN_ID"

echo ""

# User 2 declines the request (should delete the record)
echo "=== PATCH decline (user 2 declines) ==="
curl -s -X PATCH "$BACKEND_URL/api/connections/$NEW_CONN_ID/decline" \
  -H "Cookie: __Secure-better-auth.session_token=$TOKEN2" 2>&1

echo ""

# Verify both users see empty connections
echo "=== GET connections (user 1, should be empty) ==="
curl -s "$BACKEND_URL/api/connections" \
  -H "Cookie: __Secure-better-auth.session_token=$TOKEN1" 2>&1

echo ""

# Now user 1 can re-request since the record was deleted
echo "=== POST re-request (should succeed since decline deleted the record) ==="
curl -s -X POST "$BACKEND_URL/api/connections" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Cookie: __Secure-better-auth.session_token=$TOKEN1" \
  -d '{"email": "testconn2@test.com"}' 2>&1 – Test decline flow and re-request after decline

{"description":"Build privacy-first frontend system","id":"019c9067-6884-7dbe-afa7-eec83a55cff6","prompt":"You need to implement the privacy-first connection system on the frontend at /home/user/workspace/webapp/. The app uses React, TailwindCSS, shadcn/ui, Tanstack React Query, date-fns, and lucide-react icons.\n\n## IMPORTANT: Read these files first\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/types/index.ts\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Connections.tsx\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Profile.tsx\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/connections/ConnectionCard.tsx\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/connections/PendingRequestCard.tsx\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/connections/AddConnectionForm.tsx\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/lib/api.ts\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/App.tsx (to see the router setup)\n\n## BACKEND API CHANGES\nThe `GET /api/connections` endpoint now returns a DIFFERENT shape:\n```typescript\n{ data: { accepted: Connection[], pendingReceived: Connection[], pendingSent: Connection[] } }\n```\nNOT a flat array anymore.\n\nNew endpoint: `GET /api/connections/:id/profile` returns a privacy-filtered profile.\n\nThe `PATCH /api/connections/:id/decline` now DELETES the record (returns `{ data: { success: true } }`).\n\nThe `PATCH /api/connections/:id/accept` uses the endpoint path, NOT a body payload.\n\n## CHANGES NEEDED\n\n### 1. Update types/index.ts\nAdd these types:\n\n```typescript\nexport interface ConnectionsResponse {\n  accepted: Connection[];\n  pendingReceived: Connection[];\n  pendingSent: Connection[];\n}\n\nexport interface ConnectionProfile {\n  id: string;\n  name: string;\n  image: string | null;\n  personalityType?: string | null;\n  traits?: string | null;\n  strengths?: string | null;\n  growthAreas?: string | null;\n  values?: string | null;\n  communicationStyle?: string | null;\n  connectedSince: string | null;\n}\n```\n\nUpdate PrivacySettings interface to add:\n```typescript\n  showStrengths: boolean;\n  showValues: boolean;\n  showCommunicationStyle: boolean;\n```\n\nUpdate UpdatePrivacySettingsInput to add:\n```typescript\n  showStrengths?: boolean;\n  showValues?: boolean;\n  showCommunicationStyle?: boolean;\n```\n\n### 2. Rewrite Connections.tsx\nComplete rewrite with 3 tabs:\n- \"Connections\" (accepted connections count)  \n- \"Received\" (pending received count with accent badge)\n- \"Sent\" (pending sent count)\n\nThe API call: `api.get\u003cConnectionsResponse\u003e(\"/api/connections\")`\n\n**Connections tab**: Show ConnectionCard for each accepted connection. Each card should have a \"View Profile\" button that opens a sheet/modal with the privacy-filtered profile.\n\n**Received tab**: Show PendingRequestCard for incoming requests with accept/decline buttons.\n\n**Sent tab**: NEW. Show SentRequestCard for outgoing pending requests with a \"Cancel\" button (calls DELETE /api/connections/:id).\n\nKeep the AddConnectionForm at the top. Add a subtle privacy notice below the form: \"Your profile is private by default. Only accepted connections can see details you choose to share.\"\n\nDesign: Keep the dark, sophisticated glass aesthetic. Use staggered animations. The privacy notice should use a Shield icon and muted styling.\n\n### 3. Fix PendingRequestCard.tsx\nThe accept mutation URL is WRONG. It currently sends:\n```\napi.patch(`/api/connections/${connection.id}`, { status: \"accepted\" })\n```\nIt should be:\n```\napi.patch(`/api/connections/${connection.id}/accept`)\n```\n\nSame for decline - should be:\n```\napi.patch(`/api/connections/${connection.id}/decline`)\n```\n\nNo body payload needed for either.\n\n### 4. Create SentRequestCard.tsx\nNew component at `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/connections/SentRequestCard.tsx`\n\nShows:\n- Avatar with initials\n- Name and email of the person the request was sent to\n- \"Pending\" badge in muted style\n- \"Cancel Request\" button (ghost, with X icon)\n- Uses DELETE /api/connections/:id to cancel\n\n```typescript\ninterface SentRequestCardProps {\n  connection: Connection;\n}\n```\n\nThe other user is `connection.connectedUser` (since the current user is the sender = connection.user).\n\n### 5. Create ConnectionProfileSheet.tsx\nNew component at `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/connections/ConnectionProfileSheet.tsx`\n\nA slide-out sheet (use shadcn Sheet component) that shows a connected user's privacy-filtered profile.\n\nProps:\n```typescript\ninterface ConnectionProfileSheetProps {\n  connectionId: string;\n  otherUser: { id: string; name: string; email: string; image: string | null };\n  open: boolean;\n  onOpenChange: (open: boolean) =\u003e void;\n}\n```\n\nWhen open:\n1. Fetches `api.get\u003cConnectionProfile\u003e(`/api/connections/${connectionId}/profile`)`\n2. Shows loading skeleton while fetching\n3. Displays:\n   - Avatar + name\n   - \"Connected since\" date (formatted nicely)\n   - If personality data is available (fields present), show them in glass cards:\n     - Personality Type (if present)\n     - Traits (as tag badges, if present)\n     - Strengths (as tag badges, if present)\n     - Growth Areas (as tag badges, if present)\n     - Values (as tag badges, if present)\n     - Communication Style (as text, if present)\n   - If NO personality data at all: show a subtle message \"This person keeps their profile private\" with a Lock icon\n   - The sheet should feel premium and respectful of privacy\n\nUse `useQuery` with `enabled: open` so it only fetches when opened.\n\n### 6. Update ConnectionCard.tsx\nAdd a \"View Profile\" button that opens the ConnectionProfileSheet.\nThe card should manage the sheet's open state with useState.\n\n### 7. Update Profile.tsx Privacy Settings\nAdd the 3 new toggles to the Privacy \u0026 Visibility section:\n- \"Show strengths\" with description \"Display your key strengths to connections.\"\n- \"Show values\" with description \"Display your core values to connections.\"\n- \"Show communication style\" with description \"Display how you prefer to communicate.\"\n\nThese should appear after the existing toggles, before the sign out button. Same styling as existing toggles.\n\nAlso add a subtle explanation text at the top of the privacy section:\n\"Your profile is private by default. When set to 'Connections Only', connected users will see only the information you enable below.\"\n\n### 8. Add route for ConnectionProfileSheet (if needed in App.tsx)\nThe sheet is a modal, not a page - so no route needed. But verify App.tsx doesn't need changes.\n\n## DESIGN SYSTEM\n- `glass` class for glassmorphism cards\n- `bg-primary` = warm amber/gold\n- `bg-accent` = sage teal\n- `text-foreground` / `text-muted-foreground`\n- Font: DM Sans (body), Playfair Display (display)\n- `lucide-react` icons: Shield, Lock, Users, Send, X, Check, Clock, Eye, Trash2, User\n- Use `animate-fade-in-up` with staggered delays\n- Use `formatDistanceToNow` from date-fns for \"connected since\" dates\n- Badges: Use shadcn Badge with `variant=\"secondary\"` and custom colors\n\n## API PATTERNS\n```typescript\nimport { api } from \"@/lib/api\";\n\n// GET (auto-unwraps { data: T })\nconst connections = await api.get\u003cConnectionsResponse\u003e(\"/api/connections\");\n// connections.accepted, connections.pendingReceived, connections.pendingSent\n\nconst profile = await api.get\u003cConnectionProfile\u003e(`/api/connections/${id}/profile`);\n\n// PATCH (no body)\nawait api.patch(`/api/connections/${id}/accept`);\nawait api.patch(`/api/connections/${id}/decline`);\n\n// DELETE\nawait api.delete(`/api/connections/${id}`);\n```\n\n## IMPORTANT\n- Parse comma-separated strings into arrays for display: `value.split(\",\").map(s =\u003e s.trim()).filter(Boolean)`\n- The Sheet component: `import { Sheet, SheetContent, SheetHeader, SheetTitle } from \"@/components/ui/sheet\"`\n- Verify the shadcn Sheet component exists. If not, check what dialog/drawer components are available.\n","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

---
### Vibecode Agent
*2/24/2026, 10:13:14 AM*

The privacy-first connection system is fully implemented. Here's a summary:

**Backend changes:**
- `GET /api/connections` now returns 3 categories: `accepted`, `pendingReceived`, and `pendingSent` (declined connections are excluded)
- `PATCH /api/connections/:id/decline` now deletes the record, so the sender can re-request later
- `POST /api/connections` allows re-requesting after a previous decline
- New `GET /api/connections/:id/profile` endpoint returns privacy-filtered personality data based on the other user's privacy settings
- `PATCH /api/privacy` added alongside PUT for frontend compatibility
- 3 new privacy toggles: `showStrengths`, `showValues`, `showCommunicationStyle`
- Privacy-first defaults: profile is private, most show toggles are off

**Frontend changes:**
- Connections page now has 3 tabs: Connections, Received, Sent
- New "Sent" tab shows outgoing pending requests with cancel button
- "View Profile" button on accepted connections opens a slide-out sheet with privacy-filtered data
- If a connection keeps their profile private, a "This person keeps their profile private" message with a lock icon is shown
- Privacy notice on the connections page reminds users their profile is private by default
- Profile page now has 6 granular privacy toggles with correct defaults
- Fixed the PendingRequestCard to use correct accept/decline API endpoints

**Privacy principles enforced:**
- Private by default
- No public follower counts or popularity metrics
- Users control exactly what data connections can see
- Declined requests are fully removed (emotional safety)
- Connection profiles are server-side filtered based on privacy settings

---
### System — `saving progress`

---
### User
*2/24/2026, 10:22:05 AM*

Prompt 7: Personality Intelligence Engine (Distributed Experience)

Build a DISC-based personality assessment system that supports Personality Insights’ proprietary scoring and report structure, but architect it as a distributed personality intelligence engine rather than a single report page.

Core Principles:

• The assessment structure and scoring must be modular and replaceable.
• Raw responses must be stored.
• Scoring logic must be swappable later.
• Report content must be structured as reusable insight blocks, not a static report.

Assessment Requirements:

• Mobile-friendly assessment experience
• Save progress
• Store rawResponses separately from scoring
• Placeholder DISC scoring for now
• Design scoring module so proprietary logic can replace it later without UI redesign

After scoring, generate structured personality intelligence data including:

Identity Data:
• Personality blend
• Primary type
• Graph values (Environment style, Basic style)

Strength Intelligence:
• Strength summaries
• Descriptive words

Growth Intelligence:
• Keys to excellence
• Blind spots
• Growth focus areas

Relational Intelligence:
• Team member strengths
• Motivation style
• Communication needs
• Ideal environment
• Complementary styles

Stress Intelligence:
• Under pressure behaviors
• How others may misinterpret them

Store this intelligence in structured JSON blocks categorized by type (identity, growth, relational, stress, graph).

Do NOT generate a single static report page by default.

Instead:

• Feed identity blocks to Profile tab
• Feed growth blocks to Growth tab
• Feed relational blocks to Connections system
• Feed stress blocks to AI Chat context
• Feed graph data to visual cards inside Profile

Include an optional “Full Report View” for legacy compatibility, but it should not be the primary experience.

Ensure the entire scoring and content engine is modular so proprietary logic can replace placeholder logic later without breaking UI or data structure.

{"description":"Build backend assessment \u0026 intelligence engine","id":"019c9078-da5b-7a0c-b6af-edd014dd2d57","prompt":"You need to build a comprehensive DISC-based personality assessment and intelligence engine for the backend at /home/user/workspace/backend/. The backend uses Hono + Prisma (SQLite) + Better Auth.\n\n## READ THESE FILES FIRST\n- /home/user/workspace/backend/prisma/schema.prisma\n- /home/user/workspace/backend/src/types.ts\n- /home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/profile.ts\n- /home/user/workspace/backend/src/index.ts\n\n## PART 1: Schema Changes\n\nEdit `/home/user/workspace/backend/prisma/schema.prisma` to add:\n\n```prisma\nmodel Assessment {\n  id              String   @id @default(cuid())\n  userId          String\n  user            User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  version         Int      @default(1)\n  status          String   @default(\"in_progress\") // \"in_progress\", \"completed\"\n  currentQuestion Int      @default(0)\n  rawResponses    String?  // JSON: array of { questionId, answer, timestamp }\n  scores          String?  // JSON: computed DISC scores { D, I, S, C }\n  completedAt     DateTime?\n  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt\n}\n\nmodel PersonalityIntelligence {\n  id        String   @id @default(cuid())\n  userId    String\n  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  category  String   // \"identity\", \"strength\", \"growth\", \"relational\", \"stress\", \"graph\"\n  blockKey  String   // e.g., \"personality_blend\", \"blind_spots\", \"under_pressure\"\n  title     String\n  content   String   // JSON: structured data for this block\n  version   Int      @default(1)\n  createdAt DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt\n\n  @@unique([userId, category, blockKey])\n}\n```\n\nAdd relations to User model:\n```\n  assessments             Assessment[]\n  personalityIntelligence PersonalityIntelligence[]\n```\n\nAfter editing, run: `cd /home/user/workspace/backend \u0026\u0026 bunx prisma db push`\n\n## PART 2: Types\n\nEdit `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/types.ts` to add these schemas:\n\n```typescript\n// ─── Assessment ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\nexport const AssessmentQuestionSchema = z.object({\n  id: z.string(),\n  text: z.string(),\n  options: z.array(z.object({\n    value: z.string(), // \"D\", \"I\", \"S\", \"C\"\n    text: z.string(),\n  })),\n  group: z.number(), // question group (1-based)\n});\n\nexport type AssessmentQuestion = z.infer\u003ctypeof AssessmentQuestionSchema\u003e;\n\nexport const AssessmentResponseSchema = z.object({\n  questionId: z.string(),\n  answer: z.string(), // \"D\", \"I\", \"S\", \"C\"\n  timestamp: z.string(),\n});\n\nexport type AssessmentResponse = z.infer\u003ctypeof AssessmentResponseSchema\u003e;\n\nexport const SaveProgressSchema = z.object({\n  currentQuestion: z.number(),\n  responses: z.array(AssessmentResponseSchema),\n});\n\nexport type SaveProgressInput = z.infer\u003ctypeof SaveProgressSchema\u003e;\n\nexport const AssessmentSchema = z.object({\n  id: z.string(),\n  userId: z.string(),\n  version: z.number(),\n  status: z.enum([\"in_progress\", \"completed\"]),\n  currentQuestion: z.number(),\n  rawResponses: z.string().nullable(),\n  scores: z.string().nullable(),\n  completedAt: z.string().nullable(),\n  createdAt: z.string(),\n  updatedAt: z.string(),\n});\n\nexport type Assessment = z.infer\u003ctypeof AssessmentSchema\u003e;\n\n// ─── Personality Intelligence Blocks ────────────────────────────────\nexport const IntelligenceBlockSchema = z.object({\n  id: z.string(),\n  userId: z.string(),\n  category: z.enum([\"identity\", \"strength\", \"growth\", \"relational\", \"stress\", \"graph\"]),\n  blockKey: z.string(),\n  title: z.string(),\n  content: z.string(), // JSON string\n  version: z.number(),\n  createdAt: z.string(),\n  updatedAt: z.string(),\n});\n\nexport type IntelligenceBlock = z.infer\u003ctypeof IntelligenceBlockSchema\u003e;\n```\n\n## PART 3: Assessment Questions\n\nCreate `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/assessment/questions.ts`:\n\nDefine 24 DISC assessment questions grouped in 6 groups of 4. Each question presents 4 options mapped to D, I, S, C. These are \"most like me\" style questions.\n\n```typescript\nimport type { AssessmentQuestion } from \"../types\";\n\nexport const ASSESSMENT_QUESTIONS: AssessmentQuestion[] = [\n  // Group 1: Work Approach\n  {\n    id: \"q1\",\n    text: \"When starting a new project, I tend to:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Take charge and set the direction immediately\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Rally the team and brainstorm ideas together\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Create a step-by-step plan before beginning\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Research thoroughly and analyze all the details\" },\n    ],\n    group: 1,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q2\",\n    text: \"When faced with a challenge, my first instinct is to:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Tackle it head-on with decisive action\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Talk through it with others for creative solutions\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Stay calm and think through it methodically\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Gather all the facts before deciding on an approach\" },\n    ],\n    group: 1,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q3\",\n    text: \"I feel most productive when I:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Am in control and driving toward results\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Am around others, sharing energy and ideas\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Have a steady routine and clear expectations\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Can focus deeply without interruptions\" },\n    ],\n    group: 1,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q4\",\n    text: \"In conversations, I usually:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Get straight to the point\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Share stories and connect emotionally\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Listen carefully before responding\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Ask detailed questions to understand fully\" },\n    ],\n    group: 1,\n  },\n  // Group 2: Decision Making\n  {\n    id: \"q5\",\n    text: \"When making decisions, I prioritize:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Speed and results\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"How it will affect people and relationships\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Stability and minimizing risk\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Accuracy and getting it right\" },\n    ],\n    group: 2,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q6\",\n    text: \"If my idea is challenged in a meeting, I:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Defend my position firmly with confidence\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Use humor and persuasion to win people over\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Listen to the feedback and consider adjusting\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Present data and evidence to support my case\" },\n    ],\n    group: 2,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q7\",\n    text: \"I am drawn to opportunities that:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Let me lead and overcome challenges\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Let me connect with new people\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Provide security and a clear path forward\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Let me solve complex problems\" },\n    ],\n    group: 2,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q8\",\n    text: \"My ideal role involves:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Setting goals and driving the team forward\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Inspiring and motivating others\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Supporting the team and ensuring quality\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Planning, analyzing, and ensuring precision\" },\n    ],\n    group: 2,\n  },\n  // Group 3: Relationships\n  {\n    id: \"q9\",\n    text: \"I build trust with others by:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Being competent, direct, and delivering results\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Being warm, enthusiastic, and fun to be around\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Being reliable, loyal, and always there\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Being knowledgeable, fair, and consistent\" },\n    ],\n    group: 3,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q10\",\n    text: \"In a team, I naturally:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Push the group toward action and outcomes\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Keep morale high and people connected\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Mediate disagreements and keep harmony\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Ensure quality and catch oversights\" },\n    ],\n    group: 3,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q11\",\n    text: \"When a friend is going through a hard time, I:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Offer practical solutions and next steps\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Cheer them up and remind them of the positive\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Listen patiently and offer a steady presence\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Help them think through the situation logically\" },\n    ],\n    group: 3,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q12\",\n    text: \"I feel most appreciated when people:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Respect my competence and authority\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Acknowledge my energy and contributions\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Value my loyalty and dependability\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Recognize my expertise and thoroughness\" },\n    ],\n    group: 3,\n  },\n  // Group 4: Stress \u0026 Conflict\n  {\n    id: \"q13\",\n    text: \"Under pressure, I tend to become more:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Demanding and impatient\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Scattered and overly optimistic\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Resistant to change and withdrawn\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Critical and perfectionistic\" },\n    ],\n    group: 4,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q14\",\n    text: \"When there is conflict, I usually:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Confront it directly and push for resolution\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Try to smooth things over and keep it light\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Avoid it and hope it resolves naturally\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Analyze the root cause before addressing it\" },\n    ],\n    group: 4,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q15\",\n    text: \"The thing that frustrates me most is:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Inefficiency and lack of progress\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Being ignored or left out\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Sudden changes with no warning\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Sloppy work and unclear standards\" },\n    ],\n    group: 4,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q16\",\n    text: \"I recover from setbacks by:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Channeling frustration into action\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Talking it through with people I trust\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Taking time to process and regroup\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Analyzing what went wrong to prevent recurrence\" },\n    ],\n    group: 4,\n  },\n  // Group 5: Values \u0026 Environment\n  {\n    id: \"q17\",\n    text: \"I thrive in environments that are:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Fast-paced and results-oriented\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Collaborative and socially engaging\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Calm, predictable, and supportive\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Structured, organized, and detail-focused\" },\n    ],\n    group: 5,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q18\",\n    text: \"The quality I value most in others is:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Competence and self-reliance\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Openness and positivity\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Patience and sincerity\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Integrity and attention to detail\" },\n    ],\n    group: 5,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q19\",\n    text: \"I measure my success by:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"The goals I have achieved\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"The people I have inspired\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"The trust I have earned\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"The quality of my work\" },\n    ],\n    group: 5,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q20\",\n    text: \"When I learn something new, I prefer:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Hands-on practice — just let me try it\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Group discussions and interactive sessions\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Step-by-step guides at my own pace\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Reading documentation and understanding theory first\" },\n    ],\n    group: 5,\n  },\n  // Group 6: Growth \u0026 Aspiration\n  {\n    id: \"q21\",\n    text: \"The compliment that means the most to me is:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"\\\"You really get things done\\\"\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"\\\"You make everything more fun\\\"\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"\\\"I can always count on you\\\"\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"\\\"Your work is exceptional quality\\\"\" },\n    ],\n    group: 6,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q22\",\n    text: \"My biggest growth area is likely:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Being more patient and listening more\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Following through and staying organized\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Speaking up and embracing change\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Being less critical and more flexible\" },\n    ],\n    group: 6,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q23\",\n    text: \"I want people to remember me as someone who:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Made things happen and led with courage\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Brought joy and connected people\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Was always there and deeply trustworthy\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Did excellent work with integrity\" },\n    ],\n    group: 6,\n  },\n  {\n    id: \"q24\",\n    text: \"When everything is going well, I feel:\",\n    options: [\n      { value: \"D\", text: \"Empowered and ready for the next challenge\" },\n      { value: \"I\", text: \"Excited and wanting to celebrate with others\" },\n      { value: \"S\", text: \"Content and grateful for the stability\" },\n      { value: \"C\", text: \"Satisfied knowing the process worked\" },\n    ],\n    group: 6,\n  },\n];\n\nexport const TOTAL_QUESTIONS = ASSESSMENT_QUESTIONS.length;\nexport const QUESTION_GROUPS = 6;\n```\n\n## PART 4: Scoring Engine (Modular)\n\nCreate `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/assessment/scoring.ts`:\n\nThis is the PLACEHOLDER scoring module designed to be swappable later.\n\n```typescript\nimport type { AssessmentResponse } from \"../types\";\n\nexport interface DISCScores {\n  D: number; // 0-100\n  I: number;\n  S: number;\n  C: number;\n}\n\nexport interface DISCProfile {\n  primary: string;       // e.g., \"D\" or \"DI\"\n  blend: string;         // e.g., \"Dominant-Influential\"\n  scores: DISCScores;\n  environmentStyle: DISCScores; // adapted behavior\n  basicStyle: DISCScores;      // natural behavior\n}\n\n/**\n * Placeholder DISC scoring engine.\n * Designed to be replaced with proprietary scoring logic.\n * \n * Input: raw assessment responses\n * Output: scored DISC profile with environment and basic styles\n * \n * To replace: implement a new function with the same signature.\n */\nexport function scoreDISC(responses: AssessmentResponse[]): DISCProfile {\n  const counts: DISCScores = { D: 0, I: 0, S: 0, C: 0 };\n  \n  for (const r of responses) {\n    if (r.answer in counts) {\n      counts[r.answer as keyof DISCScores]++;\n    }\n  }\n  \n  const total = responses.length || 1;\n  const scores: DISCScores = {\n    D: Math.round((counts.D / total) * 100),\n    I: Math.round((counts.I / total) * 100),\n    S: Math.round((counts.S / total) * 100),\n    C: Math.round((counts.C / total) * 100),\n  };\n  \n  // Determine primary and secondary\n  const sorted = Object.entries(scores).sort((a, b) =\u003e b[1] - a[1]);\n  const primaryLetter = sorted[0][0];\n  const secondaryLetter = sorted[1][0];\n  \n  // Generate blend name\n  const nameMap: Record\u003cstring, string\u003e = {\n    D: \"Dominant\",\n    I: \"Influential\",\n    S: \"Steady\",\n    C: \"Conscientious\",\n  };\n  \n  const primary = sorted[1][1] \u003e 20 ? `${primaryLetter}${secondaryLetter}` : primaryLetter;\n  const blend = sorted[1][1] \u003e 20 \n    ? `${nameMap[primaryLetter]}-${nameMap[secondaryLetter]}`\n    : nameMap[primaryLetter];\n  \n  // Placeholder: environment style = slightly adapted, basic style = raw scores\n  // In proprietary version, these would use different question subsets\n  const environmentStyle: DISCScores = {\n    D: Math.min(100, scores.D + 5),\n    I: Math.min(100, scores.I + 3),\n    S: Math.max(0, scores.S - 3),\n    C: Math.max(0, scores.C - 2),\n  };\n  \n  return {\n    primary,\n    blend,\n    scores,\n    environmentStyle,\n    basicStyle: { ...scores },\n  };\n}\n```\n\n## PART 5: Intelligence Generator (Modular)\n\nCreate `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/assessment/intelligence.ts`:\n\nThis generates the structured intelligence blocks based on DISC scores. Designed to be replaced with proprietary logic.\n\n```typescript\nimport type { DISCProfile, DISCScores } from \"./scoring\";\n\nexport interface IntelligenceBlocks {\n  identity: IntelligenceBlock[];\n  strength: IntelligenceBlock[];\n  growth: IntelligenceBlock[];\n  relational: IntelligenceBlock[];\n  stress: IntelligenceBlock[];\n  graph: IntelligenceBlock[];\n}\n\ninterface IntelligenceBlock {\n  blockKey: string;\n  title: string;\n  content: object; // will be JSON.stringify'd\n}\n\nconst DISC_DATA: Record\u003cstring, {\n  name: string;\n  descriptors: string[];\n  strengths: string[];\n  blindSpots: string[];\n  growthKeys: string[];\n  teamStrengths: string[];\n  motivationStyle: string;\n  communicationNeeds: string[];\n  idealEnvironment: string[];\n  complementaryStyles: string[];\n  underPressure: string[];\n  misinterpretations: string[];\n}\u003e = {\n  D: {\n    name: \"Dominant\",\n    descriptors: [\"Decisive\", \"Direct\", \"Driven\", \"Bold\", \"Competitive\", \"Independent\"],\n    strengths: [\"Natural leader who drives results\", \"Thrives under pressure and challenge\", \"Makes quick, confident decisions\", \"Cuts through complexity to find solutions\"],\n    blindSpots: [\"May overlook people's feelings in pursuit of goals\", \"Can come across as impatient or blunt\", \"May struggle to delegate when quality matters\", \"Risk of burnout from constant drive\"],\n    growthKeys: [\"Practice active listening without rushing to solutions\", \"Ask for input before making final decisions\", \"Celebrate small wins, not just the end goal\", \"Develop patience with different work styles\"],\n    teamStrengths: [\"Initiates action when others hesitate\", \"Keeps the team focused on results\", \"Navigates obstacles with confidence\", \"Creates momentum and urgency\"],\n    motivationStyle: \"Motivated by challenges, autonomy, and tangible results. Needs freedom to act and authority to decide.\",\n    communicationNeeds: [\"Be direct and concise\", \"Focus on outcomes, not process\", \"Respect their time\", \"Bring solutions, not just problems\"],\n    idealEnvironment: [\"Fast-paced with clear goals\", \"Autonomy over decisions\", \"Results-based recognition\", \"Minimal bureaucracy\"],\n    complementaryStyles: [\"S types provide patience and follow-through\", \"C types add analytical depth and quality control\"],\n    underPressure: [\"Becomes more demanding and controlling\", \"May dismiss input from others\", \"Pushes harder at the expense of relationships\", \"Communicates more bluntly than intended\"],\n    misinterpretations: [\"Directness may be seen as aggression\", \"Urgency may be mistaken for impatience with people\", \"Focus on results may seem uncaring\"],\n  },\n  I: {\n    name: \"Influential\",\n    descriptors: [\"Enthusiastic\", \"Optimistic\", \"Collaborative\", \"Creative\", \"Persuasive\", \"Expressive\"],\n    strengths: [\"Inspires and energizes those around them\", \"Builds relationships quickly and naturally\", \"Brings creative ideas and fresh perspectives\", \"Creates a positive and motivating atmosphere\"],\n    blindSpots: [\"May overcommit and under-deliver\", \"Can lose focus on details and follow-through\", \"May avoid difficult conversations\", \"Optimism can sometimes override realistic assessment\"],\n    growthKeys: [\"Build systems for follow-through and accountability\", \"Practice saying no to protect commitments\", \"Balance enthusiasm with practical planning\", \"Develop comfort with constructive conflict\"],\n    teamStrengths: [\"Keeps morale high in tough times\", \"Connects people across groups and silos\", \"Generates innovative ideas and possibilities\", \"Persuades and rallies support for initiatives\"],\n    motivationStyle: \"Motivated by social recognition, collaboration, and new experiences. Needs variety and positive feedback.\",\n    communicationNeeds: [\"Allow time for discussion and stories\", \"Be warm and personal\", \"Acknowledge their contributions publicly\", \"Keep things positive and forward-looking\"],\n    idealEnvironment: [\"Collaborative and social\", \"Flexible with room for creativity\", \"Recognition and visibility\", \"Varied tasks and new projects\"],\n    complementaryStyles: [\"C types help with follow-through and quality\", \"S types provide stability and grounding\"],\n    underPressure: [\"Becomes disorganized and overly talkative\", \"May make impulsive decisions\", \"Avoids negative feedback or bad news\", \"Over-promises to maintain likability\"],\n    misinterpretations: [\"Enthusiasm may be seen as superficial\", \"Socializing may be mistaken for lack of focus\", \"Positivity may seem dismissive of real problems\"],\n  },\n  S: {\n    name: \"Steady\",\n    descriptors: [\"Patient\", \"Reliable\", \"Calm\", \"Supportive\", \"Thoughtful\", \"Loyal\"],\n    strengths: [\"Creates stability and trust in any team\", \"Listens deeply and considers all perspectives\", \"Follows through consistently and reliably\", \"Builds lasting, meaningful relationships\"],\n    blindSpots: [\"May resist change even when it's needed\", \"Can avoid conflict to the point of suppression\", \"May not speak up with important opinions\", \"Tendency to put others' needs before their own\"],\n    growthKeys: [\"Practice voicing your opinions earlier in discussions\", \"Embrace small changes to build adaptability\", \"Set boundaries to protect your own energy\", \"Develop comfort with healthy disagreement\"],\n    teamStrengths: [\"Anchors the team during uncertainty\", \"Remembers and honors commitments\", \"Mediates disagreements with empathy\", \"Creates a safe space for others to open up\"],\n    motivationStyle: \"Motivated by harmony, appreciation, and stable environments. Needs predictability and sincere recognition.\",\n    communicationNeeds: [\"Be patient and give them time to process\", \"Avoid sudden surprises or demands\", \"Show genuine appreciation for their work\", \"Provide clear expectations and timelines\"],\n    idealEnvironment: [\"Stable and predictable\", \"Collaborative with clear roles\", \"Supportive leadership\", \"Time to process before decisions\"],\n    complementaryStyles: [\"D types bring decisiveness and push for action\", \"I types add energy and variety\"],\n    underPressure: [\"Becomes passive and withdraws\", \"Agrees externally but resists internally\", \"May procrastinate on difficult decisions\", \"Over-accommodates others at personal cost\"],\n    misinterpretations: [\"Patience may be seen as lack of urgency\", \"Quietness may be mistaken for disengagement\", \"Agreeableness may seem like lack of opinion\"],\n  },\n  C: {\n    name: \"Conscientious\",\n    descriptors: [\"Analytical\", \"Precise\", \"Systematic\", \"Thoughtful\", \"Quality-driven\", \"Independent\"],\n    strengths: [\"Produces work of exceptional quality\", \"Thinks critically and spots risks early\", \"Creates systems and processes that last\", \"Maintains high standards and consistency\"],\n    blindSpots: [\"May over-analyze and delay decisions\", \"Can be overly critical of self and others\", \"May struggle with ambiguity or vague direction\", \"Perfectionism can slow down progress\"],\n    growthKeys: [\"Practice making decisions with imperfect information\", \"Share your reasoning process with others\", \"Balance quality with progress and momentum\", \"Develop tolerance for different standards\"],\n    teamStrengths: [\"Ensures quality and catches errors\", \"Provides thorough research and analysis\", \"Creates reliable systems and documentation\", \"Asks the tough questions others avoid\"],\n    motivationStyle: \"Motivated by mastery, accuracy, and logical systems. Needs clear standards, time to analyze, and respect for expertise.\",\n    communicationNeeds: [\"Provide data and logic to support your point\", \"Give them time to process information\", \"Avoid emotional pressure or vague requests\", \"Respect their expertise and thoroughness\"],\n    idealEnvironment: [\"Quiet and focused\", \"Clear standards and expectations\", \"Time for thorough analysis\", \"Structured processes\"],\n    complementaryStyles: [\"I types bring social energy and optimism\", \"D types push for action and speed\"],\n    underPressure: [\"Becomes overly critical and rigid\", \"Withdraws into analysis paralysis\", \"Sets impossibly high standards\", \"May come across as cold or detached\"],\n    misinterpretations: [\"Thoroughness may be seen as slowness\", \"Questions may seem like criticism\", \"Preference for solitude may seem antisocial\"],\n  },\n};\n\n/**\n * Placeholder intelligence generator.\n * Designed to be replaced with proprietary content engine.\n * \n * Input: DISC profile from scoring\n * Output: categorized intelligence blocks\n * \n * To replace: implement a new function with the same signature.\n */\nexport function generateIntelligence(profile: DISCProfile): IntelligenceBlocks {\n  const primaryLetter = profile.primary[0] as keyof typeof DISC_DATA;\n  const secondaryLetter = profile.primary.length \u003e 1 ? profile.primary[1] as keyof typeof DISC_DATA : null;\n  const data = DISC_DATA[primaryLetter];\n  const secondaryData = secondaryLetter ? DISC_DATA[secondaryLetter] : null;\n\n  const identity: IntelligenceBlock[] = [\n    {\n      blockKey: \"personality_blend\",\n      title: \"Your Personality Blend\",\n      content: {\n        primary: primaryLetter,\n        primaryName: data.name,\n        secondary: secondaryLetter,\n        secondaryName: secondaryData?.name ?? null,\n        blend: profile.blend,\n        description: secondaryData\n          ? `You lead with ${data.name} energy — ${data.descriptors.slice(0, 3).join(\", \").toLowerCase()} — blended with ${secondaryData.name} qualities like being ${secondaryData.descriptors.slice(0, 2).join(\" and \").toLowerCase()}.`\n          : `You are primarily ${data.name} — ${data.descriptors.slice(0, 4).join(\", \").toLowerCase()}.`,\n      },\n    },\n    {\n      blockKey: \"primary_type\",\n      title: \"Primary Type\",\n      content: {\n        type: primaryLetter,\n        name: data.name,\n        descriptors: data.descriptors,\n      },\n    },\n    {\n      blockKey: \"graph_values\",\n      title: \"DISC Graph\",\n      content: {\n        environmentStyle: profile.environmentStyle,\n        basicStyle: profile.basicStyle,\n        scores: profile.scores,\n      },\n    },\n  ];\n\n  const strength: IntelligenceBlock[] = [\n    {\n      blockKey: \"strength_summary\",\n      title: \"Your Key Strengths\",\n      content: {\n        strengths: [\n          ...data.strengths,\n          ...(secondaryData ? [secondaryData.strengths[0]] : []),\n        ],\n      },\n    },\n    {\n      blockKey: \"descriptive_words\",\n      title: \"Words That Describe You\",\n      content: {\n        words: [\n          ...data.descriptors,\n          ...(secondaryData ? secondaryData.descriptors.slice(0, 2) : []),\n        ],\n      },\n    },\n  ];\n\n  const growth: IntelligenceBlock[] = [\n    {\n      blockKey: \"keys_to_excellence\",\n      title: \"Keys to Excellence\",\n      content: {\n        keys: data.growthKeys,\n      },\n    },\n    {\n      blockKey: \"blind_spots\",\n      title: \"Blind Spots to Watch\",\n      content: {\n        blindSpots: data.blindSpots,\n      },\n    },\n    {\n      blockKey: \"growth_focus\",\n      title: \"Growth Focus Areas\",\n      content: {\n        areas: data.growthKeys.map((key, i) =\u003e ({\n          title: key.split(\" \").slice(0, 4).join(\" \"),\n          description: key,\n          priority: i + 1,\n        })),\n      },\n    },\n  ];\n\n  const relational: IntelligenceBlock[] = [\n    {\n      blockKey: \"team_strengths\",\n      title: \"What You Bring to a Team\",\n      content: { strengths: data.teamStrengths },\n    },\n    {\n      blockKey: \"motivation_style\",\n      title: \"What Motivates You\",\n      content: { description: data.motivationStyle },\n    },\n    {\n      blockKey: \"communication_needs\",\n      title: \"How to Communicate With You\",\n      content: { needs: data.communicationNeeds },\n    },\n    {\n      blockKey: \"ideal_environment\",\n      title: \"Your Ideal Environment\",\n      content: { traits: data.idealEnvironment },\n    },\n    {\n      blockKey: \"complementary_styles\",\n      title: \"Complementary Styles\",\n      content: { styles: data.complementaryStyles },\n    },\n  ];\n\n  const stress: IntelligenceBlock[] = [\n    {\n      blockKey: \"under_pressure\",\n      title: \"Under Pressure\",\n      content: { behaviors: data.underPressure },\n    },\n    {\n      blockKey: \"misinterpretations\",\n      title: \"How Others May Misread You\",\n      content: { misinterpretations: data.misinterpretations },\n    },\n  ];\n\n  const graph: IntelligenceBlock[] = [\n    {\n      blockKey: \"disc_scores\",\n      title: \"Your DISC Scores\",\n      content: {\n        scores: profile.scores,\n        environmentStyle: profile.environmentStyle,\n        basicStyle: profile.basicStyle,\n      },\n    },\n  ];\n\n  return { identity, strength, growth, relational, stress, graph };\n}\n```\n\n## PART 6: Assessment Routes\n\nCreate `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/assessment.ts`:\n\n```typescript\nimport { Hono } from \"hono\";\nimport { zValidator } from \"@hono/zod-validator\";\nimport { prisma } from \"../prisma\";\nimport { SaveProgressSchema } from \"../types\";\nimport type { AssessmentResponse } from \"../types\";\nimport type { User, Session } from \"../auth\";\nimport { ASSESSMENT_QUESTIONS, TOTAL_QUESTIONS } from \"../assessment/questions\";\nimport { scoreDISC } from \"../assessment/scoring\";\nimport { generateIntelligence } from \"../assessment/intelligence\";\n\ntype Variables = {\n  user: User | null;\n  session: Session | null;\n};\n\nconst assessmentRouter = new Hono\u003c{ Variables: Variables }\u003e();\n\n// GET /api/assessment/questions - Get all assessment questions\nassessmentRouter.get(\"/questions\", async (c) =\u003e {\n  const user = c.get(\"user\");\n  if (!user) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n  }\n  return c.json({ data: { questions: ASSESSMENT_QUESTIONS, total: TOTAL_QUESTIONS } });\n});\n\n// GET /api/assessment/current - Get current assessment progress (or null)\nassessmentRouter.get(\"/current\", async (c) =\u003e {\n  const user = c.get(\"user\");\n  if (!user) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n  }\n\n  const assessment = await prisma.assessment.findFirst({\n    where: { userId: user.id, status: \"in_progress\" },\n    orderBy: { createdAt: \"desc\" },\n  });\n\n  return c.json({ data: assessment ?? null });\n});\n\n// POST /api/assessment/start - Start a new assessment (or resume existing)\nassessmentRouter.post(\"/start\", async (c) =\u003e {\n  const user = c.get(\"user\");\n  if (!user) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n  }\n\n  // Check for existing in-progress assessment\n  const existing = await prisma.assessment.findFirst({\n    where: { userId: user.id, status: \"in_progress\" },\n    orderBy: { createdAt: \"desc\" },\n  });\n\n  if (existing) {\n    return c.json({ data: existing });\n  }\n\n  const assessment = await prisma.assessment.create({\n    data: {\n      userId: user.id,\n      version: 1,\n      status: \"in_progress\",\n      currentQuestion: 0,\n      rawResponses: \"[]\",\n    },\n  });\n\n  return c.json({ data: assessment });\n});\n\n// PUT /api/assessment/:id/progress - Save assessment progress\nassessmentRouter.put(\n  \"/:id/progress\",\n  zValidator(\"json\", SaveProgressSchema),\n  async (c) =\u003e {\n    const user = c.get(\"user\");\n    if (!user) {\n      return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n    }\n\n    const assessmentId = c.req.param(\"id\");\n    const { currentQuestion, responses } = c.req.valid(\"json\");\n\n    const assessment = await prisma.assessment.findFirst({\n      where: { id: assessmentId, userId: user.id, status: \"in_progress\" },\n    });\n\n    if (!assessment) {\n      return c.json({ error: { message: \"Assessment not found\", code: \"NOT_FOUND\" } }, 404);\n    }\n\n    const updated = await prisma.assessment.update({\n      where: { id: assessmentId },\n      data: {\n        currentQuestion,\n        rawResponses: JSON.stringify(responses),\n      },\n    });\n\n    return c.json({ data: updated });\n  }\n);\n\n// POST /api/assessment/:id/complete - Complete assessment and generate intelligence\nassessmentRouter.post(\"/:id/complete\", async (c) =\u003e {\n  const user = c.get(\"user\");\n  if (!user) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n  }\n\n  const assessmentId = c.req.param(\"id\");\n\n  const assessment = await prisma.assessment.findFirst({\n    where: { id: assessmentId, userId: user.id, status: \"in_progress\" },\n  });\n\n  if (!assessment) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Assessment not found\", code: \"NOT_FOUND\" } }, 404);\n  }\n\n  const rawResponses: AssessmentResponse[] = JSON.parse(assessment.rawResponses ?? \"[]\");\n  \n  if (rawResponses.length \u003c TOTAL_QUESTIONS) {\n    return c.json(\n      { error: { message: `Assessment incomplete. Expected ${TOTAL_QUESTIONS} responses, got ${rawResponses.length}.`, code: \"INCOMPLETE\" } },\n      400\n    );\n  }\n\n  // 1. Score the assessment\n  const discProfile = scoreDISC(rawResponses);\n\n  // 2. Generate intelligence blocks\n  const intelligence = generateIntelligence(discProfile);\n\n  // 3. Store scores on the assessment\n  const completedAssessment = await prisma.assessment.update({\n    where: { id: assessmentId },\n    data: {\n      status: \"completed\",\n      scores: JSON.stringify(discProfile.scores),\n      completedAt: new Date(),\n    },\n  });\n\n  // 4. Upsert all intelligence blocks\n  const allBlocks = [\n    ...intelligence.identity.map((b) =\u003e ({ ...b, category: \"identity\" as const })),\n    ...intelligence.strength.map((b) =\u003e ({ ...b, category: \"strength\" as const })),\n    ...intelligence.growth.map((b) =\u003e ({ ...b, category: \"growth\" as const })),\n    ...intelligence.relational.map((b) =\u003e ({ ...b, category: \"relational\" as const })),\n    ...intelligence.stress.map((b) =\u003e ({ ...b, category: \"stress\" as const })),\n    ...intelligence.graph.map((b) =\u003e ({ ...b, category: \"graph\" as const })),\n  ];\n\n  for (const block of allBlocks) {\n    await prisma.personalityIntelligence.upsert({\n      where: {\n        userId_category_blockKey: {\n          userId: user.id,\n          category: block.category,\n          blockKey: block.blockKey,\n        },\n      },\n      create: {\n        userId: user.id,\n        category: block.category,\n        blockKey: block.blockKey,\n        title: block.title,\n        content: JSON.stringify(block.content),\n        version: 1,\n      },\n      update: {\n        title: block.title,\n        content: JSON.stringify(block.content),\n        version: { increment: 1 },\n      },\n    });\n  }\n\n  // 5. Update the PersonalityProfile with key data from intelligence\n  const identityBlend = intelligence.identity.find((b) =\u003e b.blockKey === \"personality_blend\");\n  const strengthSummary = intelligence.strength.find((b) =\u003e b.blockKey === \"strength_summary\");\n  const descriptiveWords = intelligence.strength.find((b) =\u003e b.blockKey === \"descriptive_words\");\n  const growthFocus = intelligence.growth.find((b) =\u003e b.blockKey === \"growth_focus\");\n  const blindSpots = intelligence.growth.find((b) =\u003e b.blockKey === \"blind_spots\");\n  const commNeeds = intelligence.relational.find((b) =\u003e b.blockKey === \"communication_needs\");\n  const underPressure = intelligence.stress.find((b) =\u003e b.blockKey === \"under_pressure\");\n  const misinterpretations = intelligence.stress.find((b) =\u003e b.blockKey === \"misinterpretations\");\n\n  const blendContent = identityBlend?.content as { blend: string; primary: string; primaryName: string } | undefined;\n  const strengthContent = strengthSummary?.content as { strengths: string[] } | undefined;\n  const wordsContent = descriptiveWords?.content as { words: string[] } | undefined;\n  const growthContent = growthFocus?.content as { areas: { title: string; description: string }[] } | undefined;\n  const blindContent = blindSpots?.content as { blindSpots: string[] } | undefined;\n  const commContent = commNeeds?.content as { needs: string[] } | undefined;\n  const pressureContent = underPressure?.content as { behaviors: string[] } | undefined;\n  const misContent = misinterpretations?.content as { misinterpretations: string[] } | undefined;\n\n  await prisma.personalityProfile.upsert({\n    where: { userId: user.id },\n    create: {\n      userId: user.id,\n      personalityType: blendContent?.blend ?? discProfile.blend,\n      traits: wordsContent?.words.join(\", \") ?? null,\n      strengths: strengthContent?.strengths.join(\", \") ?? null,\n      growthAreas: growthContent?.areas.map((a) =\u003e a.description).join(\", \") ?? null,\n      values: null,\n      communicationStyle: commContent?.needs.join(\", \") ?? null,\n      behavioralTendencies: blindContent?.blindSpots.join(\", \") ?? null,\n      stressTendencies: pressureContent?.behaviors.join(\", \") ?? null,\n      communicationPreferences: commContent?.needs.join(\", \") ?? null,\n      assessmentCompleted: true,\n      assessmentVersion: 1,\n    },\n    update: {\n      personalityType: blendContent?.blend ?? discProfile.blend,\n      traits: wordsContent?.words.join(\", \") ?? null,\n      strengths: strengthContent?.strengths.join(\", \") ?? null,\n      growthAreas: growthContent?.areas.map((a) =\u003e a.description).join(\", \") ?? null,\n      communicationStyle: commContent?.needs.join(\", \") ?? null,\n      behavioralTendencies: blindContent?.blindSpots.join(\", \") ?? null,\n      stressTendencies: pressureContent?.behaviors.join(\", \") ?? null,\n      communicationPreferences: commContent?.needs.join(\", \") ?? null,\n      assessmentCompleted: true,\n      assessmentVersion: 1,\n    },\n  });\n\n  // 6. Sync to User model\n  await prisma.user.update({\n    where: { id: user.id },\n    data: {\n      personalityType: blendContent?.blend ?? discProfile.blend,\n      personalityScores: JSON.stringify(discProfile.scores),\n      communicationStyle: commContent?.needs[0] ?? null,\n      growthFocus: growthContent?.areas.map((a) =\u003e a.title).join(\", \") ?? null,\n    },\n  });\n\n  return c.json({ data: { assessment: completedAssessment, scores: discProfile } });\n});\n\n// GET /api/assessment/intelligence - Get all intelligence blocks for current user\nassessmentRouter.get(\"/intelligence\", async (c) =\u003e {\n  const user = c.get(\"user\");\n  if (!user) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n  }\n\n  const blocks = await prisma.personalityIntelligence.findMany({\n    where: { userId: user.id },\n    orderBy: [{ category: \"asc\" }, { blockKey: \"asc\" }],\n  });\n\n  // Group by category\n  const grouped: Record\u003cstring, typeof blocks\u003e = {};\n  for (const block of blocks) {\n    if (!grouped[block.category]) grouped[block.category] = [];\n    grouped[block.category].push(block);\n  }\n\n  return c.json({ data: grouped });\n});\n\n// GET /api/assessment/intelligence/:category - Get intelligence blocks by category\nassessmentRouter.get(\"/intelligence/:category\", async (c) =\u003e {\n  const user = c.get(\"user\");\n  if (!user) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n  }\n\n  const category = c.req.param(\"category\");\n  const validCategories = [\"identity\", \"strength\", \"growth\", \"relational\", \"stress\", \"graph\"];\n  \n  if (!validCategories.includes(category)) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Invalid category\", code: \"INVALID_CATEGORY\" } }, 400);\n  }\n\n  const blocks = await prisma.personalityIntelligence.findMany({\n    where: { userId: user.id, category },\n    orderBy: { blockKey: \"asc\" },\n  });\n\n  return c.json({ data: blocks });\n});\n\nexport { assessmentRouter };\n```\n\n## PART 7: Register Routes\n\nEdit `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/index.ts` to add:\n\n```typescript\nimport { assessmentRouter } from \"./routes/assessment\";\n```\n\nAnd add the route:\n```typescript\napp.route(\"/api/assessment\", assessmentRouter);\n```\n\n## PART 8: Update Chat Context\n\nEdit `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/chat.ts` to also include stress intelligence in the system prompt. Find where the personality profile is used in the system prompt and add:\n\nAfter fetching the personality profile, also fetch stress intelligence blocks:\n```typescript\nconst stressBlocks = await prisma.personalityIntelligence.findMany({\n  where: { userId: user.id, category: \"stress\" },\n});\n```\n\nAnd include them in the system prompt if they exist:\n```typescript\nif (stressBlocks.length \u003e 0) {\n  systemPrompt += \"\\n\\nSTRESS INTELLIGENCE:\\n\";\n  for (const block of stressBlocks) {\n    const content = JSON.parse(block.content);\n    systemPrompt += `${block.title}: ${JSON.stringify(content)}\\n`;\n  }\n}\n```\n\nThis should be added near where the existing profile data is injected into the system prompt.\n\n## IMPORTANT PATTERNS\n- All routes return `{ data: ... }` envelope\n- Errors: `{ error: { message, code } }` with appropriate status codes\n- Auth check: `const user = c.get(\"user\"); if (!user) return 401`\n- Import Prisma from `../prisma`, types from `../types`, auth from `../auth`\n- Use `zValidator` for input validation where needed\n- DO NOT break existing routes or types\n","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:backend-developer"}

bash → cd /home/user/workspace/backend && bun -e "
import { prisma } from './src/prisma';
import crypto from 'crypto';

const userId = 'htzXKoFt61sgnIjA6spQX7iDQvzLAL59';
const token = crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex');
const id = crypto.randomBytes(16).toString('hex');

// Create a new session directly
const session = await prisma.session.create({
  data: {
    id,
    token,
    userId,
    expiresAt: new Date(Date.now() + 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000), // 7 days
  }
});
console.log('Created session token:', token);
console.log('Session id:', id);
" 2>&1 – Create fresh test session token

import { auth } from './src/auth';

// Sign in to get a proper session with headers
const req = new Request('http://localhost:3000/api/auth/sign-in/email', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
  body: JSON.stringify({ email: 'disc-test@example.com', password: 'TestPassword123!' }),
});

const cookies = resp.headers.getSetCookie();
console.log('Response status:', resp.status);
console.log('Cookies:', cookies);
const body = await resp.json();
console.log('Body:', JSON.stringify(body, null, 2));
" 2>&1 – Sign in via Better Auth handler to get session cookie

### System — `Executing tool: Bash`
*2/24/2026, 10:34:22 AM*

bash → AUTH="-b __Secure-better-auth.session_token=6l0c6l20G65CAD6sQI9QpVyCkRB8LEf8.z8jxZlIXEjQewIGabwxmSPe%2FBHeAmdbBQD1nvGn9J9g%3D"
ASSESSMENT_ID="cmm0tscvc0001m2q0azde9rwl"

# Build 24 responses with a mix: heavy D, some I
RESPONSES='['
for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  if [ $i -gt 1 ]; then RESPONSES+=','; fi
  # Mix: q1-8 D, q9-14 I, q15-18 S, q19-24 D
  if [ $i -le 8 ]; then A="D";
  elif [ $i -le 14 ]; then A="I";
  elif [ $i -le 18 ]; then A="S";
  else A="D"; fi
  RESPONSES+="{\"questionId\":\"q${i}\",\"answer\":\"${A}\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-02-24T16:00:00.000Z\"}"
done
RESPONSES+=']'

echo "=== PUT progress (save 24 responses) ==="
curl -s $AUTH -X PUT "$BACKEND_URL/api/assessment/$ASSESSMENT_ID/progress" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d "{\"currentQuestion\":24,\"responses\":$RESPONSES}" 2>&1 | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('Status:', d.get('data',{}).get('status'), 'Q:', d.get('data',{}).get('currentQuestion'))" 2>&1 – Save assessment progress with 24 responses

# Build 24 responses with a mix: heavy D, some I

  if [ $i -le 8 ]; then A="D";

### System — `Executing tool: Bash`

# Build JSON responses in a temp file

import json
responses = []
for i in range(1, 25):
    if i <= 8: a = 'D'
    elif i <= 14: a = 'I'
    elif i <= 18: a = 'S'
    else: a = 'D'

payload = {'currentQuestion': 24, 'responses': responses}
print(json.dumps(payload))
" > /tmp/progress.json

curl -s -b "$AUTH" -X PUT "$BACKEND_URL/api/assessment/$ASSESSMENT_ID/progress" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @/tmp/progress.json 2>&1 – Save assessment progress using file payload

The app uses React, TailwindCSS, shadcn/ui, Tanstack React Query, and lucide-react icons.\n\n## READ THESE FILES FIRST\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/App.tsx (router)\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/types/index.ts (types)\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/lib/api.ts (API client)\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/profile/EmptyProfile.tsx (current empty state)\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/home/PersonalitySnapshot.tsx (current empty state link)\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/growth/GrowthRecommendations.tsx (current empty state link)\n\n## BACKEND API ENDPOINTS\n```\nGET  /api/assessment/questions     → { data: { questions: Question[], total: number } }\nGET  /api/assessment/current       → { data: Assessment | null }\nPOST /api/assessment/start         → { data: Assessment }\nPUT  /api/assessment/:id/progress  → { data: Assessment }\nPOST /api/assessment/:id/complete  → { data: { assessment: Assessment, scores: DISCProfile } }\nGET  /api/assessment/intelligence  → { data: Record\u003cstring, IntelligenceBlock[]\u003e }\n```\n\nAssessment question shape:\n```typescript\ninterface AssessmentQuestion {\n  id: string;\n  text: string;\n  options: { value: string; text: string }[];\n  group: number; // 1-6\n}\n```\n\nAssessment shape:\n```typescript\ninterface Assessment {\n  id: string;\n  userId: string;\n  version: number;\n  status: \"in_progress\" | \"completed\";\n  currentQuestion: number;\n  rawResponses: string | null; // JSON array of { questionId, answer, timestamp }\n  scores: string | null; // JSON: { D, I, S, C }\n  completedAt: string | null;\n  createdAt: string;\n  updatedAt: string;\n}\n```\n\n## CHANGES NEEDED\n\n### 1. Add types to types/index.ts\n\nAdd to `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/types/index.ts`:\n\n```typescript\nexport interface AssessmentQuestion {\n  id: string;\n  text: string;\n  options: { value: string; text: string }[];\n  group: number;\n}\n\nexport interface AssessmentResponse {\n  questionId: string;\n  answer: string;\n  timestamp: string;\n}\n\nexport interface Assessment {\n  id: string;\n  userId: string;\n  version: number;\n  status: \"in_progress\" | \"completed\";\n  currentQuestion: number;\n  rawResponses: string | null;\n  scores: string | null;\n  completedAt: string | null;\n  createdAt: string;\n  updatedAt: string;\n}\n\nexport interface DISCScores {\n  D: number;\n  I: number;\n  S: number;\n  C: number;\n}\n\nexport interface DISCProfile {\n  primary: string;\n  blend: string;\n  scores: DISCScores;\n  environmentStyle: DISCScores;\n  basicStyle: DISCScores;\n}\n\nexport interface IntelligenceBlock {\n  id: string;\n  userId: string;\n  category: \"identity\" | \"strength\" | \"growth\" | \"relational\" | \"stress\" | \"graph\";\n  blockKey: string;\n  title: string;\n  content: string; // JSON string\n  version: number;\n  createdAt: string;\n  updatedAt: string;\n}\n\nexport type IntelligenceByCategory = Record\u003cstring, IntelligenceBlock[]\u003e;\n```\n\n### 2. Create Assessment Page\n\nCreate `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Assessment.tsx`:\n\nThis is a full-screen assessment experience. It should:\n1. Fetch questions from `/api/assessment/questions`\n2. Check for existing progress from `/api/assessment/current`\n3. Start/resume assessment via `/api/assessment/start`\n4. Show one question at a time with 4 option cards\n5. Save progress every time user answers (call PUT /api/assessment/:id/progress)\n6. Show a progress bar at the top (current question / total)\n7. Show group labels (\"Work Approach\", \"Decision Making\", etc.)\n8. Allow going back to previous questions\n9. On the last question, show a \"Complete Assessment\" button\n10. Call POST /api/assessment/:id/complete to finish\n11. Show a beautiful results reveal animation with their DISC type\n12. Navigate to /profile after showing results\n\nDesign requirements:\n- Full-screen layout (no bottom nav) — use a fixed top bar with progress\n- Each question has a fade-in animation\n- Options are tall, full-width cards that highlight on selection\n- Selected option gets a primary border/glow\n- Transition smoothly between questions\n- Mobile-first: large touch targets, comfortable spacing\n- Progress bar: thin, amber/gold colored, at the very top\n- Back button (ChevronLeft) in top-left\n- Question counter in top-right (\"4 of 24\")\n- Group label above question text (\"Group 1: Work Approach\")\n- Results page: large DISC type display, animated score bars, \"View Your Profile\" button\n\nGroup labels:\n```\n1: \"Work Approach\"\n2: \"Decision Making\"\n3: \"Relationships\"\n4: \"Stress \u0026 Conflict\"\n5: \"Values \u0026 Environment\"\n6: \"Growth \u0026 Aspiration\"\n```\n\nThe completion/results view should show:\n- Big personality blend name (e.g., \"Dominant-Influential\")\n- Primary letter large with accent color\n- 4 DISC bars animated from 0 to their value\n- Brief description text\n- \"Explore Your Profile\" button → navigates to /profile\n\n### 3. Add Route in App.tsx\n\nAdd the assessment route to the protected routes in App.tsx:\n```tsx\n\u003cRoute path=\"/assessment\" element={\u003cAssessment /\u003e} /\u003e\n```\n\nThis should be INSIDE the ProtectedRoute wrapper but OUTSIDE the AppLayout (no bottom nav during assessment). Look at the current App.tsx structure carefully to add it correctly.\n\n### 4. Update EmptyProfile.tsx\n\nChange the \"Start Assessment\" button to link to `/assessment` instead of `/chat`:\n```tsx\n\u003cLink to=\"/assessment\"\u003e\n```\n\n### 5. Update PersonalitySnapshot.tsx\n\nChange the \"Discover Your Type\" link to `/assessment` instead of `/chat`:\n```tsx\n\u003cLink to=\"/assessment\" className=\"block group\"\u003e\n```\n\n### 6. Update GrowthRecommendations.tsx\n\nChange the \"Discover Your Growth Areas\" button to navigate to `/assessment` instead of `/chat`:\n```tsx\nonClick={() =\u003e navigate(\"/assessment\")}\n```\n\n## DESIGN SYSTEM\n- `glass` class for glassmorphism cards\n- `bg-primary` = warm amber/gold (38 92% 55%)\n- `bg-accent` = sage teal (165 35% 45%)\n- `text-foreground` / `text-muted-foreground`\n- Font: DM Sans (body), Playfair Display (display/font-display)\n- `animate-fade-in-up` with staggered delays\n- Use `lucide-react` icons: ChevronLeft, ChevronRight, Sparkles, CheckCircle2, ArrowRight\n- No emojis\n\n## API PATTERNS\n```typescript\nimport { api } from \"@/lib/api\";\n\n// GET (auto-unwraps { data: T })\nconst { questions, total } = await api.get\u003c{ questions: AssessmentQuestion[], total: number }\u003e(\"/api/assessment/questions\");\nconst assessment = await api.get\u003cAssessment | null\u003e(\"/api/assessment/current\");\n\n// POST\nconst assessment = await api.post\u003cAssessment\u003e(\"/api/assessment/start\");\nconst result = await api.post\u003c{ assessment: Assessment, scores: DISCProfile }\u003e(`/api/assessment/${id}/complete`);\n\n// PUT\nawait api.put\u003cAssessment\u003e(`/api/assessment/${id}/progress`, { currentQuestion, responses });\n```\n\n## IMPORTANT NOTES\n- The Assessment page must be FULL SCREEN — no bottom navigation bar. This means it needs to be outside the AppLayout component in the router.\n- Save progress on every answer so users don't lose their work\n- The assessment should feel premium and thoughtful, not rushed\n- Option cards should be large enough to read on mobile (min-h of about 64px)\n- Make the completion animation special — this is a big moment for the user\n","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}
