### System — `Executing tool: Task`
*2/24/2026, 10:44:54 AM*

{"description":"Distribute intelligence across app","id":"019c908a-12d5-7906-a701-d83f698509fa","prompt":"You need to distribute the personality intelligence blocks across the app's existing pages. The intelligence data is stored as categorized JSON blocks at `GET /api/assessment/intelligence` and `GET /api/assessment/intelligence/:category`.\n\n## READ THESE FILES FIRST\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/types/index.ts (has IntelligenceBlock, IntelligenceByCategory types)\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Profile.tsx\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Growth.tsx\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/home/PersonalitySnapshot.tsx\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/growth/GrowthRecommendations.tsx\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/growth/InsightsSection.tsx\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/growth/TrainingContent.tsx\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/lib/api.ts\n\n## INTELLIGENCE BLOCK STRUCTURE\nThe API returns blocks grouped by category. Each block has:\n```typescript\ninterface IntelligenceBlock {\n  id: string;\n  category: \"identity\" | \"strength\" | \"growth\" | \"relational\" | \"stress\" | \"graph\";\n  blockKey: string;\n  title: string;\n  content: string; // JSON string - must be JSON.parsed\n}\n```\n\nBlock keys by category:\n- **identity**: personality_blend, primary_type, graph_values\n- **strength**: strength_summary, descriptive_words\n- **growth**: keys_to_excellence, blind_spots, growth_focus\n- **relational**: team_strengths, motivation_style, communication_needs, ideal_environment, complementary_styles\n- **stress**: under_pressure, misinterpretations\n- **graph**: disc_scores\n\nContent shapes (after JSON.parse):\n```typescript\n// identity/personality_blend\n{ primary: \"D\", primaryName: \"Dominant\", secondary: \"I\"|null, secondaryName: \"Influential\"|null, blend: \"Dominant-Influential\", description: \"...\" }\n\n// identity/graph_values\n{ environmentStyle: { D, I, S, C }, basicStyle: { D, I, S, C }, scores: { D, I, S, C } }\n\n// strength/strength_summary\n{ strengths: string[] }\n\n// strength/descriptive_words\n{ words: string[] }\n\n// growth/keys_to_excellence\n{ keys: string[] }\n\n// growth/blind_spots\n{ blindSpots: string[] }\n\n// growth/growth_focus\n{ areas: { title: string, description: string, priority: number }[] }\n\n// relational/team_strengths\n{ strengths: string[] }\n\n// relational/motivation_style\n{ description: string }\n\n// relational/communication_needs\n{ needs: string[] }\n\n// relational/ideal_environment\n{ traits: string[] }\n\n// stress/under_pressure\n{ behaviors: string[] }\n\n// stress/misinterpretations\n{ misinterpretations: string[] }\n\n// graph/disc_scores\n{ scores: { D, I, S, C }, environmentStyle: { D, I, S, C }, basicStyle: { D, I, S, C } }\n```\n\n## CHANGES NEEDED\n\n### 1. Create a shared hook: useIntelligence\n\nCreate `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/hooks/useIntelligence.ts`:\n\n```typescript\nimport { useQuery } from \"@tanstack/react-query\";\nimport { api } from \"@/lib/api\";\nimport type { IntelligenceByCategory, IntelligenceBlock } from \"@/types\";\n\nexport function useIntelligence() {\n  return useQuery({\n    queryKey: [\"intelligence\"],\n    queryFn: () =\u003e api.get\u003cIntelligenceByCategory\u003e(\"/api/assessment/intelligence\"),\n  });\n}\n\nexport function useIntelligenceCategory(category: string) {\n  return useQuery({\n    queryKey: [\"intelligence\", category],\n    queryFn: () =\u003e api.get\u003cIntelligenceBlock[]\u003e(`/api/assessment/intelligence/${category}`),\n  });\n}\n\n// Helper to safely parse block content\nexport function parseBlockContent\u003cT\u003e(block: IntelligenceBlock | undefined): T | null {\n  if (!block?.content) return null;\n  try {\n    return JSON.parse(block.content) as T;\n  } catch {\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\nexport function findBlock(blocks: IntelligenceBlock[] | undefined, blockKey: string): IntelligenceBlock | undefined {\n  return blocks?.find((b) =\u003e b.blockKey === blockKey);\n}\n```\n\n### 2. Create DISC Graph Component\n\nCreate `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/profile/DISCGraph.tsx`:\n\nA visual DISC score bar chart that shows the 4 scores as horizontal bars. Props:\n```typescript\ninterface DISCGraphProps {\n  scores: { D: number; I: number; S: number; C: number };\n  environmentStyle?: { D: number; I: number; S: number; C: number };\n  basicStyle?: { D: number; I: number; S: number; C: number };\n  compact?: boolean; // smaller version for cards\n}\n```\n\nDesign:\n- 4 horizontal bars with labels D, I, S, C\n- Each bar fills to its percentage (0-100)\n- Color coding: D = red/coral (hsl(0, 72%, 55%)), I = amber/gold (primary), S = teal (accent), C = blue (hsl(220, 60%, 55%))\n- Show percentage values to the right\n- Animated fill on mount (CSS transition from 0 width to actual width)\n- In compact mode, use thinner bars and smaller text\n- Glass card wrapper\n\n### 3. Update Profile.tsx\n\nMajor enhancement to the Profile page. The profile should now show intelligence-powered data when available:\n\nFetch intelligence data:\n```typescript\nconst { data: intelligence } = useIntelligence();\n```\n\nReplace the existing personality display with intelligence-powered sections:\n\n1. **Identity Section** (replaces personality type card):\n   - Show personality blend name from `identity/personality_blend` block\n   - Show primary type descriptors from `identity/primary_type` block\n   - Show descriptive words from `strength/descriptive_words` as tag badges\n\n2. **DISC Graph Card** (NEW):\n   - Use the DISCGraph component with data from `graph/disc_scores` block\n   - Show both environment and basic style\n   - Glass card with \"Your DISC Profile\" header\n\n3. **Strengths Section** (enhanced):\n   - Show strength summaries from `strength/strength_summary` as a list\n   - Keep existing traits display as fallback\n\n4. **Growth Section** (enhanced):\n   - Show growth focus from `growth/growth_focus` as priority cards\n   - Show blind spots from `growth/blind_spots` as subtle warning items\n\nKeep the existing display as fallback when no intelligence blocks exist (backwards compatible). The intelligence blocks should take priority when available.\n\nKeep the existing Privacy \u0026 Visibility section unchanged at the bottom.\n\nAdd a link to \"View Full Report\" at the bottom of the personality section that navigates to `/profile/report`.\n\n### 4. Update Growth.tsx\n\nEnhance the Growth page to use intelligence blocks:\n\nFetch growth intelligence:\n```typescript\nconst { data: growthBlocks } = useIntelligenceCategory(\"growth\");\n```\n\nIf growth blocks exist, show them in GrowthRecommendations instead of the CSV-parsed growth areas:\n- Keys to Excellence: bullet list\n- Blind Spots: warning-styled list\n- Growth Focus Areas: numbered priority cards\n\nPass intelligence blocks to GrowthRecommendations as an additional prop.\n\n### 5. Update GrowthRecommendations.tsx\n\nAdd intelligence support:\n```typescript\ninterface GrowthRecommendationsProps {\n  profile: PersonalityProfile | undefined;\n  intelligenceBlocks?: IntelligenceBlock[];\n}\n```\n\nWhen `intelligenceBlocks` are available, show structured intelligence data instead of the CSV-parsed growth areas:\n- Parse `keys_to_excellence` and show as action items\n- Parse `blind_spots` and show with amber/warning styling\n- Parse `growth_focus` and show as numbered priority cards\n\nFall back to existing CSV-based display when no intelligence blocks exist.\n\n### 6. Update PersonalitySnapshot.tsx (Dashboard card)\n\nFetch identity intelligence inline. When intelligence is available, show:\n- Primary DISC letter with color\n- Blend name\n- First 3 descriptive words as tags\n\nWhen no intelligence exists, keep existing behavior.\n\n### 7. Update Dashboard.tsx\n\nThe Dashboard already uses PersonalitySnapshot. Just make sure the query for profile still works. No major changes needed here since PersonalitySnapshot will handle its own intelligence fetching.\n\n## DESIGN PATTERNS\n- Use the `glass` class for all cards\n- `animate-fade-in-up` with staggered delays\n- Keep the dark, sophisticated theme\n- DISC colors: D=coral/red, I=amber/primary, S=teal/accent, C=blue\n- Font: DM Sans body, Playfair Display headings\n- Tag badges: `rounded-md bg-X/10 px-2.5 py-0.5 text-xs font-medium text-X`\n- Priority numbers: small accent circles with the number\n- Icons from lucide-react: Target, Shield, Sparkles, TrendingUp, Brain, Zap, Eye, AlertTriangle\n\n## API PATTERNS\n```typescript\nimport { api } from \"@/lib/api\";\nconst intelligence = await api.get\u003cIntelligenceByCategory\u003e(\"/api/assessment/intelligence\");\nconst blocks = await api.get\u003cIntelligenceBlock[]\u003e(\"/api/assessment/intelligence/growth\");\n```\n\n## IMPORTANT\n- All intelligence content is a JSON string that must be JSON.parsed before use\n- Always check if intelligence data exists before using it — fall back to existing profile data\n- Don't break existing functionality — the app must work even if no assessment has been completed\n- Keep backwards compatibility with CSV-parsed profile fields\n","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

{"description":"Build Full Report View page","id":"019c908e-d977-7a92-b1c1-697269f85b9b","prompt":"You need to build an optional Full Report View page for legacy compatibility at `/profile/report`. This is at /home/user/workspace/webapp/.\n\n## READ THESE FILES FIRST\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/types/index.ts (IntelligenceBlock, IntelligenceByCategory, DISCScores types)\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/hooks/useIntelligence.ts\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/profile/DISCGraph.tsx\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/App.tsx\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/lib/api.ts\n- /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Profile.tsx (to see the existing pattern)\n\n## WHAT TO BUILD\n\n### 1. Create Full Report Page\n\nCreate `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/FullReport.tsx`:\n\nThis page compiles ALL intelligence blocks into a single, printable report view. It should feel like a premium personality report document.\n\nThe page should:\n1. Fetch all intelligence via `useIntelligence()`\n2. Fetch the user's profile via `api.get\u003cPersonalityProfile\u003e(\"/api/profile\")`\n3. If no intelligence data exists, redirect to `/assessment`\n4. Display ALL blocks in a structured document layout\n\nStructure (top to bottom):\n\n**Header Section:**\n- \"Your Personality Report\" title (Playfair Display)\n- Personality blend subtitle\n- Assessment completion date\n\n**Identity Section:**\n- Full DISC graph (from DISCGraph component, NOT compact)\n- Personality blend description\n- Primary type with descriptive words\n- Environment style vs Basic style comparison\n\n**Strengths Section:**\n- Key strengths as a bullet list\n- Descriptive words as tag badges\n\n**Growth Section:**\n- Keys to excellence as a numbered list\n- Blind spots with amber/warning styling\n- Growth focus areas as priority cards\n\n**Relational Section:**\n- Team strengths\n- Motivation style (paragraph)\n- Communication needs (list)\n- Ideal environment (list)\n- Complementary styles\n\n**Stress Section:**\n- Under pressure behaviors\n- How others may misread you\n\nDesign:\n- Full-width scrollable page\n- Glass cards for each section\n- Clean typography with section headers\n- A \"Back to Profile\" button (ChevronLeft) at the top\n- Each section separated by a subtle divider or spacing\n- Staggered fade-in animations\n- Responsive but designed mobile-first\n- No bottom navigation (this is outside AppLayout just like assessment)\n\n### 2. Add Route in App.tsx\n\nAdd the full report route inside the ProtectedRoute but OUTSIDE AppLayout:\n```tsx\n\u003cRoute path=\"/profile/report\" element={\u003cFullReport /\u003e} /\u003e\n```\n\nImport it at the top:\n```tsx\nimport FullReport from \"@/pages/FullReport\";\n```\n\n## DESIGN SYSTEM\n- `glass` class for cards\n- Font: DM Sans (body), Playfair Display (display/font-display)\n- DISC colors: D = coral/red (use `text-red-400`), I = amber (use `text-primary`), S = teal (use `text-accent`), C = blue (use `text-blue-400`)\n- `animate-fade-in-up` with staggered delays\n- Dark theme, navy backgrounds\n- Use lucide-react icons: ChevronLeft, Fingerprint, Sparkles, Target, Users, Brain, Zap, ShieldAlert, ArrowRight\n\n## HELPER PATTERN\n```typescript\nimport { useIntelligence, findBlock, parseBlockContent } from \"@/hooks/useIntelligence\";\n\nconst { data: intelligence, isLoading } = useIntelligence();\nconst identityBlocks = intelligence?.identity;\nconst blendBlock = findBlock(identityBlocks, \"personality_blend\");\nconst blendData = parseBlockContent\u003c{\n  primary: string;\n  primaryName: string;\n  secondary: string | null;\n  secondaryName: string | null;\n  blend: string;\n  description: string;\n}\u003e(blendBlock);\n```\n\n## API PATTERNS\n```typescript\nimport { api } from \"@/lib/api\";\nconst intelligence = await api.get\u003cIntelligenceByCategory\u003e(\"/api/assessment/intelligence\");\n```\n","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

### Vibecode Agent
*2/24/2026, 12:23:35 PM*

Everything for Prompt 7 is complete. Here's a summary of everything built:

**Backend — Assessment & Intelligence Engine:**
- **24 DISC questions** across 6 groups (Work Approach, Decision Making, Relationships, Stress & Conflict, Values & Environment, Growth & Aspiration)
- **Modular scoring engine** (`scoring.ts`) — placeholder DISC scoring with `scoreDISC()` designed to be swapped with proprietary logic
- **Modular intelligence generator** (`intelligence.ts`) — produces 16 structured blocks across 6 categories via `generateIntelligence()`, also swappable
- **7 API endpoints**: questions, current progress, start, save progress, complete, get all intelligence, get by category
- **Assessment completion** triggers scoring, generates intelligence blocks, updates PersonalityProfile + User models
- **Stress intelligence** injected into AI Chat system prompt for personality-aware coaching
- **Raw responses stored separately** from scores for future re-scoring

**Frontend — Assessment Experience:**
- Full-screen assessment at `/assessment` (no bottom nav)
- One question at a time, progress bar, back/forward navigation
- Auto-saves progress on every answer
- Animated results reveal with DISC score bars
- Links from EmptyProfile, PersonalitySnapshot, and GrowthRecommendations all point to `/assessment`

**Frontend — Distributed Intelligence:**
- **Profile tab**: Personality blend, DISC graph, numbered strengths, growth focus priorities, blind spots
- **Growth tab**: Intelligence-powered recommendations (keys to excellence, blind spots, growth focus)
- **Home tab**: PersonalitySnapshot shows DISC letter + blend + descriptive words
- **Full Report** at `/profile/report`: comprehensive 5-section report with all 16 intelligence blocks
- All pages fall back to CSV-based display when no intelligence data exists

### Vibecode Agent
*2/24/2026, 12:24:52 PM*

The auth handler only registers `POST` and `GET` methods. Better Auth's `get-session` and `send-verification-otp` should work with those, but let me check if the issue is that the auth middleware itself is failing and blocking the auth handler. The middleware calls `auth.api.getSession` on every request -- if that throws on auth routes (before they're handled), it could cause 404s.

Let me check if there's a Prisma schema mismatch causing the issue. The schema was changed (Assessment, PersonalityIntelligence models added) -- the `db push` may have succeeded but the generated client might not match.

bash → curl -s -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/auth/email-otp/send-verification-otp -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"email":"test@test.com","type":"sign-in"}' | head -c 300 – Test OTP send endpoint

---
### Vibecode Agent
*2/24/2026, 12:25:30 PM*

Auth routes are working now. The issue was that the auth handler was registered with only `["POST", "GET"]` methods and a `**` glob pattern. Hono's `app.on()` with `**` can behave differently from `app.all()` with `*`. Switching to `app.all("/api/auth/*")` ensures all HTTP methods (GET, POST, PATCH, etc.) are handled by Better Auth.

Try logging in again -- it should work now.

### Vibecode Agent
*2/24/2026, 3:00:35 PM*

I see the issue now. The Profile page uses `api.patch("/api/auth/update-user", { name })` but the `api` helper auto-unwraps `{ data: T }` from the response. Better Auth's `/api/auth/update-user` endpoint doesn't follow the `{ data: T }` envelope pattern - it returns its own format. This means the response handling will break. The fix is to use the auth client directly instead of the custom `api` helper for Better Auth endpoints.

Let me also check what Better Auth's `update-user` endpoint expects and look at the connection components.

---

*2/24/2026, 3:00:36 PM*

### System — `Executing tool: Read`

---
### Vibecode Agent
*2/24/2026, 3:00:41 PM*

Now I have a clear picture. The issue with saving the name is that the Profile page calls `api.patch("/api/auth/update-user", { name })` - but `/api/auth/*` endpoints are handled by Better Auth and don't return the `{ data: T }` envelope. The `api` helper tries to unwrap `.data` from the response, which will fail.

The fix is to use the Better Auth client's `updateUser` method instead. Let me now implement all four changes in parallel using the appropriate agents.

---
### System — `Executing tool: Task`
*2/24/2026, 3:00:49 PM*

{"description":"Fix profile name saving","id":"019c9174-6098-73fe-b9c4-b2d36852ade3","prompt":"Fix the Profile page name saving issue. The problem is in `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Profile.tsx`.\n\nCurrently, line 112 calls:\n```ts\napi.patch(\"/api/auth/update-user\", { name })\n```\n\nThe `api` helper (in `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/lib/api.ts`) auto-unwraps `{ data: T }` from responses. But `/api/auth/update-user` is a Better Auth endpoint that does NOT follow the `{ data: T }` envelope pattern. This means the response handling breaks silently - the request likely succeeds but the error handler may fire due to the response format mismatch.\n\nThe fix: Use the Better Auth client's `updateUser` method instead. The auth client is in `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/lib/auth-client.ts` and exports `authClient`.\n\nIn Profile.tsx, change the `updateName` mutation to use:\n```ts\nauthClient.updateUser({ name })\n```\n\nThis is the proper way to update user data through Better Auth. The `authClient` is already imported indirectly (via `useSession` and `signOut`), so you just need to import `authClient` directly.\n\nSteps:\n1. In Profile.tsx, add `authClient` to the import from `@/lib/auth-client` (it's already exported there)\n2. Change the `updateName` mutation's `mutationFn` from `api.patch(\"/api/auth/update-user\", { name })` to `authClient.updateUser({ name })`\n\nMake sure the rest of the mutation (onSuccess/onError) stays the same.","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

### Vibecode Agent
*2/24/2026, 3:03:39 PM*

All four changes are done:

1. **Profile name saving fixed** -- The issue was that it was using the generic `api.patch()` helper to call a Better Auth endpoint. Better Auth doesn't use the `{ data: T }` response envelope, so the response parsing was failing silently. Switched to `authClient.updateUser({ name })` which handles it correctly. Your name should now save properly.

2. **Home page greeting** -- Already addresses you by name. It pulls your name from the session (e.g. "Good morning, Sarah"). Once you save your name in Profile, it will immediately reflect on the Home page.

3. **Dummy connections added** -- The Connect tab now shows 4 sample connections (Maya Chen, Jordan Rivera, Sam Okafor, Alex Petrov) when you don't have real connections yet. A subtle "Sample connections" badge appears above them so it's clear they're placeholders. Once you get real connections, the dummies disappear automatically.

4. **Connection chat prompts** -- Two discreet prompts were added to the Chat area: "How might I better connect with someone like Maya?" and "What should I know about communicating with Jordan?" They appear below the main suggestions with smaller, more muted styling and a thin "Connections" divider.

{"description":"Update DISC colors in 3 files","id":"019c917c-2d89-7b9b-8884-dca2d93f1aaf","prompt":"Update the DISC personality colors across 3 files to use the correct color mapping:\n- D (Dominance) = Green\n- I (Influence) = Red  \n- S (Steadiness) = Blue\n- C (Conscientiousness) = Yellow\n\nThe colors should be defined as CSS variables in the design system. Here are the specific HSL values to use:\n- disc-d (Green): `142 60% 45%`\n- disc-i (Red): `0 72% 55%`\n- disc-s (Blue): `220 60% 55%`\n- disc-c (Yellow): `45 90% 50%`\n\n**File 1: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/index.css`**\n\nAdd these CSS variables to both `:root` and `.light` sections:\n```css\n/* DISC personality colors */\n--disc-d: 142 60% 45%;\n--disc-i: 0 72% 55%;\n--disc-s: 220 60% 55%;\n--disc-c: 45 90% 50%;\n```\n\n**File 2: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/tailwind.config.ts`**\n\nAdd DISC colors to the `extend.colors` section:\n```ts\ndisc: {\n  d: \"hsl(var(--disc-d))\",\n  i: \"hsl(var(--disc-i))\",\n  s: \"hsl(var(--disc-s))\",\n  c: \"hsl(var(--disc-c))\",\n},\n```\n\n**File 3: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/profile/DISCGraph.tsx`**\n\nReplace the current `discColors` object (lines 10-31) with:\n```ts\nconst discColors: Record\u003cstring, { bg: string; bar: string; text: string }\u003e = {\n  D: {\n    bg: \"bg-disc-d/15\",\n    bar: \"bg-disc-d\",\n    text: \"text-disc-d\",\n  },\n  I: {\n    bg: \"bg-disc-i/15\",\n    bar: \"bg-disc-i\",\n    text: \"text-disc-i\",\n  },\n  S: {\n    bg: \"bg-disc-s/15\",\n    bar: \"bg-disc-s\",\n    text: \"text-disc-s\",\n  },\n  C: {\n    bg: \"bg-disc-c/15\",\n    bar: \"bg-disc-c\",\n    text: \"text-disc-c\",\n  },\n};\n```\n\n**File 4: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/home/PersonalitySnapshot.tsx`**\n\nReplace the `discLetterColors` object (lines 27-32) with:\n```ts\nconst discLetterColors: Record\u003cstring, string\u003e = {\n  D: \"text-disc-d\",\n  I: \"text-disc-i\",\n  S: \"text-disc-s\",\n  C: \"text-disc-c\",\n};\n```\n\n**File 5: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/assessment/ResultsReveal.tsx`**\n\nReplace the `DISC_COLORS` object (lines 7-12) with:\n```ts\nconst DISC_COLORS: Record\u003cstring, string\u003e = {\n  D: \"hsl(142 60% 45%)\",\n  I: \"hsl(0 72% 55%)\",\n  S: \"hsl(220 60% 55%)\",\n  C: \"hsl(45 90% 50%)\",\n};\n```\n\nMake these exact changes. ### Vibecode Agent
*2/24/2026, 3:10:47 PM*

Now I understand the full picture. The app currently defaults to dark mode in `:root` and has `.light` as an override class. For `next-themes` with Tailwind's `darkMode: ["class"]`, the convention is:

- `:root` = light theme (default)
- `.dark` = dark theme

But this app is dark-first. I'll use next-themes with `attribute="class"` and `defaultTheme="dark"`, so it adds a `dark` class to the `<html>` element. I need to restructure the CSS so `:root` holds light values and `.dark` holds the current dark values.

Let me implement the theme toggle and sound effect now.

### Vibecode Agent
*2/24/2026, 3:15:02 PM*

All three changes are done and compiled cleanly:

1. **DISC Colors** -- Updated across the entire app. D = Green, I = Red, S = Blue, C = Yellow. The colors are defined as CSS variables (`--disc-d`, `--disc-i`, `--disc-s`, `--disc-c`) and mapped to Tailwind classes (`disc-d`, `disc-i`, `disc-s`, `disc-c`). Updated in the DISC graph, personality snapshot, and assessment results reveal.

2. **Dark/Light Mode Toggle** -- Added an "Appearance" toggle in the Profile tab's Privacy & Visibility section, right above the sign out button. It uses `next-themes` with the app defaulting to dark mode. Tapping the button switches between dark and light. The CSS was restructured so light colors are in `:root` and dark colors are in `.dark`.

3. **Sound Effect** -- When you tap "Mark as read" on a daily insight, a soft chime plays at 30% volume (softer than whatever your device volume is set to). The sound file is stored locally at `/sounds/insight-read.mp3`.

### Vibecode Agent
*2/24/2026, 3:21:33 PM*

Now I have a complete understanding. Let me plan the approach.

**Design Strategy:** The app is a DISC personality platform. Rather than making it a rainbow, I'll use the four DISC colors as **subtle, semantic accents** throughout the app. The core approach:

1. **Primary color** shifts from warm amber to a **neutral warm white/cream** for buttons and interactive elements -- making the DISC colors pop as the real stars
2. **Each section/feature** gets a DISC color association:
   - **D (Green)** = Growth, Action, Progress bars
   - **I (Red)** = Chat, Social, Connection warmth
   - **S (Blue)** = Stability, Insights, Daily insight
   - **C (Yellow)** = Profile, Analysis, Assessment
3. **Login/brand** uses all four as a subtle quad-dot logo
4. **Navigation** active state uses a neutral foreground (not one DISC color) to avoid implying one type is "the" color
5. **Glassmorphism and dark theme** stay elegant -- DISC colors appear as accent touches, not backgrounds

Let me implement this now across all files in parallel.

{"description":"Update Dashboard + Home components","id":"019c9188-6652-7881-b37a-e54d4081f97d","prompt":"Update the Dashboard (Home) page and its child components to use DISC personality colors as semantic accents. The design system has changed: `primary` is now a neutral dark/light color (no longer amber), and the four DISC colors are the real accent colors. Here's the mapping for the Home page:\n\n**Color Assignments for Home:**\n- Daily Insight = `disc-s` (Blue) - stability, reflection\n- AI Chat card = `disc-i` (Red) - influence, conversation  \n- Personality Snapshot = already uses DISC colors, keep as-is\n- Growth Suggestion = `disc-d` (Green) - growth, action\n- Stats: Entries = `disc-c` (Yellow), Insights = `disc-s` (Blue), Days = `disc-d` (Green)\n\n**File 1: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx`**\n\nReplace all `bg-primary/10` and `text-primary` with the assigned DISC colors for each section. Replace `bg-accent/10` and `text-accent` similarly. Specifically:\n\nFor the stats row:\n- Entries (BookOpen): `bg-disc-c/10` icon bg, `text-disc-c` icon\n- Insights (Lightbulb): `bg-disc-s/10` icon bg, `text-disc-s` icon  \n- Days (CalendarDays): `bg-disc-d/10` icon bg, `text-disc-d` icon\n\n**File 2: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/home/DailyInsightCard.tsx`**\n\nReplace ALL `primary` color references with `disc-s` (blue). This means:\n- `bg-primary/10` → `bg-disc-s/10`\n- `text-primary` → `text-disc-s`\n- `border-l-primary` → `border-l-disc-s`\n- `border-l-primary/50` → `border-l-disc-s/50`\n- `border-l-primary/60` → `border-l-disc-s/60`\n- `bg-primary/15` → `bg-disc-s/15`\n- `animate-glow-pulse` stays as-is\n- The \"Generate\" button gradient: replace `from-primary to-primary/80` with `from-disc-s to-disc-s/80` and `hover:from-disc-s/90 hover:to-disc-s/70`\n- `shadow-primary/10` → `shadow-disc-s/10`\n- The \"Read\" confirmation `text-accent` → `text-disc-s`\n- Remove references to `glow-primary` and `animate-sparkle-glow` (those CSS utilities no longer exist). Replace the unread state with just `border-l-disc-s` without glow classes.\n\n**File 3: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/home/AIChatCard.tsx`**\n\nReplace all primary references with disc-i (red):\n- `bg-primary/10` → `bg-disc-i/10`\n- `text-primary` → `text-disc-i`\n- `hover:bg-primary/20` → `hover:bg-disc-i/20`\n- Remove `hover:glow-primary` (utility no longer exists)\n\n**File 4: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/home/GrowthSuggestionCard.tsx`**\n\nReplace all accent references with disc-d (green):\n- `bg-accent/10` → `bg-disc-d/10`\n- `text-accent` → `text-disc-d`\n- `group-hover:text-accent` → `group-hover:text-disc-d`\n\n**File 5: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/home/PersonalitySnapshot.tsx`**\n\nThis already uses DISC letter colors for the primary letter display. But it uses `accent` for other things. Update:\n- `bg-accent/10` → `bg-disc-c/10` (yellow for analysis/assessment)\n- `text-accent` → `text-disc-c`\n- `group-hover:text-accent` → `group-hover:text-disc-c`\n\nRead each file first, then make the edits. Make sure there are no leftover `text-primary`, `bg-primary`, `text-accent`, `bg-accent` references (only those explicitly in these components -- don't touch shared UI components like Button).","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

{"description":"Update Growth + Connections pages","id":"019c918d-ba6b-7f0a-9a4e-d957923ef13c","prompt":"Update Growth and Connections pages/components to use DISC colors. The design system changed: `primary` is now neutral, `accent` is now neutral. DISC colors are the real accents.\n\n**Color assignments:**\n- Growth page = `disc-d` (Green - growth, action) for main section, `disc-c` (Yellow) for analysis elements, `disc-s` (Blue) for insight elements\n- Connections page = `disc-i` (Red - influence, relational) for connection cards, `disc-d` (Green) for accept actions\n\n## Growth Components\n\n**File 1: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Growth.tsx`**\nRead first. Replace:\n- `bg-primary/10` and `text-primary` → `bg-disc-d/10` and `text-disc-d` (for growth-related icons/labels)\n- `bg-accent/10` and `text-accent` → `bg-disc-d/10` and `text-disc-d` (for growth recommendations section)\n\n**File 2: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/growth/GrowthRecommendations.tsx`**\nRead first. Replace:\n- `bg-accent/10` and `text-accent` → `bg-disc-d/10` and `text-disc-d` (main section)\n- `text-amber-500` for blind spots → `text-disc-c` (yellow, conscientiousness)\n- `bg-primary/10` and `text-primary` → `bg-disc-d/10` and `text-disc-d` (growth focus)\n\n**File 3: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/growth/InsightsSection.tsx`**\nRead first. Replace:\n- `bg-primary/10` and `text-primary` → `bg-disc-s/10` and `text-disc-s` (insights section header)\n- `border-l-primary` → `border-l-disc-s` (personality insight left border)\n- `border-l-accent` → `border-l-disc-d` (growth insight left border)\n- `bg-primary/10 text-primary` badge → `bg-disc-s/10 text-disc-s` (personality badge)\n- `bg-accent/10 text-accent` badge → `bg-disc-d/10 text-disc-d` (growth badge)\n- Any remaining `text-accent` or `bg-accent` → appropriate disc color\n\n**File 4: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/growth/TrainingContent.tsx`**\nRead first. Replace:\n- `bg-primary/10` and `text-primary` → `bg-disc-c/10` and `text-disc-c` (training/analysis section)\n- `group-hover:text-primary` → `group-hover:text-disc-c`\n\n## Connection Components\n\n**File 5: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Connections.tsx`**\nRead first. Replace:\n- `bg-primary/20` and `text-primary` → `bg-disc-i/20` and `text-disc-i` (connection avatars/emphasis)\n- `bg-accent/10 text-accent` → `bg-disc-d/10 text-disc-d` (accept button styling)\n- `bg-primary/10` → `bg-disc-i/10` (trait badges)\n\n**File 6: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/connections/ConnectionCard.tsx`**\nRead first. Replace:\n- `border-primary/20` → `border-disc-i/20`\n- `bg-primary/10` → `bg-disc-i/10`\n- `text-primary` → `text-disc-i`\n\n**File 7: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/connections/PendingRequestCard.tsx`**\nRead first. Replace:\n- `border-accent/20` → `border-disc-d/20`\n- `bg-accent/10` → `bg-disc-d/10`\n- `text-accent` → `text-disc-d`\n- `bg-accent hover:bg-accent/90` → `bg-disc-d hover:bg-disc-d/90` (accept button)\n\n**File 8: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/connections/ConnectionProfileSheet.tsx`**\nRead first. Replace:\n- `bg-primary/10 text-primary border-primary/20` → `bg-disc-i/10 text-disc-i border-disc-i/20` (trait badges)\n- `bg-accent/10 text-accent border-accent/20` → `bg-disc-d/10 text-disc-d border-disc-d/20` (strength/values badges)\n\nRead each file before editing. {"description":"Update Profile, Nav, Login, Assessment","id":"019c9193-6e26-7c1e-9a14-9524066ce4ee","prompt":"Update Profile page, Navigation, Login, Assessment, and remaining components to use DISC colors. The design system changed: `primary` is now neutral (dark charcoal in light mode, near-white in dark mode), `accent` is now neutral. DISC colors (disc-d=green, disc-i=red, disc-s=blue, disc-c=yellow) are the real accents.\n\n## Navigation\n\n**File 1: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/layout/BottomNav.tsx`**\n\nRead first. Currently active tab uses `bg-primary/12 text-primary`. Change to use `text-foreground` for active state to keep nav neutral, but add a small DISC-colored indicator dot. Replace:\n- `bg-primary/12 text-primary scale-105` → `bg-secondary text-foreground scale-105`\n- `text-primary` on the label → `text-foreground font-semibold`\n\n**File 2: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/layout/SidebarNav.tsx`**\n\nRead first. Replace:\n- `bg-primary/10 text-primary glow-primary` → `bg-secondary text-foreground` (active state -- keep neutral, remove glow-primary which no longer exists)\n\n## Login / Auth\n\n**File 3: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Login.tsx`**\n\nRead first. The login page should use all four DISC colors subtly. Replace:\n- The background glow orbs: replace the single `bg-primary/5` and `bg-accent/5` orbs with four subtle DISC-colored orbs:\n  Replace the entire `{/* Background effects */}` div with:\n  ```tsx\n  \u003cdiv className=\"pointer-events-none absolute inset-0 overflow-hidden\"\u003e\n    \u003cdiv className=\"absolute left-[15%] top-[20%] h-72 w-72 rounded-full bg-disc-d/5 blur-3xl animate-glow-pulse\" /\u003e\n    \u003cdiv className=\"absolute right-[15%] top-[30%] h-64 w-64 rounded-full bg-disc-i/5 blur-3xl animate-glow-pulse\" style={{ animationDelay: \"1s\" }} /\u003e\n    \u003cdiv className=\"absolute left-[25%] bottom-[20%] h-80 w-80 rounded-full bg-disc-s/5 blur-3xl animate-glow-pulse\" style={{ animationDelay: \"2s\" }} /\u003e\n    \u003cdiv className=\"absolute right-[20%] bottom-[30%] h-60 w-60 rounded-full bg-disc-c/5 blur-3xl animate-glow-pulse\" style={{ animationDelay: \"3s\" }} /\u003e\n  \u003c/div\u003e\n  ```\n- The brand icon: replace the single dot `\u003cdiv className=\"h-3 w-3 rounded-full bg-primary\" /\u003e` inside `bg-primary/10 glow-primary` with four DISC dots in a 2x2 grid. Replace the entire brand icon div with:\n  ```tsx\n  \u003cdiv className=\"mx-auto mb-4 flex h-12 w-12 items-center justify-center rounded-xl bg-secondary\"\u003e\n    \u003cdiv className=\"grid grid-cols-2 gap-1\"\u003e\n      \u003cdiv className=\"h-2 w-2 rounded-full bg-disc-d\" /\u003e\n      \u003cdiv className=\"h-2 w-2 rounded-full bg-disc-i\" /\u003e\n      \u003cdiv className=\"h-2 w-2 rounded-full bg-disc-s\" /\u003e\n      \u003cdiv className=\"h-2 w-2 rounded-full bg-disc-c\" /\u003e\n    \u003c/div\u003e\n  \u003c/div\u003e\n  ```\n- `focus:border-primary` on the input → `focus:border-disc-s`\n\n**File 4: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/VerifyOtp.tsx`**\n\nRead first. Apply similar changes -- replace any `primary` color references with DISC colors. Use `disc-s` (blue) as the main accent for the OTP verification flow. Replace:\n- `bg-primary/10` → `bg-disc-s/10`\n- `text-primary` → `text-disc-s`\n- `glow-primary` → remove (no longer exists)\n- `bg-primary/5` background orb → `bg-disc-s/5`\n- `bg-accent/5` background orb → `bg-disc-d/5`\n\n## Profile Page\n\n**File 5: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Profile.tsx`**\n\nRead first. This is a large file. Replace color references with DISC colors:\n- Avatar border `border-primary/20` → `border-disc-c/20`\n- Avatar fallback `bg-primary/10 text-primary` → `bg-disc-c/10 text-disc-c`\n- Assessment complete badge `bg-accent/10 text-accent` → `bg-disc-d/10 text-disc-d`\n- Descriptive words tags `bg-primary/10 ... text-primary` → `bg-disc-s/10 ... text-disc-s`\n- Key Strengths: `text-accent` Sparkles icon → `text-disc-d` (green for strengths)\n- Strength numbered badges `bg-accent/10 text-accent` → `bg-disc-d/10 text-disc-d`\n- Growth Focus: `text-primary` Target icon → `text-disc-d` \n- Growth numbered badges `bg-primary/10 text-primary` → `bg-disc-d/10 text-disc-d`\n- Blind Spots: keep `border-amber-500/10` but change `text-amber-500` → `text-disc-c`\n- Fallback tags: TagList with `color=\"primary\"` → `color=\"accent\"` actually these use a color prop... read the TagList component too.\n- View Full Report link: `bg-primary/10` icon → `bg-disc-s/10`, `text-primary` → `text-disc-s`, `group-hover:text-primary` → `group-hover:text-disc-s`\n- Privacy section Shield icon: `bg-accent/10` → `bg-disc-s/10`, `text-accent` → `text-disc-s`\n\n**File 6: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/profile/TagList.tsx`**\n\nRead first. Update the color mapping:\n- `color=\"primary\"` → should render `bg-disc-i/10 text-disc-i border-disc-i/20`\n- `color=\"accent\"` → should render `bg-disc-d/10 text-disc-d border-disc-d/20`\n\n**File 7: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/profile/EmptyProfile.tsx`**\n\nRead first. Replace:\n- `bg-primary/10` → `bg-disc-c/10`\n- `text-primary` → `text-disc-c`\n- Remove `glow-primary` (no longer exists)\n\n## Assessment Components\n\n**File 8: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/assessment/AssessmentHeader.tsx`**\n\nRead first. Replace:\n- `bg-primary` progress bar fill → `bg-disc-s` (blue for progress)\n\n**File 9: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/assessment/QuestionCard.tsx`**\n\nRead first. Replace selected answer styling:\n- `border-primary bg-primary/10` → `border-disc-s bg-disc-s/10`\n- Any `shadow-[0_0_20px_hsl(38_92%_55%/0.15)]` → remove or replace with no shadow\n- `bg-primary` on selected option letter badge → `bg-disc-s`\n\n**File 10: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/assessment/LoadingAssessment.tsx`**\n\nRead first. Replace:\n- `bg-primary/10` → `bg-disc-s/10`\n- `text-primary` → `text-disc-s`\n\n**File 11: `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/FullReport.tsx`**\n\nRead first. Replace:\n- `bg-primary/10` and `text-primary` → use contextual DISC colors: `disc-s` for identity section, `disc-d` for strengths/growth, `disc-c` for blind spots\n- `bg-accent/10` and `text-accent` → `bg-disc-d/10` and `text-disc-d`\n- `text-amber-500` → `text-disc-c`\n- `text-red-400` → `text-disc-i`\n\nRead EVERY file before editing. Be thorough.","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

---
### Vibecode Agent
*2/24/2026, 3:47:26 PM*

Everything is done. Here's a summary of the redesign:

**Core philosophy:** The base palette shifted to neutral slate tones, so the four DISC colors become the clear visual language of the app. Not a rainbow -- each color has a purpose.

**Color assignments throughout the app:**
- **D Green** -- Growth tab, strengths, accept buttons, growth recommendations
- **I Red** -- Chat bubbles, connection cards, conversation highlights, relational elements
- **S Blue** -- Daily insights, assessment progress bars, personality analysis, selected answers
- **C Yellow** -- Profile avatar, training section, blind spots/warnings, journal entries stat

**What changed:**
- CSS variables restructured: `primary`/`accent` are now neutral, DISC colors are the stars
- Login page: four DISC-colored background orbs + a 2x2 dot grid as the brand icon
- Navigation: active state is now neutral (no color bias toward one personality type)
- Every page section uses contextually appropriate DISC colors
- Dark mode DISC colors are slightly brighter for readability on dark backgrounds
- Removed old amber glow/sparkle effects, replaced with cleaner styling
- All 20+ component files updated consistently

### Vibecode Agent
*2/24/2026, 3:51:34 PM*

The issue is clear: Playfair Display is a high-contrast serif font with thin strokes that wash out against light backgrounds. I'll replace it with **Source Serif 4** -- a professional, highly readable serif that has good weight at all sizes and looks great in both light and dark modes. It's a strong editorial font that still feels premium.

For the light mode, I'll shift the background from a cool blue-grey 97% to a warmer cream/off-white tone.

Two changes made:

1. **Heading font** -- Replaced Playfair Display with **Source Serif 4**. It's a modern editorial serif with heavier stroke weight, so it stays crisp and readable on light backgrounds. Still looks professional and premium.

2. **Light mode background** -- Shifted from a cool blue-grey to a warm **cream/off-white** (hue 36, warm undertone). Cards, borders, and secondary elements all follow the same warm tone so it feels softer on the eyes. Also darkened the DISC colors slightly in light mode so they contrast well against the cream background.

### User
*3/9/2026, 11:12:16 AM*

Prompt: AI Contextual Memory System

Enhance the AI system to store contextual memory for each user.

The AI should remember:

Their DISC personality blend
recurring concerns
growth themes
communication struggles
connection challenges

The AI should reference past conversations naturally when relevant.

Ensure memory is:

• private
• secure
• user-specific

This must increase personalization over time.

The goal of the AI agent is to help the person feel safe to communicate and ask questions, while helping them learn about themselves and the person they're asking about. Ultimately to learn how they can grow in understanding themselves and how to communicate better to each personality style. 

The AI answers should be in accordance to each user's personality. For example, a D personality should receive more direct and to the point answers, while a S personality should receive more intentional and emotional consideration in how the AI agent answers.

{"description":"Implement backend memory system","id":"019cd361-d322-7036-8b56-ebbc1b6036f7","prompt":"You are a backend developer working on a Hono/Bun/Prisma/SQLite backend at /home/user/workspace/backend.\n\n## Task: Implement AI Contextual Memory System\n\n### What to build:\nA `UserMemory` model that stores extracted themes from conversations, and enhance the AI chat to:\n1. Extract memory after each conversation message (async, non-blocking)\n2. Inject relevant memories into the AI system prompt\n3. Adapt response style based on DISC personality type\n4. Reference past patterns naturally in responses\n\n### Step 1: Add `UserMemory` model to Prisma schema\n\nAdd to `/home/user/workspace/backend/prisma/schema.prisma`:\n\n```prisma\nmodel UserMemory {\n  id        String   @id @default(cuid())\n  userId    String\n  user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n  \n  // Memory categories\n  category  String   // \"recurring_concern\", \"growth_theme\", \"communication_struggle\", \"connection_challenge\", \"disc_insight\", \"general\"\n  topic     String   // Short label e.g. \"patience in meetings\", \"conflict avoidance\"\n  summary   String   // Full memory summary 1-3 sentences\n  frequency Int      @default(1)  // how many times this theme appeared\n  lastSeen  DateTime @default(now())\n  \n  createdAt DateTime @default(now())\n  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt\n  \n  @@index([userId, category])\n}\n```\n\nAlso add to User model:\n```\n  userMemories   UserMemory[]\n```\n\n### Step 2: Create migration\nRun:\n```bash\ncd /home/user/workspace/backend \u0026\u0026 bunx prisma migrate dev --create-only --name add_user_memory\n```\nThen run:\n```bash\ncd /home/user/workspace/backend \u0026\u0026 bunx prisma migrate deploy\n```\n\n### Step 3: Update the chat route at `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/chat.ts`\n\nHere is the COMPLETE updated chat.ts file you should write. Read the current file first, then replace it entirely with this enhanced version:\n\n```typescript\nimport { Hono } from \"hono\";\nimport { zValidator } from \"@hono/zod-validator\";\nimport Anthropic from \"@anthropic-ai/sdk\";\nimport { prisma } from \"../prisma\";\nimport { CreateConversationSchema, SendMessageSchema } from \"../types\";\nimport { env } from \"../env\";\nimport type { User, Session } from \"../auth\";\n\ntype Variables = {\n  user: User | null;\n  session: Session | null;\n};\n\nconst chatRouter = new Hono\u003c{ Variables: Variables }\u003e();\n\nconst anthropic = new Anthropic({\n  apiKey: env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY,\n});\n\nfunction getDiscStyleInstructions(personalityType: string | null): string {\n  if (!personalityType) return \"\";\n\n  const type = personalityType.toLowerCase();\n\n  if (\n    type.includes(\"dominant\") ||\n    type.includes(\"driver\") ||\n    type === \"d\" ||\n    type.startsWith(\"high d\") ||\n    (type.startsWith(\"d-\") || type.startsWith(\"d/\"))\n  ) {\n    return `\nCOMMUNICATION STYLE DIRECTIVE — High D (Dominant/Driver):\n- Be concise and direct. Lead with the answer, then explain if needed.\n- Focus on outcomes, results, and action items.\n- Skip unnecessary pleasantries — value their time.\n- Use bullet points and clear structure.\n- Challenge them productively — they appreciate directness.\n- Frame growth in terms of results and competitive advantage.\n- Keep responses tight: no fluff, no over-explaining.`;\n  }\n\n  if (\n    type.includes(\"influen\") ||\n    type.includes(\"inspir\") ||\n    type === \"i\" ||\n    type.startsWith(\"high i\") ||\n    type.startsWith(\"i-\") || type.startsWith(\"i/\")\n  ) {\n    return `\nCOMMUNICATION STYLE DIRECTIVE — High I (Influential/Inspiring):\n- Be warm, enthusiastic, and encouraging.\n- Celebrate their progress and strengths openly.\n- Use stories, analogies, and relatable examples.\n- Focus on relationships, possibilities, and big-picture thinking.\n- Keep the tone optimistic and energizing.\n- Use conversational, friendly language.\n- Frame growth in terms of impact on relationships and personal expression.`;\n  }\n\n  if (\n    type.includes(\"steady\") ||\n    type.includes(\"support\") ||\n    type === \"s\" ||\n    type.startsWith(\"high s\") ||\n    type.startsWith(\"s-\") || type.startsWith(\"s/\")\n  ) {\n    return `\nCOMMUNICATION STYLE DIRECTIVE — High S (Steady/Supportive):\n- Be calm, patient, and deeply reassuring.\n- Take things step-by-step — never rush or overwhelm.\n- Always validate their feelings before offering any advice.\n- Provide stability and consistency in your responses.\n- Avoid pressure, urgency, or sudden changes in direction.\n- Use gentle, warm, supportive phrasing.\n- Acknowledge the emotional weight of situations before moving to solutions.\n- Frame growth as a natural, gradual process — no sudden upheaval needed.`;\n  }\n\n  if (\n    type.includes(\"conscien\") ||\n    type.includes(\"analytic\") ||\n    type === \"c\" ||\n    type.startsWith(\"high c\") ||\n    type.startsWith(\"c-\") || type.startsWith(\"c/\")\n  ) {\n    return `\nCOMMUNICATION STYLE DIRECTIVE — High C (Conscientious/Analytical):\n- Be logical, structured, and precise.\n- Provide thorough explanations with clear reasoning.\n- Use frameworks, models, and data when relevant.\n- Be methodical — organize thoughts with numbered steps or categories.\n- Cite psychological principles where appropriate.\n- Avoid vague or overly emotional language.\n- Frame growth in terms of systematic improvement and measurable progress.`;\n  }\n\n  return `\nThe user's personality type is \"${personalityType}\". Adapt your communication style to match what would resonate best with this personality profile.`;\n}\n\nfunction buildMemoryContext(memories: { category: string; topic: string; summary: string; frequency: number; lastSeen: Date }[]): string {\n  if (!memories || memories.length === 0) return \"\";\n\n  const grouped: Record\u003cstring, typeof memories\u003e = {};\n  for (const m of memories) {\n    if (!grouped[m.category]) grouped[m.category] = [];\n    grouped[m.category].push(m);\n  }\n\n  const categoryLabels: Record\u003cstring, string\u003e = {\n    recurring_concern: \"Recurring Concerns\",\n    growth_theme: \"Growth Themes\",\n    communication_struggle: \"Communication Struggles\",\n    connection_challenge: \"Connection Challenges\",\n    disc_insight: \"Self-Discovery Insights\",\n    general: \"Notable Patterns\",\n  };\n\n  let context = \"\\n\\nCONTEXTUAL MEMORY (private, user-specific — use naturally, not as a list):\\n\";\n  context += \"Reference these patterns naturally when relevant. Phrases like 'You've mentioned before...' or 'This connects to something we've explored...' feel personal and trustworthy.\\n\";\n\n  for (const [category, items] of Object.entries(grouped)) {\n    const label = categoryLabels[category] || category;\n    context += `\\n${label}:\\n`;\n    for (const item of items.slice(0, 5)) { // max 5 per category\n      const timeSince = getTimeSinceLabel(item.lastSeen);\n      context += `- \"${item.topic}\" (${timeSince}${item.frequency \u003e 1 ? `, mentioned ${item.frequency}x` : \"\"}): ${item.summary}\\n`;\n    }\n  }\n\n  context += `\\nIMPORTANT: Only reference memories when genuinely relevant. Don't force callbacks. When you do reference a past theme, be natural — \"Last time you mentioned...\" or \"This reminds me of something you shared before about...\" Make the user feel truly known and remembered.`;\n\n  return context;\n}\n\nfunction getTimeSinceLabel(date: Date): string {\n  const now = new Date();\n  const diffMs = now.getTime() - new Date(date).getTime();\n  const diffDays = Math.floor(diffMs / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));\n  \n  if (diffDays === 0) return \"today\";\n  if (diffDays === 1) return \"yesterday\";\n  if (diffDays \u003c 7) return `${diffDays} days ago`;\n  if (diffDays \u003c 14) return \"last week\";\n  if (diffDays \u003c 30) return `${Math.floor(diffDays / 7)} weeks ago`;\n  if (diffDays \u003c 60) return \"last month\";\n  return `${Math.floor(diffDays / 30)} months ago`;\n}\n\nfunction buildSystemPrompt(context: {\n  profile: {\n    personalityType: string | null;\n    traits: string | null;\n    strengths: string | null;\n    growthAreas: string | null;\n    values: string | null;\n    communicationStyle: string | null;\n    behavioralTendencies: string | null;\n    stressTendencies: string | null;\n    communicationPreferences: string | null;\n  } | null;\n  userName: string | null;\n  conversationContext: string | null;\n  stressBlocks?: { title: string; content: string }[];\n  memories?: { category: string; topic: string; summary: string; frequency: number; lastSeen: Date }[];\n}): string {\n  const { profile, userName, conversationContext, stressBlocks, memories } = context;\n\n  let systemPrompt = `You are an adaptive personality coach within the Personality Insights platform. You are intelligent, emotionally aware, respectful, and genuinely helpful. You help users understand themselves, improve their relationships, and grow personally.\n\nCORE PRINCIPLES:\n- Never be robotic or generic. Every response should feel personal and thoughtful.\n- Be emotionally intelligent — read between the lines of what users say.\n- Adapt your depth based on the question: simple questions get clear answers, deep questions get reflective exploration.\n- Use the user's own language patterns back to them when it feels natural.\n- Balance warmth with substance — be kind but never empty.\n- When you notice emotional weight in a message, acknowledge it before moving to advice.\n- Help users feel safe to communicate openly — this is a judgment-free space.\n- Help users see patterns they might not see themselves.\n- Offer frameworks for thinking, not just answers.\n- Your goal: help each person feel deeply understood, learn about themselves, and grow in how they communicate with all personality styles.`;\n\n  if (userName) {\n    systemPrompt += `\\n\\nThe user's name is ${userName}. Use it occasionally and naturally — not in every response, but enough to feel personal.`;\n  }\n\n  if (profile) {\n    const discStyle = getDiscStyleInstructions(profile.personalityType);\n    if (discStyle) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\n${discStyle}`;\n    }\n\n    systemPrompt += `\\n\\nUSER PERSONALITY PROFILE:`;\n\n    if (profile.personalityType) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nPersonality Type: ${profile.personalityType}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.traits) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nKey Traits: ${profile.traits}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.strengths) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nStrengths: ${profile.strengths}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.growthAreas) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nGrowth Areas: ${profile.growthAreas}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.values) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nCore Values: ${profile.values}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.communicationStyle) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nCommunication Style: ${profile.communicationStyle}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.behavioralTendencies) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nBehavioral Tendencies: ${profile.behavioralTendencies}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.stressTendencies) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nStress Response Patterns: ${profile.stressTendencies}`;\n    }\n    if (profile.communicationPreferences) {\n      systemPrompt += `\\nCommunication Preferences: ${profile.communicationPreferences}`;\n    }\n\n    systemPrompt += `\\n\\nLeverage this profile to personalize your coaching. Reference specific traits when relevant, but weave them in naturally — don't list them back to the user. Your goal is to make the user feel deeply understood.`;\n  } else {\n    systemPrompt += `\\n\\nThis user hasn't completed their personality profile yet. Gently encourage self-exploration through your conversation. Ask reflective questions that help them discover their patterns. If appropriate, suggest they explore the personality assessment features of the platform.`;\n  }\n\n  if (stressBlocks \u0026\u0026 stressBlocks.length \u003e 0) {\n    systemPrompt += \"\\n\\nSTRESS INTELLIGENCE:\\n\";\n    for (const block of stressBlocks) {\n      try {\n        const content = JSON.parse(block.content);\n        systemPrompt += `${block.title}: ${JSON.stringify(content)}\\n`;\n      } catch {\n        systemPrompt += `${block.title}: ${block.content}\\n`;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Inject memory context\n  if (memories \u0026\u0026 memories.length \u003e 0) {\n    systemPrompt += buildMemoryContext(memories);\n  }\n\n  if (conversationContext) {\n    systemPrompt += `\\n\\nCONVERSATION CONTEXT: ${conversationContext}`;\n  }\n\n  return systemPrompt;\n}\n\n// Async memory extraction — runs after response is sent, non-blocking\nasync function extractAndStoreMemories(\n  userId: string,\n  userMessage: string,\n  assistantResponse: string,\n  personalityType: string | null\n): Promise\u003cvoid\u003e {\n  try {\n    const extractionPrompt = `You are a memory extraction system for a personality coaching app. Analyze this conversation exchange and extract any meaningful themes, concerns, or patterns worth remembering for future conversations.\n\nUser message: \"${userMessage}\"\nAssistant response: \"${assistantResponse}\"\nUser's DISC personality type: ${personalityType || \"unknown\"}\n\nExtract 0-3 memory items from this exchange. Only extract genuinely meaningful themes — not trivial chitchat.\n\nFor each memory, return a JSON object with:\n- category: one of \"recurring_concern\", \"growth_theme\", \"communication_struggle\", \"connection_challenge\", \"disc_insight\", \"general\"\n- topic: a short 3-8 word label (e.g., \"patience in meetings\", \"conflict avoidance with family\")\n- summary: 1-2 sentences capturing the key insight or concern\n\nReturn ONLY a JSON array (can be empty []). No explanation, just the JSON array.\n\nExamples of what to extract:\n- User mentions struggling with a specific situation repeatedly → recurring_concern\n- User is working toward a personal growth goal → growth_theme  \n- User mentions difficulty communicating with someone → communication_struggle\n- User talks about a challenging relationship → connection_challenge\n- User has an insight about their own DISC tendencies → disc_insight\n\nDo NOT extract:\n- One-off questions about DISC theory\n- General greetings or small talk\n- Questions about other people's personalities (not the user's own struggles)`;\n\n    const response = await anthropic.messages.create({\n      model: \"claude-haiku-4-20250514\",\n      max_tokens: 512,\n      messages: [{ role: \"user\", content: extractionPrompt }],\n    });\n\n    const responseText = response.content[0]?.type === \"text\" ? response.content[0].text : \"[]\";\n    \n    // Parse the JSON response\n    let memories: { category: string; topic: string; summary: string }[] = [];\n    try {\n      // Find JSON array in response\n      const jsonMatch = responseText.match(/\\[[\\s\\S]*\\]/);\n      if (jsonMatch) {\n        memories = JSON.parse(jsonMatch[0]);\n      }\n    } catch {\n      return; // Silently fail on parse error\n    }\n\n    // Upsert each memory\n    for (const memory of memories) {\n      if (!memory.topic || !memory.summary || !memory.category) continue;\n      \n      // Normalize category\n      const validCategories = [\"recurring_concern\", \"growth_theme\", \"communication_struggle\", \"connection_challenge\", \"disc_insight\", \"general\"];\n      const category = validCategories.includes(memory.category) ? memory.category : \"general\";\n\n      // Check if similar topic exists for this user\n      const existing = await prisma.userMemory.findFirst({\n        where: {\n          userId,\n          topic: {\n            contains: memory.topic.toLowerCase().slice(0, 20),\n          },\n        },\n      });\n\n      if (existing) {\n        // Update frequency and refresh summary\n        await prisma.userMemory.update({\n          where: { id: existing.id },\n          data: {\n            frequency: existing.frequency + 1,\n            summary: memory.summary, // Update with latest context\n            lastSeen: new Date(),\n          },\n        });\n      } else {\n        await prisma.userMemory.create({\n          data: {\n            userId,\n            category,\n            topic: memory.topic,\n            summary: memory.summary,\n            frequency: 1,\n            lastSeen: new Date(),\n          },\n        });\n      }\n    }\n  } catch (error) {\n    console.error(\"Memory extraction error (non-critical):\", error);\n    // Non-critical — don't affect the main response\n  }\n}\n\n// GET /api/chat/conversations - List conversations\nchatRouter.get(\"/conversations\", async (c) =\u003e {\n  const user = c.get(\"user\");\n  if (!user) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n  }\n\n  const conversations = await prisma.chatConversation.findMany({\n    where: { userId: user.id },\n    orderBy: { updatedAt: \"desc\" },\n  });\n\n  return c.json({ data: conversations });\n});\n\n// POST /api/chat/conversations - Create new conversation\nchatRouter.post(\n  \"/conversations\",\n  zValidator(\"json\", CreateConversationSchema),\n  async (c) =\u003e {\n    const user = c.get(\"user\");\n    if (!user) {\n      return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n    }\n\n    const body = c.req.valid(\"json\");\n\n    const conversation = await prisma.chatConversation.create({\n      data: {\n        userId: user.id,\n        title: body.title ?? \"New Conversation\",\n        context: body.context ?? null,\n      },\n    });\n\n    return c.json({ data: conversation });\n  }\n);\n\n// GET /api/chat/conversations/:id/messages - Get messages for a conversation\nchatRouter.get(\"/conversations/:id/messages\", async (c) =\u003e {\n  const user = c.get(\"user\");\n  if (!user) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n  }\n\n  const conversationId = c.req.param(\"id\");\n\n  const conversation = await prisma.chatConversation.findFirst({\n    where: { id: conversationId, userId: user.id },\n  });\n\n  if (!conversation) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Conversation not found\", code: \"NOT_FOUND\" } }, 404);\n  }\n\n  const messages = await prisma.chatMessage.findMany({\n    where: { conversationId },\n    orderBy: { createdAt: \"asc\" },\n  });\n\n  return c.json({ data: messages });\n});\n\n// GET /api/chat/memory - Get user's memory summary (for display)\nchatRouter.get(\"/memory\", async (c) =\u003e {\n  const user = c.get(\"user\");\n  if (!user) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n  }\n\n  const memories = await prisma.userMemory.findMany({\n    where: { userId: user.id },\n    orderBy: [{ frequency: \"desc\" }, { lastSeen: \"desc\" }],\n    take: 20,\n  });\n\n  return c.json({ data: memories });\n});\n\n// DELETE /api/chat/memory/:id - Delete a specific memory\nchatRouter.delete(\"/memory/:id\", async (c) =\u003e {\n  const user = c.get(\"user\");\n  if (!user) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n  }\n\n  const memoryId = c.req.param(\"id\");\n\n  const memory = await prisma.userMemory.findFirst({\n    where: { id: memoryId, userId: user.id },\n  });\n\n  if (!memory) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Memory not found\", code: \"NOT_FOUND\" } }, 404);\n  }\n\n  await prisma.userMemory.delete({ where: { id: memoryId } });\n\n  return c.json({ data: { success: true } });\n});\n\n// POST /api/chat/conversations/:id/messages - Send message and get AI response\nchatRouter.post(\n  \"/conversations/:id/messages\",\n  zValidator(\"json\", SendMessageSchema),\n  async (c) =\u003e {\n    const user = c.get(\"user\");\n    if (!user) {\n      return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n    }\n\n    const conversationId = c.req.param(\"id\");\n    const body = c.req.valid(\"json\");\n\n    const conversation = await prisma.chatConversation.findFirst({\n      where: { id: conversationId, userId: user.id },\n    });\n\n    if (!conversation) {\n      return c.json({ error: { message: \"Conversation not found\", code: \"NOT_FOUND\" } }, 404);\n    }\n\n    // Save the user's message\n    const userMessage = await prisma.chatMessage.create({\n      data: {\n        conversationId,\n        role: \"user\",\n        content: body.content,\n      },\n    });\n\n    // Fetch all context in parallel for performance\n    const [profile, stressBlocks, memories, history] = await Promise.all([\n      prisma.personalityProfile.findUnique({ where: { userId: user.id } }),\n      prisma.personalityIntelligence.findMany({ where: { userId: user.id, category: \"stress\" } }),\n      prisma.userMemory.findMany({\n        where: { userId: user.id },\n        orderBy: [{ frequency: \"desc\" }, { lastSeen: \"desc\" }],\n        take: 15, // Top 15 most relevant memories\n      }),\n      prisma.chatMessage.findMany({\n        where: { conversationId },\n        orderBy: { createdAt: \"asc\" },\n      }),\n    ]);\n\n    // Build Anthropic messages from history\n    const anthropicMessages: Anthropic.MessageParam[] = history.map((msg) =\u003e ({\n      role: msg.role as \"user\" | \"assistant\",\n      content: msg.content,\n    }));\n\n    try {\n      const response = await anthropic.messages.create({\n        model: \"claude-sonnet-4-20250514\",\n        max_tokens: 1024,\n        system: buildSystemPrompt({\n          profile,\n          userName: user.name ?? null,\n          conversationContext: conversation.context,\n          stressBlocks,\n          memories,\n        }),\n        messages: anthropicMessages,\n      });\n\n      const assistantContent =\n        response.content[0]?.type === \"text\"\n          ? response.content[0].text\n          : \"I apologize, but I was unable to generate a response.\";\n\n      // Save assistant message\n      const assistantMessage = await prisma.chatMessage.create({\n        data: {\n          conversationId,\n          role: \"assistant\",\n          content: assistantContent,\n        },\n      });\n\n      // Update conversation timestamp\n      await prisma.chatConversation.update({\n        where: { id: conversationId },\n        data: { updatedAt: new Date() },\n      });\n\n      // Fire-and-forget memory extraction (non-blocking)\n      extractAndStoreMemories(\n        user.id,\n        body.content,\n        assistantContent,\n        profile?.personalityType ?? null\n      ).catch(() =\u003e {}); // Silently swallow errors\n\n      return c.json({\n        data: {\n          userMessage,\n          assistantMessage,\n        },\n      });\n    } catch (error) {\n      console.error(\"Anthropic API error:\", error);\n      return c.json(\n        { error: { message: \"Failed to generate AI response\", code: \"AI_ERROR\" } },\n        500\n      );\n    }\n  }\n);\n\nexport { chatRouter };\n```\n\n### Step 4: Add UserMemory types to backend/src/types.ts\n\nRead the current types.ts file first, then add these Zod schemas:\n```typescript\nexport const UserMemorySchema = z.object({\n  id: z.string(),\n  userId: z.string(),\n  category: z.enum([\"recurring_concern\", \"growth_theme\", \"communication_struggle\", \"connection_challenge\", \"disc_insight\", \"general\"]),\n  topic: z.string(),\n  summary: z.string(),\n  frequency: z.number(),\n  lastSeen: z.string(),\n  createdAt: z.string(),\n  updatedAt: z.string(),\n});\n\nexport type UserMemory = z.infer\u003ctypeof UserMemorySchema\u003e;\n```\n\n### Important notes:\n- The memory extraction is fire-and-forget (async, non-blocking) — it should NOT delay the AI response\n- Memories are user-specific and private (always filtered by userId)\n- Use `claude-haiku-4-20250514` for memory extraction (cheaper, faster)\n- Use `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` for main chat responses\n\nPlease implement all steps above. Run the migration commands and verify the server still runs without errors by checking the backend server log at `/home/user/workspace/backend/server.log` after making changes.","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:backend-developer"}

{"description":"Add memory UI to frontend","id":"019cd365-3181-7244-9357-82874091b29d","prompt":"You are a frontend developer working on a React/Vite/Tailwind webapp at /home/user/workspace/webapp.\n\n## Task: Add AI Memory UI to the Chat page\n\nThe backend now has:\n- `GET /api/chat/memory` — returns user's stored memories (array of UserMemory objects)\n- `DELETE /api/chat/memory/:id` — deletes a memory\n\nEach memory has:\n```typescript\n{\n  id: string,\n  category: \"recurring_concern\" | \"growth_theme\" | \"communication_struggle\" | \"connection_challenge\" | \"disc_insight\" | \"general\",\n  topic: string,        // e.g. \"patience in meetings\"\n  summary: string,      // 1-2 sentence description\n  frequency: number,    // how many times this theme appeared\n  lastSeen: string,     // ISO date\n  createdAt: string,\n  updatedAt: string,\n}\n```\n\n### What to build:\n\nAdd a \"What I Remember\" / memory panel to the Chat page that shows the AI's contextual memory about the user.\n\nPlease:\n\n1. First, read these files to understand the current codebase:\n   - `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Chat.tsx`\n   - `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/types/index.ts`\n   - `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/lib/api.ts`\n\n2. Add `UserMemory` type to `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/types/index.ts`:\n```typescript\nexport interface UserMemory {\n  id: string;\n  userId: string;\n  category: 'recurring_concern' | 'growth_theme' | 'communication_struggle' | 'connection_challenge' | 'disc_insight' | 'general';\n  topic: string;\n  summary: string;\n  frequency: number;\n  lastSeen: string;\n  createdAt: string;\n  updatedAt: string;\n}\n```\n\n3. Create `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/chat/MemoryPanel.tsx`:\n\nThis component should:\n- Accept props: `{ isOpen: boolean, onClose: () =\u003e void }`\n- Fetch memories from `/api/chat/memory` using react-query (`useQuery`)\n- Display memories grouped by category with nice labels:\n  - recurring_concern → \"Recurring Concerns\" (with a cycle/repeat icon)\n  - growth_theme → \"Growth Themes\" (with a sprout/growth icon)\n  - communication_struggle → \"Communication Struggles\" (with a message icon)\n  - connection_challenge → \"Connection Challenges\" (with a link/people icon)\n  - disc_insight → \"Self-Discovery\" (with a lightbulb icon)\n  - general → \"Notable Patterns\" (with a star icon)\n- Each memory card shows:\n  - Topic (bold)\n  - Summary (muted text)\n  - Frequency badge if \u003e 1 (e.g., \"3 times\")\n  - Time since last seen\n  - A small X/trash button to delete it (calls DELETE /api/chat/memory/:id)\n- Show a loading skeleton while fetching\n- Show empty state: \"The AI hasn't learned about you yet. Start chatting and it will remember recurring themes and patterns from your conversations.\"\n- Design it as a slide-in panel/drawer from the right side (fixed position, z-50)\n- Use a clean, modern design with the app's existing color scheme (look at existing components for reference)\n- Add a header: \"What I Remember\" with a subtitle \"Private and secure — only visible to you\"\n- Add a small info note at the bottom: \"Memories are extracted automatically from your conversations to personalize your experience over time.\"\n\n4. Update `/home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Chat.tsx`:\n- Add a \"Memory\" button to the chat header area (brain/memory icon + \"Memory\" label)\n- Import and render the `MemoryPanel` component\n- Wire up the open/close state\n\nUse the existing design patterns from other components (Tailwind classes, color variables, etc). Make it look polished and trustworthy since it deals with personal data.\n\nUse `useQuery` and `useMutation` from `@tanstack/react-query` for API calls.\nUse the existing `api` helper from `../lib/api` for requests.\nInvalidate the `['chat-memory']` query after deleting a memory.\n\nImportant: Look at existing components like ConversationList.tsx or ChatBubble.tsx for design patterns and class naming conventions before writing new code. Match the existing style closely.","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

### Vibecode Agent
*3/9/2026, 11:23:11 AM*

Everything is live and working. Here's what was built:

---

## AI Contextual Memory System — Complete

### How it works

**Memory Extraction (automatic, non-blocking)**
After every AI response, the system silently analyzes the conversation exchange using a lightweight Claude Haiku model and extracts 0–3 meaningful themes. These are stored per-user and never shared.

**Memory is private & secure** — every read/write is filtered by `userId` with auth required. Users can also delete any memory at any time.

### What the AI remembers

| Category | Examples |
|---|---|
| Recurring Concerns | "patience in meetings", "fear of confrontation" |
| Growth Themes | "working on listening more", "learning to set boundaries" |
| Communication Struggles | "difficulty with direct feedback", "conflict with colleagues" |
| Connection Challenges | "tension with spouse", "managing team dynamics" |
| Self-Discovery | "realizing I'm more D than I thought" |
| Notable Patterns | any other meaningful themes |

### Personality-adaptive responses

The AI now gives distinctly different responses based on DISC type:
- **D personality** — Direct, concise, outcome-focused, no fluff
- **I personality** — Warm, enthusiastic, story-driven, relational
- **S personality** — Calm, patient, emotionally validating first, then advice
- **C personality** — Logical, structured, frameworks and reasoning

### Memory Panel (UI)

In the Chat screen, tap the **"Memory" button** (brain icon) in the header to see what the AI knows about you — grouped by category, with frequency counts, timestamps, and the ability to delete any entry.

The AI will naturally reference past themes: *"You've mentioned before..."* or *"This connects to something we explored last week..."*

{"description":"Update backend for phone support","id":"019cd409-f1d6-7430-bd67-053eaddbf2bd","prompt":"I need you to update the backend in /home/user/workspace/backend to support connecting by either email OR phone number.\n\n## Changes needed:\n\n### 1. Add `phone` field to User model in `/home/user/workspace/backend/prisma/schema.prisma`\nAdd `phone String? @unique` to the User model after the `image` field.\n\n### 2. Create and run migration\nRun: `cd /home/user/workspace/backend \u0026\u0026 bunx prisma migrate dev --create-only --name add-user-phone`\nThen: `cd /home/user/workspace/backend \u0026\u0026 bunx prisma migrate deploy`\n\n### 3. Update `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/types.ts`\nFind `CreateConnectionSchema` and update it to accept either email OR phone:\n```ts\nexport const CreateConnectionSchema = z.union([\n  z.object({ email: z.string().email(), phone: z.undefined().optional() }),\n  z.object({ phone: z.string().min(7).max(20), email: z.undefined().optional() }),\n]);\n```\nAlso update `CreateConnectionInput` type if needed (it's inferred from the schema).\n\n### 4. Update `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/connections.ts`\nThe POST handler currently does:\n```ts\nconst { email } = c.req.valid(\"json\");\n```\nChange it to handle both cases. Find the target user by email OR phone:\n```ts\nconst { email, phone } = c.req.valid(\"json\") as { email?: string; phone?: string };\n\n// Cannot connect to yourself\nif (email \u0026\u0026 email === user.email) { ... }\nif (phone \u0026\u0026 phone === (user as any).phone) { ... }\n\n// Find target user\nconst targetUser = await prisma.user.findFirst({\n  where: email ? { email } : { phone },\n});\n\nif (!targetUser) {\n  return c.json(\n    { error: { message: `No user found with that ${email ? \"email\" : \"phone number\"}`, code: \"USER_NOT_FOUND\" } },\n    404\n  );\n}\n```\nKeep rest of the route unchanged.\n\nMake all these changes and run the migration.","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:backend-developer"}

### System — `Executing tool: Bash`

---
### Vibecode Agent
*3/9/2026, 2:44:16 PM*

No upload endpoint. The `AvatarImage` from shadcn/ui supports a `src` prop and Better Auth's `updateUser` accepts an `image` field (a URL string). The cleanest approach for this app is to use a file input that converts the image to a base64 data URL and stores it via `authClient.updateUser({ image })`. This requires no new backend routes.

{"description":"Add upload endpoint to backend","id":"019cd429-755d-79e8-b92c-9e5bedeb7434","prompt":"Add a file upload endpoint to the backend at /home/user/workspace/backend/src/index.ts.\n\nThe `createVibecodeSDK` is already imported and instantiated as `vibecode` in `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/auth.ts`. You need to import it in index.ts.\n\nAdd this to `/home/user/workspace/backend/src/index.ts`:\n\n1. Import StorageError from \"@vibecodeapp/backend-sdk\" and import the vibecode instance from \"./auth\" (check how auth.ts exports it - if it doesn't export vibecode, create a new instance in index.ts using `createVibecodeSDK` from \"@vibecodeapp/backend-sdk\").\n\n2. Add an authenticated upload route BEFORE the existing app routes:\n```typescript\napp.post(\"/api/upload\", async (c) =\u003e {\n  const user = c.get(\"user\");\n  if (!user) return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n\n  const formData = await c.req.formData();\n  const file = formData.get(\"file\");\n\n  if (!file || !(file instanceof File)) {\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"No file provided\", code: \"NO_FILE\" } }, 400);\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const result = await vibecode.storage.upload(file);\n    return c.json({ data: result });\n  } catch (error) {\n    const { StorageError } = await import(\"@vibecodeapp/backend-sdk\");\n    if (error instanceof StorageError) {\n      return c.json({ error: { message: error.message, code: \"STORAGE_ERROR\" } }, error.statusCode as 400 | 500);\n    }\n    return c.json({ error: { message: \"Upload failed\", code: \"UPLOAD_FAILED\" } }, 500);\n  }\n});\n```\n\nMake sure to check if StorageError can be imported statically at the top. Read the file first, then make the minimal changes needed.","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:backend-developer"}

### Vibecode Agent
*3/9/2026, 2:57:19 PM*

Everything should be working now. Here's what the full flow looks like:

1. **Tap the avatar** → a permission dialog appears explaining that only your selected photo is used, never stored or shared without permission, with "Choose a photo" and "Not now" buttons
2. **Tap "Choose a photo"** → your device's photo picker opens
3. **Select a photo** → the crop modal appears with your image in a circular preview
4. **Drag to reposition**, scroll/pinch to zoom — the image always stays within the circle
5. **Tap Save** → the cropped image uploads to CDN and sets your profile photo

### Vibecode Agent
*3/9/2026, 3:08:14 PM*

The issue is the canvas output is a fixed square (`300x300`) which crops the image. The right approach is: save the full original image as-is (no canvas cropping), and store the pan/zoom offset values so the avatar *displays* the repositioned image using CSS `object-position`. That way the original image is preserved at full quality and the icon just shows the framed portion.

1. Upload the **original file** unchanged to storage
2. The crop modal returns `{ url, offsetX, offsetY, scale }` — no canvas, no re-encoding
3. Store the position metadata in `localStorage` (keyed by user ID) since Better Auth's `image` field only holds a URL
4. The avatar renders with `object-fit: cover` + `object-position` driven by the stored offset

Claude Code working hard on your project.

---
### Vibecode Agent
*3/9/2026, 3:23:41 PM*

The root issue is that `AvatarImage` (which is a shadcn `<img>`) defaults to `object-fit: cover` which fills the square/circle and crops — but when we apply a `scale` transform on top, things get distorted. Let me scrap the CSS tricks and just do it the right way: render the image directly with `object-fit: cover` and `object-position` only, no scaling transform. The crop modal should output position as a simple `x% y%` value, nothing else.

---
### System — `Executing tool: Read`
*3/9/2026, 3:23:42 PM*

Read: Profile.tsx

---
### Vibecode Agent
*3/9/2026, 3:23:48 PM*

Now I'll rewrite everything cleanly. The correct approach:

1. **Modal** outputs only `{ x: number, y: number }` — pure CSS `object-position` percentages, no scale
2. **Avatar** uses `object-fit: cover` + `object-position` — browser handles fitting without distortion
3. The zoom in the modal only helps the user *preview* where they're framing — it doesn't change what gets stored

### System — `Executing tool: Read`

Read: avatar.tsx

---
### Vibecode Agent
*3/9/2026, 3:29:41 PM*

There's the problem. `AvatarImage` has `aspect-square h-full w-full` hardcoded — that forces the image into a square and overrides any `object-fit`/`object-position` we pass via `style`. The `aspect-square` class forces a 1:1 ratio regardless of the image's natural dimensions.

The fix: stop using `AvatarImage` for the profile photo entirely. Replace the avatar in the Profile page with a plain `<img>` inside the circular container, using `object-fit: cover` and `object-position`. This is the correct approach — no framework fighting, full control.

### User
