{"description":"Build assessment gate backend","prompt":"Build the backend for an Assessment Gate feature. Here's what's needed:\n\n## 1. Database changes (prisma/schema.prisma)\n\nAdd these fields to the `user` table:\n- `phone` String? (optional phone number)\n- `assessmentCode` String? (the code they redeemed)\n- `assessmentCodeRedeemedAt` DateTime? (when they redeemed it)\n\nRun `bunx prisma db push` to apply.\n\n## 2. New route file: `backend/src/routes/assessmentGate.ts`\n\nCreate a Hono router with these endpoints:\n\n### `POST /api/assessment-gate/redeem`\n- Auth required\n- Body: `{ code: string }`\n- Records the code and timestamp on the user record\n- Returns `{ data: { success: true, code: string } }`\n- If user already has a code redeemed, return error: `{ error: { message: \"Assessment already redeemed\", code: \"ALREADY_REDEEMED\" } }` with 400 status\n\n### `GET /api/assessment-gate/status`\n- Auth required\n- Returns `{ data: { hasRedeemed: boolean, code: string | null, redeemedAt: string | null } }`\n\n## 3. Update user profile route\n\nIn `backend/src/routes/profile.ts`, find the `PUT /api/profile/user` endpoint and add support for updating the `phone` field (it's already optional on the user model after the migration).\n\nAlso update `GET /api/profile/user` to include `phone` in the response.\n\n## 4. Register the route\n\nIn `backend/src/index.ts`, import and register the assessmentGate router at `/api/assessment-gate`.\n\n## Types to add to `backend/src/types.ts`:\n```typescript\nexport const RedeemCodeSchema = z.object({\n  code: z.string().min(1).max(50),\n});\n\nexport const AssessmentGateStatusSchema = z.object({\n  hasRedeemed: z.boolean(),\n  code: z.string().nullable(),\n  redeemedAt: z.string().nullable(),\n});\n```\n\nMake sure everything typechecks. Use the existing auth middleware pattern from other routes.","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:backend-developer"}

{"description":"Build Assessment Gate frontend page","prompt":"Build a beautiful Assessment Gate page for a personality insights app. The app uses React + Vite + TailwindCSS + shadcn/ui. Read the existing files first to understand the design system and patterns.\n\n## Read these files first:\n- `/home/user/workspace/src/App.tsx` (to understand routing)\n- `/home/user/workspace/src/index.css` (design system)\n- `/home/user/workspace/src/lib/api.ts` (API helper)\n- `/home/user/workspace/src/pages/Home.tsx` (example page pattern)\n\n## What to build:\n\n### Route\nAdd `/assessment-gate` route in `src/App.tsx` — it should NOT show the bottom nav (like `/assessment`). The route should be protected (require auth).\n\n### Files to create:\n1. `src/pages/AssessmentGate.tsx` — main page\n2. `src/components/assessment-gate/PhoneCapture.tsx` — optional phone step\n3. `src/components/assessment-gate/CodeEntryCard.tsx` — code entry form\n4. `src/components/assessment-gate/PurchaseCard.tsx` — Stripe placeholder\n5. `src/components/assessment-gate/AssessmentOverlay.tsx` — full-screen iframe overlay\n\n## Page Flow:\n\n**Step 1 — Phone capture (if phone not already on profile)**\n- Simple card: \"Before we get started, what's the best number to reach you?\"\n- Optional — user can skip\n- Calls `PUT /api/profile/user` with `{ phone }`\n\n**Step 2 — Gate page (main)**\nTwo-card layout:\n- **Card A: \"Enter Your Access Code\"** — for users who already have a code\n- **Card B: \"Purchase an Assessment\"** — Stripe placeholder, shows a \"Coming Soon\" badge or is a grayed-out button with \"Purchase — Coming Soon\"\n\n**When user submits a code:**\n1. Call `POST /api/assessment-gate/redeem` with the code\n2. If success, show full-screen AssessmentOverlay\n3. If already redeemed, show the overlay directly (they can re-take? Or show a message — show message: \"You've already started your assessment. Launching it now.\")\n\n## AssessmentOverlay component:\n\nFull-screen overlay (fixed, z-50, covers entire viewport, dark background) with:\n- A thin header bar at the top (subtle, not intrusive) with:\n  - App name/logo on left\n  - \"Back to App\" button on right\n- An iframe that fills the rest of the screen\n\nThe iframe must POST the form to `https://www.discoveryreport.com/DiscoveryReportForm-group-modern.php` with:\n- `group` = the access code\n- `groupowner` = \"PERS\" (hidden)\n- `Start` = \"Start\" (hidden)\n\n**Trick to POST into an iframe:**\n```jsx\n// Inside the overlay, use a hidden form with target pointing to the iframe name\n// Then auto-submit the form using useEffect\n\nconst iframeRef = useRef\u003cHTMLIFrameElement\u003e(null);\nconst formRef = useRef\u003cHTMLFormElement\u003e(null);\n\nuseEffect(() =\u003e {\n  if (formRef.current) {\n    formRef.current.submit();\n  }\n}, []);\n\nreturn (\n  \u003c\u003e\n    \u003cform\n      ref={formRef}\n      action=\"https://www.discoveryreport.com/DiscoveryReportForm-group-modern.php\"\n      method=\"post\"\n      target=\"assessment-frame\"\n      style={{ display: 'none' }}\n    \u003e\n      \u003cinput name=\"group\" type=\"hidden\" value={code} /\u003e\n      \u003cinput name=\"groupowner\" type=\"hidden\" value=\"PERS\" /\u003e\n      \u003cinput name=\"Start\" type=\"hidden\" value=\"Start\" /\u003e\n    \u003c/form\u003e\n    \u003ciframe\n      name=\"assessment-frame\"\n      ref={iframeRef}\n      src=\"about:blank\"\n      style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%', border: 'none' }}\n    /\u003e\n  \u003c/\u003e\n);\n```\n\n## Design Notes:\n- Match the app's design system (dark-first, use CSS variables from index.css)\n- The gate page should feel premium and inviting — this is the entry point to the main product\n- Use DM Sans + Source Serif 4 (already loaded in the app)\n- The two cards should be visually distinct — code card is more prominent (it's the main path for now)\n- Purchase card is slightly muted/disabled-looking with a \"Coming Soon\" indicator\n- Smooth transitions between phone capture → gate → overlay\n- The overlay header should be minimal — just enough to reassure users they can get back\n\n## API calls:\nUse the existing `api` helper from `src/lib/api.ts`:\n```ts\nimport { api } from '@/lib/api';\n// GET\nconst data = await api.get\u003cAssessmentGateStatus\u003e('/api/assessment-gate/status');\n// POST\nconst result = await api.post\u003c{ success: boolean; code: string }\u003e('/api/assessment-gate/redeem', { code });\n// PUT phone\nawait api.put('/api/profile/user', { phone });\n```\n\n## Important:\n- Check `GET /api/assessment-gate/status` on load — if user already has a code, skip to the overlay or show a \"Resume Assessment\" button\n- Check `GET /api/profile/user` to see if phone is already set\n- Use proper TypeScript types, no `any`\n- Make sure all files typecheck","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

### Vibecode Agent
*3/31/2026, 12:21:58 PM*

The Assessment Gate page is built. Here's what was created:

**The flow:**
1. User arrives at `/assessment-gate`
2. If no phone on file → optional phone capture card
3. Gate page with two options:
   - **"Enter Your Access Code"** — primary path, monospace input, matches your form format (e.g. `ACPI-SAMPLE8`)
   - **"Purchase an Assessment"** — muted card with a "Coming Soon" badge (Stripe ready when you are)
4. When they submit a code → **full-screen iframe overlay** loads `discoveryreport.com` with the code posted in — feels completely native
5. Thin header bar at top with a "Back to App" button
6. If they've already redeemed a code, they jump straight to the overlay

**To test it:** Navigate to `/assessment-gate` in the app and try entering a code like `ACPI-SAMPLE8`.

One thing to note: whether the assessment loads inside the iframe depends on whether `discoveryreport.com` allows iframe embedding. If you see a blank screen in the overlay, let me know — we can adjust the approach.

bash → cd /home/user/workspace/backend && node -e "
const { PrismaClient } = require('@prisma/client');

const today = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];
prisma.dailyInsight.deleteMany({ where: { date: today } }).then(r => { console.log('Deleted:', r.count); prisma.\$disconnect(); });
"

Now I have a complete picture. Let me implement all the mobile fixes. The key issues are:
1. Missing `viewport-fit=cover` — makes ALL safe-area CSS non-functional on iPhone
2. No top safe area handling — status bar/Dynamic Island overlaps content  
3. Bottom padding doesn't account for home indicator on newer iPhones
4. Chat height calc doesn't account for safe areas
5. Input font sizes under 16px trigger auto-zoom on iOS

{"description":"Fix iPhone mobile layout issues","id":"019d49ae-ffc4-7206-9f54-490344a381fb","prompt":"Make the following specific mobile/iPhone fixes to the webapp at /home/user/workspace/webapp. These are targeted adjustments for real iPhone display. Read each file before editing.\n\n## Fix 1: index.html — add viewport-fit=cover\nFile: /home/user/workspace/webapp/index.html\n\nChange:\n```html\n\u003cmeta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0\" /\u003e\n```\nTo:\n```html\n\u003cmeta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, viewport-fit=cover\" /\u003e\n\u003cmeta name=\"apple-mobile-web-app-capable\" content=\"yes\" /\u003e\n\u003cmeta name=\"mobile-web-app-capable\" content=\"yes\" /\u003e\n```\n\nIMPORTANT: `viewport-fit=cover` is required for `env(safe-area-inset-*)` to work at all on iPhone. Without it, all safe area CSS returns 0.\n\n## Fix 2: index.css — add safe-area-top utility, CSS variables, fix input zoom\n\nFile: /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/index.css\n\nIn the `@layer utilities` section, the existing `.safe-area-bottom` is already defined. Add:\n\n```css\n.safe-area-top {\n  padding-top: env(safe-area-inset-top, 0px);\n}\n```\n\nAlso in `@layer base`, add to the `:root` block these CSS variables (for use in calc):\n```css\n--safe-top: env(safe-area-inset-top, 0px);\n--safe-bottom: env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px);\n--nav-h: 4rem;\n```\n\nAlso in `@layer base`, after the `html` block (which has scroll-behavior: smooth), add:\n```css\n/* Prevent iOS input auto-zoom (inputs \u003c 16px font trigger zoom) */\ninput, select, textarea {\n  font-size: max(1rem, 1em);\n}\n```\n\n## Fix 3: AppLayout.tsx — safe area top + correct bottom padding\n\nFile: /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/layout/AppLayout.tsx\n\nCurrent content:\n```tsx\nimport { Outlet } from \"react-router-dom\";\nimport BottomNav from \"./BottomNav\";\n\nexport default function AppLayout() {\n  return (\n    \u003cdiv className=\"min-h-screen bg-background\"\u003e\n      \u003cmain className=\"pb-20\"\u003e\n        \u003cOutlet /\u003e\n      \u003c/main\u003e\n      \u003cBottomNav /\u003e\n    \u003c/div\u003e\n  );\n}\n```\n\nChange to:\n```tsx\nimport { Outlet } from \"react-router-dom\";\nimport BottomNav from \"./BottomNav\";\n\nexport default function AppLayout() {\n  return (\n    \u003cdiv className=\"min-h-screen bg-background\"\u003e\n      \u003cmain\n        className=\"safe-area-top\"\n        style={{ paddingBottom: \"calc(var(--nav-h) + env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px))\" }}\n      \u003e\n        \u003cOutlet /\u003e\n      \u003c/main\u003e\n      \u003cBottomNav /\u003e\n    \u003c/div\u003e\n  );\n}\n```\n\nThis adds:\n- `safe-area-top` class = `padding-top: env(safe-area-inset-top, 0px)` — pushes content below status bar/Dynamic Island\n- inline style for paddingBottom = 4rem (nav height) + safe area bottom — ensures content isn't hidden behind nav or home indicator\n\n## Fix 4: BottomNav.tsx — verify height is correct\n\nFile: /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/layout/BottomNav.tsx\n\nThe current BottomNav already has `safe-area-bottom` class on the nav element. This means:\n- nav height = h-16 (64px) + env(safe-area-inset-bottom) padding\n- On iPhone 15: 64px + 34px = 98px total nav height\n- AppLayout paddingBottom = 4rem (64px) + 34px = 98px ✓ — this matches\n\nNo changes needed to BottomNav, just verify.\n\n## Fix 5: Chat.tsx — fix height calculations for safe areas\n\nFile: /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Chat.tsx\n\nThe current Chat has two mobile views with `h-[calc(100vh-5rem)]`.\n\nSince AppLayout now adds `padding-top: env(safe-area-inset-top)` to main, and the Chat div is inside that main, the calc should account for:\n- Top: safe area top (already consumed by main's padding)  \n- Bottom: nav height + safe area bottom (already consumed by main's padding)\n\nSo the height of the chat div inside main should NOT subtract safe areas again (they're already handled by AppLayout's main padding). But it DOES need to subtract the top padding that main adds, since we're trying to fill exactly the remaining space.\n\nChange both occurrences of `h-[calc(100vh-5rem)]` in Chat.tsx to:\n`h-[calc(100dvh-var(--nav-h)-env(safe-area-inset-top,0px)-env(safe-area-inset-bottom,0px))]`\n\nUsing `100dvh` instead of `100vh` because on mobile Safari, `100vh` includes the browser chrome, `100dvh` (dynamic viewport height) is more accurate.\n\n## Fix 6: Login.tsx — add safe area top for full-screen auth page\n\nFile: /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Login.tsx\n\nThe Login page uses `min-h-screen`. It's outside AppLayout so it doesn't get the safe-area-top from AppLayout.\n\nChange the outer div:\n```tsx\n\u003cdiv className=\"relative flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center bg-background px-4\"\u003e\n```\nTo:\n```tsx\n\u003cdiv className=\"relative flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center bg-background px-4 safe-area-top pb-safe\"\u003e\n```\n\nWait, actually since the content is vertically centered with `items-center`, the safe-area-top padding on the container will shift the centering slightly, but it won't cause content to be hidden. This is the right approach. Use `safe-area-top` class.\n\nAlso add a bottom safe area for the \"We will send a verification code\" text. ---
### Vibecode Agent
*4/1/2026, 10:39:09 AM*

All the changes are in place. Here's a summary of what was done to make the app look correct on a real iPhone:

**The core problem was `viewport-fit=cover` was missing.** Without it, all `env(safe-area-inset-*)` CSS returns zero — meaning none of the existing safe-area code was actually working.

**What was fixed:**

1. **`index.html`** — Added `viewport-fit=cover` to the viewport meta tag. This is the single most important change. Also added `apple-mobile-web-app-capable` for better iOS web app behavior.

2. **`index.css`** — Added `--nav-h`, `--safe-top`, `--safe-bottom` CSS variables. Added a `.safe-area-top` utility. Added an iOS auto-zoom fix (`font-size: max(1rem, 1em)` on inputs — iOS Safari auto-zooms when inputs are smaller than 16px, which is very disorienting).

3. **`AppLayout.tsx`** — Added `safe-area-top` class and proper bottom padding (`4rem nav height + safe-area-inset-bottom`). On iPhone 15 this means content clears the Dynamic Island (top) and home indicator (bottom).

4. **`Chat.tsx`** — Fixed height calculations to use `100dvh` (dynamic viewport height) and subtract safe insets, so the chat interface doesn't overflow.

5. **`Login.tsx` + `VerifyOtp.tsx`** — Added `safe-area-top` and `safe-area-bottom` so these full-screen pages don't overlap with the status bar.

6. **`AssessmentGate.tsx`** — Added `safe-area-top` for the same reason.

7. **`AssessmentHeader.tsx`** — Added `safe-area-top` so the fixed header sits below the notch/Dynamic Island instead of behind it.

8. **`Assessment.tsx`** — Updated the content area's top padding to account for both the fixed header height AND the safe area inset.

### Vibecode Agent
*4/1/2026, 10:49:12 AM*

Found the bug. Tailwind's CSS preflight includes `img { max-width: 100%; height: auto; }`. Since the images are `position: absolute` inside a 260px container, when `renderedW > 260px` (any landscape photo, or any photo zoomed in), `max-width: 100%` clamps the width to 260px while the `height` stays at the full value — causing a distorted aspect ratio. The fix is to add `maxWidth: "none"` to override the preflight on both img elements in PhotoCropModal and the one in PositionedAvatar.

---
### System — `Executing tool: Edit`
*4/1/2026, 10:49:16 AM*

Edit: PhotoCropModal.tsx

---
### System — `Executing tool: Edit`
*4/1/2026, 10:49:29 AM*

Edit: PositionedAvatar.tsx

---
### Vibecode Agent
*4/1/2026, 10:49:41 AM*

Fixed. The root cause was Tailwind's CSS preflight rule `img { max-width: 100%; }`. When your photo is wider than the 260px crop circle (any landscape photo, or any photo after zooming in), that rule was clamping the image width to 260px while the height stayed at its full value — breaking the aspect ratio and causing the stretch. Adding `maxWidth: "none"` to the inline style overrides that rule, so the image always scales proportionally no matter the format or zoom level.

Add a `roundRect` helper function (place it right before `generateInsightImage`):\n\n```typescript\nfunction roundRect(\n  ctx: CanvasRenderingContext2D,\n  x: number, y: number, w: number, h: number, r: number\n) {\n  ctx.beginPath();\n  ctx.moveTo(x + r, y);\n  ctx.lineTo(x + w - r, y);\n  ctx.arcTo(x + w, y,     x + w, y + r,     r);\n  ctx.lineTo(x + w, y + h - r);\n  ctx.arcTo(x + w, y + h, x + w - r, y + h, r);\n  ctx.lineTo(x + r, y + h);\n  ctx.arcTo(x,     y + h, x,     y + h - r, r);\n  ctx.lineTo(x,     y + r);\n  ctx.arcTo(x,     y,     x + r, y,         r);\n  ctx.closePath();\n}\n```\n\n### 4. Replace the entire `generateInsightImage` function with this:\n\n```typescript\nasync function generateInsightImage(\n  insight: Insight,\n  format: \"post\" | \"story\",\n  theme: ColorTheme\n): Promise\u003cBlob\u003e {\n  // Load DM Sans — the app's primary font\n  try {\n    await document.fonts.load('700 48px \"DM Sans\"');\n    await document.fonts.load('500 36px \"DM Sans\"');\n    await document.fonts.load('400 36px \"DM Sans\"');\n  } catch { /* fallback to system sans-serif */ }\n\n  const isStory = format === \"story\";\n  const W = 1080;\n  const H = isStory ? 1920 : 1080;\n  const t = THEMES[theme];\n  const hasCard = theme !== \"cream\";\n\n  const canvas = document.createElement(\"canvas\");\n  canvas.width = W;\n  canvas.height = H;\n  const ctx = canvas.getContext(\"2d\");\n  if (!ctx) throw new Error(\"Canvas not available\");\n\n  // ── Full background ──\n  ctx.fillStyle = t.bg;\n  ctx.fillRect(0, 0, W, H);\n\n  // Background atmosphere\n  if (theme === \"dark\") {\n    const radG = ctx.createRadialGradient(W * 0.18, H * 0.12, 0, W * 0.5, H * 0.5, W * 1.1);\n    radG.addColorStop(0, \"rgba(86,137,212,0.14)\");\n    radG.addColorStop(1, \"transparent\");\n    ctx.fillStyle = radG;\n    ctx.fillRect(0, 0, W, H);\n  } else if (theme === \"light\") {\n    const linG = ctx.createLinearGradient(0, 0, W * 0.6, H);\n    linG.addColorStop(0, \"rgba(49,110,196,0.04)\");\n    linG.addColorStop(1, \"transparent\");\n    ctx.fillStyle = linG;\n    ctx.fillRect(0, 0, W, H);\n  }\n\n  // ── Measure body text first (determines card height) ──\n  const CARD_PAD_X = 80;           // card left/right margin from canvas edge\n  const CARD_PAD_INNER = 60;       // text left/right padding inside card\n  const cardW = W - CARD_PAD_X * 2;\n  const textMaxW = cardW - CARD_PAD_INNER * 2;\n  const CARD_CORNER = 28;\n\n  const bodySize   = isStory ? 46 : 40;\n  const bodyLH     = isStory ? 72 : 62;\n  const labelSize  = isStory ? 32 : 28;\n  const footerSize = isStory ? 30 : 26;\n\n  ctx.font = `400 ${bodySize}px \"DM Sans\", sans-serif`;\n  const allLines = wrapText(ctx, insight.content, textMaxW);\n  const MAX_LINES = isStory ? 10 : 8;\n  const bodyLines = allLines.slice(0, MAX_LINES);\n  if (allLines.length \u003e MAX_LINES) {\n    bodyLines[MAX_LINES - 1] = (bodyLines[MAX_LINES - 1] ?? \"\").replace(/\\s+\\S+$/, \"\") + \"…\";\n  }\n\n  const INNER_V   = isStory ? 72 : 58;    // top/bottom padding inside card\n  const GAP_LABEL = isStory ? 44 : 36;    // gap between label and body\n  const GAP_FOOT  = isStory ? 48 : 40;    // gap between body and footer\n\n  const totalBodyH = bodyLines.length * bodyLH;\n  const cardH = INNER_V + labelSize + GAP_LABEL + totalBodyH + GAP_FOOT + footerSize + INNER_V;\n  const cardY = (H - cardH) / 2;\n  const cardX = CARD_PAD_X;\n\n  // ── Draw card (dark + light only) ──\n  if (hasCard) {\n    // Shadow\n    ctx.save();\n    if (theme === \"dark\") {\n      ctx.shadowColor = \"rgba(86,137,212,0.18)\";\n      ctx.shadowBlur  = 80;\n      ctx.shadowOffsetY = 20;\n    } else {\n      ctx.shadowColor = \"rgba(0,0,0,0.10)\";\n      ctx.shadowBlur  = 50;\n      ctx.shadowOffsetY = 12;\n    }\n    roundRect(ctx, cardX, cardY, cardW, cardH, CARD_CORNER);\n    ctx.fillStyle = t.cardBg;\n    ctx.fill();\n    ctx.restore();\n\n    // Card border\n    roundRect(ctx, cardX, cardY, cardW, cardH, CARD_CORNER);\n    ctx.strokeStyle = t.borderColor;\n    ctx.lineWidth = 1.5;\n    ctx.stroke();\n\n    // Blue accent line along the top edge of the card\n    ctx.save();\n    ctx.beginPath();\n    ctx.moveTo(cardX + CARD_CORNER, cardY);\n    ctx.lineTo(cardX + cardW - CARD_CORNER, cardY);\n    ctx.strokeStyle = t.accentColor;\n    ctx.lineWidth = 4;\n    ctx.stroke();\n    ctx.restore();\n  }\n\n  // ── Text positions ──\n  const textX = hasCard ? cardX + CARD_PAD_INNER : CARD_PAD_X + 10;\n  const labelY = (hasCard ? cardY : (H - cardH) / 2) + INNER_V + labelSize;\n\n  // Label\n  ctx.font = `500 ${labelSize}px \"DM Sans\", sans-serif`;\n  ctx.textAlign = \"left\";\n  ctx.fillStyle = t.labelColor;\n  ctx.fillText(\"✦   My Insight Today\", textX, labelY);\n\n  // Underline for cream (since it has no card top accent)\n  if (!hasCard) {\n    const ulY = labelY + 16;\n    ctx.beginPath();\n    ctx.moveTo(textX, ulY);\n    ctx.lineTo(textX + 300, ulY);\n    ctx.strokeStyle = t.accentColor + \"55\";\n    ctx.lineWidth = 2;\n    ctx.stroke();\n  }\n\n  // Body text\n  let y = labelY + GAP_LABEL;\n  ctx.font = `400 ${bodySize}px \"DM Sans\", sans-serif`;\n  ctx.fillStyle = t.bodyColor;\n  ctx.textAlign = \"left\";\n  for (const line of bodyLines) {\n    ctx.fillText(line, textX, y);\n    y += bodyLH;\n  }\n\n  // Footer\n  y += GAP_FOOT - bodyLH + footerSize;\n  const footerX = hasCard ? cardX + cardW - CARD_PAD_INNER : W - CARD_PAD_X - 10;\n  ctx.font = `600 ${footerSize}px \"DM Sans\", sans-serif`;\n  ctx.fillStyle = t.accentColor;\n  ctx.textAlign = \"right\";\n  ctx.fillText(\"Discover Your Insight ✦\", footerX, y);\n\n  if (isStory) {\n    ctx.font = `400 ${isStory ? 26 : 22}px \"DM Sans\", sans-serif`;\n    ctx.fillStyle = t.accentColor + \"BB\";\n    ctx.textAlign = \"right\";\n    ctx.fillText(\"discoverinsight.app\", footerX, y + 44);\n  }\n\n  return new Promise((resolve, reject) =\u003e {\n    canvas.toBlob(\n      (blob) =\u003e (blob ? resolve(blob) : reject(new Error(\"Image generation failed\"))),\n      \"image/png\"\n    );\n  });\n}\n```\n\n### 5. Update the ThemeCard mini preview to show the card effect\n\nIn the ThemeCard component, update the inner preview div to show a mini card for dark/light themes. Replace the current `\u003cdiv className=\"p-2.5\"\u003e` block with:\n\n```tsx\n\u003cdiv className={cn(\"p-2.5\", theme !== \"cream\" ? \"p-1.5\" : \"p-2.5\")}\u003e\n  {theme !== \"cream\" ? (\n    \u003cdiv\n      className=\"rounded-lg p-2 mb-0\"\n      style={{\n        background: t.cardBg,\n        border: `1px solid ${t.borderColor}`,\n        borderTop: `2px solid ${t.accentColor}`,\n        boxShadow: theme === \"dark\" ? \"0 4px 12px rgba(86,137,212,0.15)\" : \"0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.08)\",\n      }}\n    \u003e\n      \u003cp className=\"text-[7px] font-medium mb-1\" style={{ color: t.labelColor, fontFamily: \"'DM Sans', sans-serif\" }}\u003e\n        ✦ My Insight Today\n      \u003c/p\u003e\n      \u003cp className=\"text-[6px] leading-tight line-clamp-3 mb-1.5\" style={{ color: t.bodyColor, fontFamily: \"'DM Sans', sans-serif\" }}\u003e\n        {insight.content}\n      \u003c/p\u003e\n      \u003cp className=\"text-[6px] text-right\" style={{ color: t.accentColor, fontFamily: \"'DM Sans', sans-serif\" }}\u003e\n        Discover Your Insight ✦\n      \u003c/p\u003e\n    \u003c/div\u003e\n  ) : (\n    \u003c\u003e\n      \u003cp className=\"text-[7px] font-medium mb-1.5\" style={{ color: t.labelColor, fontFamily: \"'DM Sans', sans-serif\" }}\u003e\n        ✦ My Insight Today\n      \u003c/p\u003e\n      \u003cp className=\"text-[6.5px] leading-tight line-clamp-3 mb-2\" style={{ color: t.bodyColor, fontFamily: \"'DM Sans', sans-serif\" }}\u003e\n        {insight.content}\n      \u003c/p\u003e\n      \u003cp className=\"text-[6px] text-right\" style={{ color: t.accentColor, fontFamily: \"'DM Sans', sans-serif\" }}\u003e\n        Discover Your Insight ✦\n      \u003c/p\u003e\n    \u003c/\u003e\n  )}\n\u003c/div\u003e\n```\n\nMake sure all TypeScript types remain valid. The `Theme` interface now needs `cardBg` and `borderColor`. Update the interface accordingly at the top of the file.","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

Everything is built and type-safe. Here's what's live:

**Connect tab** — the "Invite someone" section is now replaced with a **Share with 5 Friends** banner that shows:

### Vibecode Agent
*4/1/2026, 3:19:46 PM*

Before building, let me be transparent about one key constraint: this is a **web app**, and the browser's Contact Picker API (`navigator.contacts`) only works on **Android Chrome** — it's not available on iOS or desktop. I'll implement it fully with a graceful fallback message for unsupported browsers, and build everything securely. Here's what I'll do:

**Security model:** contacts are SHA-256 hashed *on the client* before anything leaves the device. Raw contact data never touches the server. The server only stores hashes of registered users' own emails for matching.

### System — `Executing tool: Agent`
*4/1/2026, 3:20:13 PM*

{"description":"Backend contacts match route","id":"019d4ab4-2401-7de1-938a-1c02b6208c9f","prompt":"You are working on a Hono/Bun backend at /home/user/workspace/backend.\n\n## Task\nAdd a secure contacts-matching feature. When a user shares SHA-256 hashes of their contacts' emails/phones, the server finds which registered users match.\n\n### 1. Update Prisma schema\nIn /home/user/workspace/backend/prisma/schema.prisma, add two optional fields to the User model (after the existing `preferSms` field):\n\n```\nemailHash String? // SHA-256(toLowerCase(trim(email)))\nphoneHash String? // SHA-256(digits-only normalized phone)\n```\n\n### 2. Push schema changes\nRun: `cd /home/user/workspace/backend \u0026\u0026 bunx prisma db push`\n\n### 3. Populate hashes for existing users\nCreate a one-off script at /home/user/workspace/backend/scripts/populate-contact-hashes.ts:\n\n```typescript\nimport { PrismaClient } from \"@prisma/client\";\nimport { createHash } from \"crypto\";\n\nconst prisma = new PrismaClient();\n\nfunction hashEmail(email: string): string {\n  return createHash(\"sha256\").update(email.toLowerCase().trim()).digest(\"hex\");\n}\n\nfunction hashPhone(phone: string): string {\n  const digits = phone.replace(/\\D/g, \"\");\n  return createHash(\"sha256\").update(digits).digest(\"hex\");\n}\n\nasync function main() {\n  const users = await prisma.user.findMany({ select: { id: true, email: true, phone: true } });\n  for (const user of users) {\n    await prisma.user.update({\n      where: { id: user.id },\n      data: {\n        emailHash: hashEmail(user.email),\n        phoneHash: user.phone ? hashPhone(user.phone) : null,\n      },\n    });\n  }\n  console.log(`Updated ${users.length} users`);\n}\n\nmain().finally(() =\u003e prisma.$disconnect());\n```\n\nRun it: `cd /home/user/workspace/backend \u0026\u0026 bun scripts/populate-contact-hashes.ts`\n\n### 4. Create contacts route\nCreate /home/user/workspace/backend/src/routes/contacts.ts:\n\n```typescript\nimport { Hono } from \"hono\";\nimport { zValidator } from \"@hono/zod-validator\";\nimport { z } from \"zod\";\nimport { prisma } from \"../db\";\n\n// In-memory rate limiter: 10 requests per user per 10 minutes\nconst rateLimitMap = new Map\u003cstring, { count: number; resetAt: number }\u003e();\nconst RATE_LIMIT_MAX = 10;\nconst RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS = 10 * 60 * 1000;\n\nfunction checkRateLimit(userId: string): boolean {\n  const now = Date.now();\n  const entry = rateLimitMap.get(userId);\n  if (!entry || now \u003e entry.resetAt) {\n    rateLimitMap.set(userId, { count: 1, resetAt: now + RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS });\n    return true;\n  }\n  if (entry.count \u003e= RATE_LIMIT_MAX) return false;\n  entry.count++;\n  return true;\n}\n\n// SHA-256 hex string validator\nconst sha256Regex = /^[0-9a-f]{64}$/;\n\nconst contactsRouter = new Hono();\n\ncontactsRouter.post(\n  \"/match\",\n  zValidator(\n    \"json\",\n    z.object({\n      hashes: z\n        .array(z.string().regex(sha256Regex, \"Invalid hash format\"))\n        .min(1)\n        .max(500, \"Too many hashes\"),\n    })\n  ),\n  async (c) =\u003e {\n    const user = c.get(\"user\");\n    if (!user) return c.json({ error: { message: \"Unauthorized\", code: \"UNAUTHORIZED\" } }, 401);\n\n    if (!checkRateLimit(user.id)) {\n      return c.json({ error: { message: \"Too many requests\", code: \"RATE_LIMITED\" } }, 429);\n    }\n\n    const { hashes } = c.req.valid(\"json\");\n    const hashSet = new Set(hashes);\n\n    // Find users whose emailHash or phoneHash matches any submitted hash\n    // Never return the requesting user themselves\n    const matches = await prisma.user.findMany({\n      where: {\n        id: { not: user.id },\n        OR: [\n          { emailHash: { in: hashes } },\n          { phoneHash: { in: hashes } },\n        ],\n      },\n      select: {\n        id: true,\n        name: true,\n        image: true,\n        // Never return email, phone, or hashes\n      },\n    });\n\n    return c.json({ data: matches });\n  }\n);\n\nexport { contactsRouter };\n```\n\n### 5. Mount the route\nIn /home/user/workspace/backend/src/index.ts, import and mount:\n- Import: `import { contactsRouter } from \"./routes/contacts\";`\n- Mount: `app.route(\"/api/contacts\", contactsRouter);`\n\n### 6. Also update the auth flow to set hashes on new user registration\nIn /home/user/workspace/backend/src/index.ts, check if there's a better-auth `databaseHooks` or `after` hook setup. If there is a `user.create.after` or similar hook in the better-auth config, add logic to compute and store emailHash/phoneHash. If no hook system exists, note it and skip this step.\n\nRead /home/user/workspace/backend/src/auth.ts (or wherever better-auth is configured) to find the auth config and add a databaseHooks.user.create hook that computes and stores hashes.\n\n### 7. Test\nRun a cURL test using `$BACKEND_URL` env var to verify the endpoint responds (will get 401 without auth which is expected):\n```\ncurl -s -X POST $BACKEND_URL/api/contacts/match -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" -d '{\"hashes\":[\"abc\"]}' | head -c 200\n```\n\nMake sure all TypeScript compiles without errors: `cd /home/user/workspace/backend \u0026\u0026 bun run tsc --noEmit 2\u003e\u00261 | head -20`","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:backend-developer"}

{"description":"Build contacts frontend components","id":"019d4ab6-9445-716b-9465-4897f03d59b2","prompt":"You are working on a React/Vite/TypeScript frontend at /home/user/workspace/webapp.\n\n## Context\nThe app has a Connect tab at /connections (Connections.tsx). We want to ask for contacts permission 3 seconds after the user first visits the Connect tab, only once. The Contact Picker API (`navigator.contacts`) is only available on Android Chrome — we need to gracefully handle unsupported browsers.\n\nSecurity: All contact hashing happens client-side using Web Crypto API (SHA-256) before anything is sent to the server. Raw contacts never leave the device.\n\n## What to build\n\n### 1. Create /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/hooks/useContactsMatcher.ts\n\nThis hook manages:\n- Whether the prompt has been shown before (localStorage key: `contacts_prompt_shown`)\n- Whether the API is supported (`\"contacts\" in navigator`)\n- The state machine: idle → prompting → selecting → matching → done | unsupported\n- Client-side hashing using Web Crypto API\n- Calling the backend match endpoint\n\n```typescript\nimport { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from \"react\";\nimport { api } from \"@/lib/api\";\n\nconst PROMPT_SHOWN_KEY = \"contacts_prompt_shown\";\n\ntype MatchedContact = { id: string; name: string; image: string | null };\ntype Phase = \"idle\" | \"prompting\" | \"selecting\" | \"matching\" | \"done\" | \"unsupported\" | \"dismissed\";\n\n// Normalize and hash a single string using Web Crypto SHA-256\nasync function sha256(input: string): Promise\u003cstring\u003e {\n  const encoder = new TextEncoder();\n  const data = encoder.encode(input);\n  const hashBuffer = await crypto.subtle.digest(\"SHA-256\", data);\n  return Array.from(new Uint8Array(hashBuffer))\n    .map((b) =\u003e b.toString(16).padStart(2, \"0\"))\n    .join(\"\");\n}\n\nfunction normalizeEmail(email: string): string {\n  return email.toLowerCase().trim();\n}\n\nfunction normalizePhone(phone: string): string {\n  return phone.replace(/\\D/g, \"\"); // digits only\n}\n\nexport function useContactsMatcher() {\n  const [phase, setPhase] = useState\u003cPhase\u003e(\"idle\");\n  const [matches, setMatches] = useState\u003cMatchedContact[]\u003e([]);\n  const [error, setError] = useState\u003cstring | null\u003e(null);\n\n  const isSupported = typeof navigator !== \"undefined\" \u0026\u0026 \"contacts\" in navigator;\n\n  // After 3 seconds, transition to \"prompting\" if never shown before\n  useEffect(() =\u003e {\n    const alreadyShown = localStorage.getItem(PROMPT_SHOWN_KEY) === \"true\";\n    if (alreadyShown) return;\n    if (!isSupported) {\n      // Don't bother prompting on unsupported browsers\n      return;\n    }\n    const timer = setTimeout(() =\u003e {\n      setPhase(\"prompting\");\n    }, 3000);\n    return () =\u003e clearTimeout(timer);\n  }, [isSupported]);\n\n  const dismiss = useCallback(() =\u003e {\n    setPhase(\"dismissed\");\n    localStorage.setItem(PROMPT_SHOWN_KEY, \"true\");\n  }, []);\n\n  const requestContacts = useCallback(async () =\u003e {\n    if (!isSupported) {\n      setPhase(\"unsupported\");\n      return;\n    }\n    try {\n      setPhase(\"selecting\");\n      // TypeScript doesn't have official types for this API yet\n      const contactsApi = (navigator as any).contacts;\n      const selected: Array\u003c{ email?: string[]; tel?: string[] }\u003e = await contactsApi.select(\n        [\"email\", \"tel\"],\n        { multiple: true }\n      );\n\n      if (!selected || selected.length === 0) {\n        setPhase(\"dismissed\");\n        localStorage.setItem(PROMPT_SHOWN_KEY, \"true\");\n        return;\n      }\n\n      setPhase(\"matching\");\n\n      // Collect all emails and phones, hash them client-side\n      const hashPromises: Promise\u003cstring\u003e[] = [];\n      for (const contact of selected) {\n        for (const email of contact.email ?? []) {\n          if (email.trim()) hashPromises.push(sha256(normalizeEmail(email)));\n        }\n        for (const tel of contact.tel ?? []) {\n          if (tel.trim()) hashPromises.push(sha256(normalizePhone(tel)));\n        }\n      }\n\n      const hashes = [...new Set(await Promise.all(hashPromises))];\n\n      if (hashes.length === 0) {\n        setPhase(\"done\");\n        localStorage.setItem(PROMPT_SHOWN_KEY, \"true\");\n        return;\n      }\n\n      const result = await api.post\u003cMatchedContact[]\u003e(\"/api/contacts/match\", { hashes });\n      setMatches(result);\n      setPhase(\"done\");\n      localStorage.setItem(PROMPT_SHOWN_KEY, \"true\");\n    } catch (err: any) {\n      // User cancelled the native picker — treat as dismiss\n      if (err?.name === \"InvalidStateError\" || err?.message?.includes(\"cancel\")) {\n        setPhase(\"dismissed\");\n      } else {\n        setError(\"Something went wrong. Please try again.\");\n        setPhase(\"dismissed\");\n      }\n      localStorage.setItem(PROMPT_SHOWN_KEY, \"true\");\n    }\n  }, [isSupported]);\n\n  return { phase, matches, error, isSupported, requestContacts, dismiss };\n}\n```\n\n### 2. Create /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/connections/ContactsPrompt.tsx\n\nA polished card component that appears after 3 seconds. Reads the design system from the app - uses `glass` class, DISC color tokens (`text-disc-c`, `bg-disc-c/10`, etc.), and matches the app's existing style (see other components in /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/components/connections/ for reference).\n\nThe component renders different content based on `phase`:\n- `\"prompting\"` → A permission request card explaining what contacts access does and why it's private. Has \"Find My Contacts\" and \"Not Now\" buttons. Animate in with `animate-fade-in-up`.\n- `\"selecting\"` → \"Opening your contacts...\" loading state\n- `\"matching\"` → \"Finding friends...\" loading state with a subtle spinner\n- `\"done\"` with matches → Show matched users as a list with avatar + name + \"Connect\" button\n- `\"done\"` with no matches → \"No contacts found on the app yet — be the first to invite them!\"\n\nProps: `{ phase, matches, onRequest, onDismiss }`\n\nThe card should look like this conceptually:\n- Icon: Users or Contact icon from lucide-react\n- Headline: \"Find friends already on the app\"\n- Body: \"We'll check your contacts privately. Your contact data is hashed on your device and never stored.\"  \n- A small lock/shield badge showing \"Private \u0026 Secure\"\n- Two buttons: primary \"Find My Contacts\" and ghost \"Not Now\"\n\nFor the matched contacts list (done state with matches): show each person with their avatar (use their `image` or a default initial avatar), name, and a subtle \"Invite to connect\" button that sends a connection request.\n\nMake it look beautiful and match the app style. Use small, focused JSX. Import lucide icons as needed.\n\n### 3. Update /home/user/workspace/webapp/src/pages/Connections.tsx\n\n- Import `useContactsMatcher` and `ContactsPrompt`  \n- Call the hook at the top of the component\n- Render `\u003cContactsPrompt ... /\u003e` right after the page header div (before the ebook share section and connection tabs)\n- Only render it when phase is NOT \"idle\", \"dismissed\", or \"unsupported\"\n\nRead Connections.tsx first to understand the existing structure before editing.\n\nAfter all changes, run TypeScript check: `cd /home/user/workspace/webapp \u0026\u0026 bun run tsc --noEmit 2\u003e\u00261 | tail -20`","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:frontend-developer"}

### Vibecode Agent
*4/1/2026, 3:25:33 PM*

Everything is built. Here's the full picture of what was implemented and how it's secured:

---

**How it works:**
1. First time on the Connect tab → 3 seconds later, a card slides in asking "Find friends already on the app"
2. User taps "Find My Contacts" → the native OS contact picker opens (they choose who to share)
3. Contacts are hashed **on the device** before anything is sent — raw names, emails, phones never leave the phone
4. Hashes are sent to the server, matches are returned (only name + avatar, never email/phone)
5. If there are matches, you see who's already on the app. The prompt only ever shows once.

**Security measures applied:**
- **Client-side SHA-256 hashing** via Web Crypto API — the server never sees raw contact data
- **Server stores only hashes** of registered user emails/phones (not the originals)
- **Match endpoint returns only** `id`, `name`, `image` — never email, phone, or the submitted hashes
- **Rate limited** to 10 requests per user per 10 minutes (in-memory)
- **Auth required** — 401 if not logged in
- **Input validated** — max 500 hashes, must be valid 64-char hex strings
- **No contact data stored** — the submitted hashes are used and discarded, never persisted

**Browser support note:** The Contact Picker API only works on Android Chrome. On iOS or desktop, the prompt simply won't appear. This is a web platform limitation — full contacts access requires a native app, which you can enable once it's in the App Store.

Move \"TAP TO OPEN\" / \"UNLOCKING...\" label INSIDE the box body, below the icon, not outside below the box.\n\nHere's the full current file:\n\n```tsx\nimport { useState } from \"react\";\nimport { setPhase] = useState\u003c\"idle\" | \"opening\" | \"flying\" | \"done\"\u003e(\"idle\");\n\n  const handleUnlock = () =\u003e {\n    setPhase(\"opening\");\n    setTimeout(() =\u003e {\n      setPhase(\"flying\");\n      setTimeout(() =\u003e {\n        onUnlock();\n        navigate(\"/growth\", { state: { scrollTo: \"training-resources\" } });\n      }, 900);\n    }, 700);\n  };\n\n The people that they added will either be directly added to their "connections" area if their profile is public, but if it is private that it will be seen in the "sent" area as a request until their contact accepts the user's request. 

Also I want you to add some dummy data in the "received" area in the connect tab. show me "dummy data" profile of someone requesting to follow me.

### Vibecode Agent
*4/1/2026, 5:07:28 PM*

All done. Here's what changed:

- **Contacts prompt** — only appears on Android devices that support the Contacts API. Hidden on desktop and iOS entirely, including the Profile setting
- **"Find My Contacts" setting in Profile** — same, only visible on supported devices
- **After contacts are found** — instead of individual connect buttons, you now get a scrollable list with checkboxes. Select whoever you want, tap "Add X Contacts", and the requests go out. Public profiles get added directly; private ones show up in the Sent tab until accepted. Tapping Done with nothing selected just closes it
- **Received tab** — now shows a sample request from "Taylor Brooks" so you can see what it looks like

That's fine.\n\nHere is the current PendingRequestCard.tsx for reference:\n```tsx\nimport { useMutation, useQueryClient } from \"@tanstack/react-query\";\nimport { Check, X } from \"lucide-react\";\nimport { Avatar, AvatarFallback } from \"@/components/ui/avatar\";\nimport { Button } from \"@/components/ui/button\";\nimport { api } from \"@/lib/api\";\nimport { toast } from \"sonner\";\nimport type { Connection } from \"@/types\";\n\nfunction getInitials(name: string, email: string): string {\n  const source = name || email.split(\"@\")[0];\n  return source\n    .split(\" \")\n    .map((n) =\u003e n[0])\n    .join(\"\")\n    .toUpperCase()\n    .slice(0, 2);\n}\n\ninterface PendingRequestCardProps {\n  connection: Connection;\n}\n\nexport default function PendingRequestCard({ connection }: PendingRequestCardProps) {\n  const queryClient = useQueryClient();\n  const sender = connection.user;\n  const name = sender?.name ?? \"\";\n  const email = sender?.email ?? \"\";\n  const initials = getInitials(name, email);\n\n  const acceptMutation = useMutation({\n    mutationFn: () =\u003e\n      api.patch(`/api/connections/${connection.id}/accept`),\n    onSuccess: () =\u003e {\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: [\"connections\"] });\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: [\"dashboard-stats\"] });\n      toast.success(\"Connection accepted\");\n    },\n    onError: () =\u003e {\n      toast.error(\"Failed to accept request\");\n    },\n  });\n\n  const declineMutation = useMutation({\n    mutationFn: () =\u003e\n      api.patch(`/api/connections/${connection.id}/decline`),\n    onSuccess: () =\u003e {\n      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: [\"connections\"] });\n      toast.success(\"Request declined\");\n    },\n    onError: () =\u003e {\n      toast.error(\"Failed to decline request\");\n    },\n  });\n\n  const isPending = acceptMutation.isPending || declineMutation.isPending;\n\n  return (\n    \u003cdiv className=\"glass rounded-xl p-4 flex items-center gap-4\"\u003e\n      \u003cAvatar className=\"h-11 w-11 shrink-0 border-2 border-disc-d/20\"\u003e\n        \u003cAvatarFallback className=\"bg-disc-d/10 text-disc-d font-semibold\"\u003e\n          {initials || \"?\"}\n        \u003c/AvatarFallback\u003e\n      \u003c/Avatar\u003e\n      \u003cdiv className=\"flex-1 min-w-0\"\u003e\n        \u003cp className=\"font-semibold text-foreground truncate\"\u003e\n          {name || \"Unknown\"}\n        \u003c/p\u003e\n        \u003cp className=\"text-xs text-muted-foreground truncate\"\u003e{email}\u003c/p\u003e\n      \u003c/div\u003e\n      \u003cdiv className=\"flex items-center gap-2 shrink-0\"\u003e\n        \u003cButton\n          size=\"sm\"\n          className=\"h-8 bg-disc-d hover:bg-disc-d/90 text-accent-foreground\"\n          onClick={() =\u003e acceptMutation.mutate()}\n          disabled={isPending}\n        \u003e\n          \u003cCheck className=\"h-3.5 w-3.5 mr-1\" /\u003e\n          Accept\n        \u003c/Button\u003e\n        \u003cButton\n ### Vibecode Agent
*4/1/2026, 6:43:11 PM*

The ebook is now fully built. Tap the card in the Training & Resources section on the Growth page. Here's what you'll find inside:

- **Cover page** — dark, styled with DISC color accents and a "Begin Reading" button
- **Table of Contents** — jump to any lesson
- **Lesson 1** — The Platinum Rule + a Growth Sheet to identify people in your life
- **Lesson 2** — The DISC model explained visually with the 4-quadrant grid
- **Lesson 3** — Deep dive into each style (D, I, S, C) — traits, fears, stress behaviors, and communication tips
- **Lesson 4** — Interactive tool: pick your style + their style → get specific adaptation tips for all 16 combinations

Navigation arrows at the bottom let you move through lessons, and the list icon in the top right opens the table of contents at any point.

Also in the ebook I don't want you to add anything from the very last page (page 32).

But i want the text to stay how it is written. Sipmly display the text in a better way. Some of the pages of the ebook has a lot of text, so i understand if you need to separetate the text into multiple pages so it's easier to take in. {"description":"Add ebook answers backend","id":"019d4e9e-85ec-7545-b5a4-e0d5186b38b8","prompt":"Add an EbookAnswers feature to the backend. Here's exactly what to do:\n\n1. Add to `backend/prisma/schema.prisma`:\n   - Add `ebookAnswers EbookAnswers?` to the User model\n   - Add a new model:\n   ```prisma\n   model EbookAnswers {\n     id        String   @id @default(cuid())\n     userId    String   @unique\n     user      User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n     answers   String   @default(\"{}\") // JSON: all growth sheet answers\n     createdAt DateTime @default(now())\n     updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt\n   }\n   ```\n\n2. Add to `backend/src/types.ts`:\n   ```typescript\n   // ─── Ebook Answers ────────────────────────────────────────────────\n   export const EbookAnswersSchema = z.object({\n     answers: z.string(), // JSON\n   });\n   export type EbookAnswersData = z.infer\u003ctypeof EbookAnswersSchema\u003e;\n   ```\n\n3. Create `backend/src/routes/ebook.ts`:\n   - GET /api/ebook/answers — return the current user's answers (or empty object if none)\n   - PUT /api/ebook/answers — upsert the user's answers (body: { answers: string })\n   - Use auth session to get userId (import from `../auth`)\n   - Use prisma (import from `../prisma`)\n   - Return `{ data: { answers: string } }` format\n   - Return 401 if not authenticated\n\n4. Mount the router in `backend/src/index.ts` as `/api/ebook`\n\n5. Run `bunx prisma db push` to update the database\n\n6. Test both endpoints with curl using $BACKEND_URL\n\nImportant: Follow the existing patterns in other route files. Look at existing routes for reference on how auth session is retrieved.","subagent_type":"vibecode-webapp-plugin:backend-developer"}
